Task instruction sent to agent 
# Task: Implement CSS Container Queries Size Evaluation in Servo

## Objective
Add CSS Container Queries support to Servo's style system, implementing the `@container` at-rule with size-based queries (`min-width`, `max-width`, `min-height`, `max-height`, `width`, `height`).

CSS Container Queries (CSS Containment Level 3) allow elements to be styled based on their container's size rather than the viewport. A container is designated via `container-type: size | inline-size` and queried with `@container (min-width: 400px) { ... }`.

## Requirements

1. **Define container type properties** in Servo's style properties:
   - Add `container-type` CSS property (values: `normal`, `size`, `inline-size`)
   - Add `container-name` CSS property (values: `none` or custom ident)
   - These should be defined in `components/style/properties/` following Servo's property definition pattern

2. **Parse @container rules** in the CSS parser:
   - Extend `components/style/stylesheets/` to handle `@container` at-rules
   - Parse container size conditions: `(min-width: Npx)`, `(max-width: Npx)`, `(width > Npx)`, etc.
   - Store parsed container queries in the stylesheet data structures

3. **Implement container query evaluation**:
   - In the style resolution path, evaluate container size queries against the actual container dimensions
   - Walk up the DOM tree to find the nearest container ancestor with matching `container-name`
   - Compare container dimensions against the query conditions

4. **Integrate with style matching**:
   - During rule matching, check if `@container` rules apply given the current container sizes
   - Rules inside a matching `@container` block should be included in the cascade

## Key Reference Files
- `components/style/properties/longhands/` — longhand property definitions (add container-type, container-name here)
- `components/style/stylesheets/rule_parser.rs` — at-rule parsing
- `components/style/stylesheets/container_rule.rs` — may already exist as a stub
- `components/style/matching.rs` — style matching/cascade logic
- `components/style/values/specified/length.rs` — length value parsing
- `components/style/media_queries/` — reference pattern for conditional rules (similar to @media)

## Success Criteria
- `container-type` property definition exists in properties
- `container-name` property definition exists in properties
- Container query parsing structures defined (ContainerCondition or similar)
- @container at-rule parsing integrated into stylesheet parser
- Size query evaluation logic exists (comparing dimensions against conditions)
- Integration point in style matching that checks container queries

     9→  .size { container-type: size; }
    10→  .inline.outer { width: 500px; }

    12→  .inline.inner { width: 300px; }

    33→      ref.style = '';
    34→    }
    35→  }
    36→

     4→<script src="/resources/testharness.js"></script>
     5→<script src="/resources/testharnessreport.js"></script>
     6→<script src="support/cq-testcommon.js"></script>
     7→<div style="container-name:name;container-type:size; width:100px; height:100px">
     8→  <main id="cq-main"></main>
     9→</div>
    10→<script>
    11→  setup(() => assert_implements_size_container_queries());
    12→
    13→  test_cq_condition_known('(width)');
    14→  test_cq_condition_known('(min-width: 0px)');
    15→  test_cq_condition_known('(max-width: 0px)');
    16→  test_cq_condition_known('(height)');
    17→  test_cq_condition_known('(min-height: 0px)');
    18→  test_cq_condition_known('(max-height: 0px)');
    19→  test_cq_condition_known('(aspect-ratio)');
    20→  test_cq_condition_known('(min-aspect-ratio: 1/2)');
    21→  test_cq_condition_known('(max-aspect-ratio: 1/2)');
    22→  test_cq_condition_known('(orientation: portrait)');
    23→  test_cq_condition_known('(inline-size)');
    24→  test_cq_condition_known('(min-inline-size: 0px)');
    25→  test_cq_condition_known('(max-inline-size: 0px)');
    26→  test_cq_condition_known('(block-size)');
    27→  test_cq_condition_known('(min-block-size: 0px)');
    28→  test_cq_condition_known('(max-block-size: 0px)');
    29→
    30→  test_cq_condition_known('(width: 100px)');
    31→  test_cq_condition_known('((width: 100px))');
    32→  test_cq_condition_known('(not (width: 100px))');
    33→  test_cq_condition_known('((width: 100px) and (height: 100px))');
    34→  test_cq_condition_known('(((width: 40px) or (width: 50px)) and (height: 100px))');
    35→  test_cq_co

35-usertool_resultRead
     1→function assert_implements_size_container_queries() {
     2→  assert_implements(CSS.supports("container-type:size"),
     3→                    "Basic support for size container queries required");
     4→}
     5→
     6→function assert_implements_scroll_state_container_queries() {
     7→  assert_implements(CSS.supports("container-type:scroll-state"),
     8→                    "Basic support for scroll-state container queries required");
     9→}
    10→
    11→function assert_implements_style_container_queries() {
    12→  // TODO: Replace with CSS.supports() when/if this can be expressed with at-rule().
    13→  const sheet = new CSSStyleSheet();
    14→  // No support means the style() function is <general-enclosed> which should
    15→  // affect serialization. Although serialization for <general-enclosed> is not
    16→  // specified[1], unknown function names are unlikely to be resolved to be
    17→  // serialized lower-case. Also, keeping the case is currently interoperable.
    18→  //
    19→  // [1] https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/7266
    20→  sheet.replaceSync('@container STYLE(--foo: bar){}');
    21→  assert_implements(sheet.cssRules[0].containerQuery === "style(--foo: bar)",
    22→                    "Basic support for style container queries required");
    23→}
    24→
    25→function cleanup_container_query_main() {
    26→  const main = document.querySelector("#cq-main");
    27→  while (main.firstChild)
    28→    main.firstChild.remove();
    29→}
    30→
    31→function set_container_query_style(text) {
    32→  let style = document.createElement('style');
    33→  style.innerText = text;
    34→  document.querySelector("#cq-main").append(style);
    35→  return style;
    36→}
    37→
    38→function test_cq_rule_invalid(qu
... (truncated)

     1→<!doctype html>
     2→<meta charset="utf-8">
     3→<title>CSS Conditional Test: Parsing of container</title>
     4→<link rel="help" href="https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#container-name">
     5→<link rel="help" href="https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#container-type">
     6→<script src="/resources/testharness.js"></script>

     9→<script src="support/cq-testcommon.js"></script>
    10→<div id="target"></div>
    11→<script>
    12→setup(() => assert_implements_size_container_queries());
    13→
    14→test_valid_value('container', 'initial');
    15→test_valid_value('container', 'inherit');
    16→test_valid_value('container', 'unset');
    17→test_valid_value('container', 'revert');
    18→test_valid_value('container', 'none');
    19→test_valid_value('container', 'none / normal', 'none');
    20→test_valid_value('container', 'inline-size');
    21→test_valid_value('container', 'none / inline-size', 'none / inline-size');
    22→test_valid_value('container', 'size');
    23→test_valid_value('container', 'block-size / size');
    24→test_valid_value('container', 'inline-size / inline-size');
    25→test_valid_value('container', 'size / size');
    26→test_valid_value('container', 'foo');
    27→test_valid_value('container', 'foo / normal', 'foo');
    28→test_valid_value('container', 'foo bar / size');
    29→test_valid_value('container', 'foo bar / normal', 'foo bar');
    30→test_valid_value('container', 'FOO / size');
    31→test_valid_value('container', 'FOO/size', 'FOO / size');
    32→test_valid_value('container', '  FOO  /size', 'FOO / size');
    33→test_valid_value('container', 'normal / size');
    34→

68-usertool_resultBash
[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = [
    "components/xpath",
    "ports/servoshell",
    "tests/unit/*",
]
default-members = ["ports/servoshell"]
exclude = [".cargo", "support/crown"]

[workspace.package]
version = "0.0.1"
authors = ["The Servo Project Developers"]
license = "MPL-2.0"
edition = "2024"
publish = false
# We have yet to decide on a policy for updating the minimum supported Rust version.
# Before increasing this, please open a discussion on Zulip explaining the reason
# why we should consider increasing our minimum supported Rust version.
# Please also note, that the **default** Rust version in rust-toolchain.toml may be
# bumped freely.
# Skip 1.90 which causes mis-compilation: https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/147265
rust-version = "1.86.0"

[workspace.dependencies]
accesskit = { version = "0.21.1", features = ["serde"] }
accountable-refcell = "0.2.2"
aes = "0.8.4"
aes-gcm = "0.10.3"
aes-kw = { version = "0.2.1", features = ["alloc"] }
app_units = "0.7"
arboard = "3"
argon2 = { version = "0.5", features = ["alloc"] }
arrayvec = "0.7"
async-tungstenite = { version = "0.32", features = ["tokio-rustls-webpki-roots"] }
atomic_refcell = "0.1.13"
aws-lc-rs = { version = "1.15", default-features = false, features = ["aws-lc-sys"] }
background_hang_monitor_api = { path = "components/shared/background_hang_monitor" }
backtrace = "0.3"
base = { path = "components/shared/base" }
base64 = "0.22.1"
base64ct = { version = "1.8", features = ["alloc"] }
bitflags = "2.10"
bluetooth_traits = { path = "components/shared/bluetooth" }
brotli = "8.0.2"
bytemuck = "1"
byteorder = "1.5"
canvas_traits = { path = "components/shared/canvas" }
cbc = "0.1.2"
cfg-if = "1.0.4"

     2→<meta charset="utf-8">
     3→<title>CSS Conditional Test: Parsing of container-type</title>
     4→<link rel="help" href="https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#container-type">
     5→<script src="/resources/testharness.js"></script>
     6→<script src="/resources/testharnessreport.js"></script>
     7→<script src="/css/support/parsing-testcommon.js"></script>
     8→<script src="support/cq-testcommon.js"></script>
     9→<div id="target"></div>
    10→<script>
    11→setup(() => assert_implements_size_container_queries());
    12→
    13→test_valid_value('container-type', 'initial');
    14→test_valid_value('container-type', 'inherit');
    15→test_valid_value('container-type', 'unset');
    16→test_valid_value('container-type', 'revert');
    17→test_valid_value('container-type', 'normal');
    18→test_valid_value('container-type', 'size');
    19→test_valid_value('container-type', 'inline-size');

    21→test_invalid_value('container-type', 'none');
    22→test_invalid_value('container-type', 'auto');
    23→test_invalid_value('container-type', 'block-size');

    26→test_invalid_value('container-type', 'inline-size normal');
    27→test_invalid_value('container-type', 'inline-size inline-size');
    28→test_invalid_value('container-type', 'inline-size block-size');
    29→test_invalid_value('container-type', 'block-size inline-size');
    30→test_invalid_value('container-type', 'size inline-size');
    31→test_invalid_value('container-type', 'inline-size size');
    32→test_invalid_value('container-type', 'normal, normal');

     1→<!doctype html>
     2→<title>Container Relative Units: cqi, cqb, etc</title>
     3→<link rel="help" href="https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#container-lengths">
     4→<script src="/resources/testharness.js"></script>
     5→<script src="/resources/testharnessreport.js"></script>
     6→<script src="support/cq-testcommon.js"></script>
     7→<style>
     8→  .inline { container-type: inline-size; }
     9→  .size { container-type: size; }
    10→  .inline.outer { width: 500px; }
    11→  .size.outer { height: 400px; }
    12→  .inline.inner { width: 300px; }
    13→</style>
    14→<div id=ref></div>
    15→<div class="inline outer">
    16→  <div class="size outer">
    17→    <div class="inline inner">
    18→      <div id=child>Test</div>
    19→    </div>
    20→  </div>
    21→</div>
    22→<script>
    23→  setup(() => assert_implements_size_container_queries());
    24→
    25→  function assert_unit_equals(element, actual, expected) {
    26→    try {
    27→      element.style.padding = actual;
    28→      ref.style.padding = expected;
    29→      assert_equals(getComputedStyle(element).paddingLeft,
    30→                    getComputedStyle(ref).paddingLeft);
    31→    } finally {
    32→      element.style = '';
    33→      ref.style = '';
    34→    }
    35→  }
    36→
    37→  test(function() {
    38→    assert_unit_equals(child, '0cqi', '0px');
    39→    assert_unit_equals(child, '1cqi', '3px');
    40→    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqi', '30px');
    41→    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqw', '30px');
    42→    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqb', '40px');
    43→    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqh', '40px');
    44→    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqmin', '30px');
    45→    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqmax', '40px');
    46→

98-usertool_resultAgent
Excellent! Now let me create a comprehensive final report:

## Summary of Container Query Test Files

I've completed a thorough search of the codebase for container query test files. Here's what I found:

### Test File Locations

**Primary Container Query Test Directory:**
- `/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/`

**Subdirectories:**
- `container-queries/` - 201 main test files
- `scroll-state/` - 55 scroll-state specific tests  
- `crashtests/` - 58 crashtest files
- `support/` - Support utilities including `cq-testcommon.js`

**Total Test Files: 314 HTML test files**

### Test Support Infrastructure

**Key Support File:**
- `/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/support/cq-testcommon.js`
  - Contains test helper functions:
    - `assert_implements_size_container_queries()`
    - `assert_implements_scroll_state_container_queries()`
    - `assert_implements_style_container_queries()`
    - `test_cq_condition_known()`, `test_cq_condition_unknown()`
    - `test_cq_rule_valid()`, `test_cq_rule_invalid()`
    - `test_container_name_valid()`, `test_container_name_invalid()`
    - `test_cq_condition_serialization()`

### Major Test Categories

**1. Core Parsing & Serialization Tests:**
- `at-container-parsing.html` - @container rule parsing validation
- `at-container-serialization.html` - Serialization of @container rules
- `at-container-style-parsing.html` - style() function parsing in @container
- `at-container-style-serialization.html` - Serialization of style queries
- `container-parsing.html` - container property parsing
- `container-type-parsing.html` - container-type property parsing
- `container-name-parsing.html` - container-name property parsing

**2. Container Type Tests:**
- `container-type-containment.html` 
... (truncated)

/workspace/Cargo.toml
112-assistanttool_useRead
113-usertool_resultRead
     1→[workspace]
     2→resolver = "2"
     3→members = [
     4→    "components/xpath",
     5→    "ports/servoshell",
     6→    "tests/unit/*",
     7→]
     8→default-members = ["ports/servoshell"]
     9→exclude = [".cargo", "support/crown"]
    10→
    11→[workspace.package]
    12→version = "0.0.1"
    13→authors = ["The Servo Project Developers"]
    14→license = "MPL-2.0"
    15→edition = "2024"
    16→publish = false
    17→# We have yet to decide on a policy for updating the minimum supported Rust version.
    18→# Before increasing this, please open a discussion on Zulip explaining the reason
    19→# why we should consider increasing our minimum supported Rust version.
    20→# Please also note, that the **default** Rust version in rust-toolchain.toml may be
    21→# bumped freely.
    22→# Skip 1.90 which causes mis-compilation: https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/147265
    23→rust-version = "1.86.0"
    24→

#!/bin/sh
# This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
# License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
# file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.

# The beginning of this script is both valid shell and valid python, such that
# the script starts with the shell and is reexecuted with `uv run`. This
# ensures that the Python provided by the virtual environment (in the .venv
# directory) is used. If the virtual environment does not exist, `uv run` will
# still use the correct version of Python given in `.python-version` and
# python/mach_bootstrap.py will provision a new environment that will be used
# for the subsequent runs.
''':' && {
    MACH_DIR=$(dirname "$0");
    run_in_nix_if_needed() {
        if { [ -f /etc/NIXOS ] || [ -n "${MACH_USE_NIX}" ]; } && [ -z "${IN_NIX_SHELL}" ]; then
            EXTRA_NIX_ARGS=${SERVO_ANDROID_BUILD:+'--arg buildAndroid true'}

            # `nix-shell` needs the whole command passed as a single argument, so the arguments need
            # to be shell-quoted. Rotate through the arguments, replacing them with quoted versions.
            for arg in "$@"; do
                set -- "$@" "$(printf \%q "$1")"
                shift
            done

            echo "NOTE: Entering nix-shell ${MACH_DIR}/shell.nix"
            exec nix-shell "${MACH_DIR}/shell.nix" $EXTRA_NIX_ARGS --run "$*"
        else
            exec "$@"
        fi
    }

    run_in_nix_if_needed uv run --frozen python ${MACH_DIR}/mach "$@"
}
'''

import os
import sys

# On NixOS, prepend nix-provided binary paths so they take precedence over .venv/bin
if 'SERVO_NIX_BIN_DIR' in os.environ:
    os.environ['PATH'] = os.environ['SERVO_NIX_BIN_DIR'] + os.pathsep + os.environ.get('PATH', '')

def main(args):

     1→function assert_implements_size_container_queries() {
     2→  assert_implements(CSS.supports("container-type:size"),
     3→                    "Basic support for size container queries required");
     4→}
     5→
     6→function assert_implements_scroll_state_container_queries() {
     7→  assert_implements(CSS.supports("container-type:scroll-state"),
     8→                    "Basic support for scroll-state container queries required");
     9→}
    10→
    11→function assert_implements_style_container_queries() {
    12→  // TODO: Replace with CSS.supports() when/if this can be expressed with at-rule().
    13→  const sheet = new CSSStyleSheet();
    14→  // No support means the style() function is <general-enclosed> which should
    15→  // affect serialization. Although serialization for <general-enclosed> is not
    16→  // specified[1], unknown function names are unlikely to be resolved to be
    17→  // serialized lower-case. Also, keeping the case is currently interoperable.
    18→  //
    19→  // [1] https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/7266
    20→  sheet.replaceSync('@container STYLE(--foo: bar){}');
    21→  assert_implements(sheet.cssRules[0].containerQuery === "style(--foo: bar)",
    22→                    "Basic support for style container queries required");
    23→}
    24→
    25→function cleanup_container_query_main() {
    26→  const main = document.querySelector("#cq-main");
    27→  while (main.firstChild)
    28→    main.firstChild.remove();
    29→}
    30→
    31→function set_container_query_style(text) {
    32→  let style = document.createElement('style');
    33→  style.innerText = text;
    34→  document.querySelector("#cq-main").append(style);
    35→  return style;
    36→}
    37→

140-usertext-
Search the Servo codebase to find:
1. Where CSS property definitions are (look for longhand, shorthand, properties)
2. Where at-rule parsing is handled (look for RuleParser, AtRuleParser, @media, @supports)
3. Where style matching/cascade logic is implemented
4. How media queries are implemented as a reference for container queries

Check in all components directories, especially layout, script, and shared components. Be comprehensive in searching for any CSS-related code patterns.

     1→[workspace]
     2→resolver = "2"
     3→members = [
     4→    "components/xpath",
     5→    "ports/servoshell",
     6→    "tests/unit/*",
     7→]
     8→default-members = ["ports/servoshell"]
     9→exclude = [".cargo", "support/crown"]
    10→
    11→[workspace.package]
    12→version = "0.0.1"
    13→authors = ["The Servo Project Developers"]
    14→license = "MPL-2.0"
    15→edition = "2024"
    16→publish = false
    17→# We have yet to decide on a policy for updating the minimum supported Rust version.
    18→# Before increasing this, please open a discussion on Zulip explaining the reason
    19→# why we should consider increasing our minimum supported Rust version.
    20→# Please also note, that the **default** Rust version in rust-toolchain.toml may be
    21→# bumped freely.
    22→# Skip 1.90 which causes mis-compilation: https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/147265
    23→rust-version = "1.86.0"
    24→
    25→[workspace.dependencies]
    26→accesskit = { version = "0.21.1", features = ["serde"] }
    27→accountable-refcell = "0.2.2"
    28→aes = "0.8.4"
    29→aes-gcm = "0.10.3"
    30→aes-kw = { version = "0.2.1", features = ["alloc"] }
    31→app_units = "0.7"
    32→arboard = "3"
    33→argon2 = { version = "0.5", features = ["alloc"] }
    34→arrayvec = "0.7"
    35→async-tungstenite = { version = "0.32", features = ["tokio-rustls-webpki-roots"] }
    36→atomic_refcell = "0.1.13"
    37→aws-lc-rs = { version = "1.15", default-features = false, features = ["aws-lc-sys"] }
    38→background_hang_monitor_api = { path = "components/shared/background_hang_monitor" }
    39→backtrace = "0.3"
    40→base = { path = "components/shared/base" }
    41→base64 = "0.22.1"
    42→base64ct = { version = "1.8", features = ["alloc"] }
    43→bitflags = "2
... (truncated)

     1→/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
     2→ * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
     3→ * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */
     4→
     5→use style::media_queries::Device;
     6→use style::shared_lock::SharedRwLockReadGuard;
     7→use style::stylesheet_set::{AuthorStylesheetSet, DocumentStylesheetSet};
     8→use style::stylesheets::{CustomMediaMap, StylesheetInDocument};
     9→
    10→/// Functionality common to DocumentStylesheetSet and AuthorStylesheetSet.
    11→pub(crate) enum StylesheetSetRef<'a, S>
    12→where
    13→    S: StylesheetInDocument + PartialEq + 'static,
    14→{
    15→    /// Author stylesheet set.
    16→    Author(&'a mut AuthorStylesheetSet<S>),
    17→    /// Document stylesheet set.
    18→    Document(&'a mut DocumentStylesheetSet<S>),
    19→}
    20→
    21→impl<S> StylesheetSetRef<'_, S>
    22→where
    23→    S: StylesheetInDocument + PartialEq + 'static,
    24→{
    25→    /// Appends a new stylesheet to the current set.
    26→    ///
    27→    /// No device implies not computing invalidations.
    28→    pub(crate) fn append_stylesheet(
    29→        &mut self,
    30→        device: Option<&Device>,
    31→        sheet: S,
    32→        guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,
    33→    ) {
    34→        let custom_media = &CustomMediaMap::default();
    35→        match self {
    36→            StylesheetSetRef::Author(set) => {
    37→                set.append_stylesheet(device, custom_media, sheet, guard)
    38→            },
    39→            StylesheetSetRef::Document(set) => {
    40→                set.append_stylesheet(device, custom_media, sheet, guard)
    41→            },
    42→        }
    43→    }
    44→
    4
... (truncated)

     1→/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
     2→ * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
     3→ * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */
     4→
     5→//! Helpers for CSS value parsing.
     6→
     7→use style::context::QuirksMode;
     8→use style::error_reporting::ParseErrorReporter;
     9→use style::parser::ParserContext;
    10→use style::stylesheets::{CssRuleType, Origin, UrlExtraData};
    11→use style_traits::ParsingMode;
    12→
    13→use crate::dom::document::Document;
    14→
    15→/// Creates a `ParserContext` from the given document.
    16→///
    17→/// Automatically configures quirks mode and error reporter from the document.
    18→pub(crate) fn parser_context_for_document<'a>(
    19→    document: &'a Document,
    20→    rule_type: CssRuleType,
    21→    parsing_mode: ParsingMode,
    22→    url_data: &'a UrlExtraData,
    23→) -> ParserContext<'a> {
    24→    let quirks_mode = document.quirks_mode();
    25→    let error_reporter = document.window().css_error_reporter();
    26→
    27→    ParserContext::new(
    28→        Origin::Author,
    29→        url_data,
    30→        Some(rule_type),
    31→        parsing_mode,
    32→        quirks_mode,
    33→        /* namespaces = */ Default::default(),
    34→        Some(error_reporter),
    35→        None,
    36→    )
    37→}
    38→
    39→/// Like [`parser_context_for_document`], but with a custom error reporter.
    40→pub(crate) fn parser_context_for_document_with_reporter<'a>(
    41→    document: &'a Document,
    42→    rule_type: CssRuleType,
    43→    parsing_mode: ParsingMode,
    44→    url_data: &'a UrlExtraData,
    45→    error_reporter: &'a dyn ParseErrorReporter,
    46→) -> ParserC
... (truncated)

     5→use cssparser::{Parser, ParserInput};
     6→use style::stylesheets::supports_rule::SupportsCondition;
     7→use style_traits::ToCss;
     8→
     9→#[test]
    10→fn test_supports_condition() {
    11→    assert_roundtrip!(SupportsCondition::parse, "(margin: 1px)");
    12→    assert_roundtrip!(SupportsCondition::parse, "not (--be: to be)");
    13→    assert_roundtrip!(
    14→        SupportsCondition::parse,
    15→        "(color: blue) and future-extension(4)"
    16→    );
    17→    assert_roundtrip!(SupportsCondition::parse, "future-\\1 extension(4)");
    18→    assert_roundtrip!(SupportsCondition::parse, "((test))");
    19→}
    20→

     5→//! Tests for parsing and serialization of values/properties
     6→
     7→use cssparser::{Parser, ParserInput};
     8→use style::context::QuirksMode;
     9→use style::parser::ParserContext;
    10→use style::stylesheets::{CssRuleType, Origin};
    11→use style_traits::{ParseError, ParsingMode};
    12→use url::Url;
    13→
    14→fn parse<T, F>(f: F, s: &'static str) -> Result<T, ParseError<'static>>
    15→where
    16→    F: for<'t> Fn(&ParserContext, &mut Parser<'static, 't>) -> Result<T, ParseError<'static>>,
    17→{
    18→    let mut input = ParserInput::new(s);
    19→    parse_input(f, &mut input)
    20→}
    21→
    22→fn parse_input<'i: 't, 't, T, F>(f: F, input: &'t mut ParserInput<'i>) -> Result<T, ParseError<'i>>
    23→where
    24→    F: Fn(&ParserContext, &mut Parser<'i, 't>) -> Result<T, ParseError<'i>>,
    25→{
    26→    let url_data = Url::parse("http://localhost").unwrap().into();
    27→    let context = ParserContext::new(
    28→        Origin::Author,
    29→        &url_data,
    30→        Some(CssRuleType::Style),
    31→        ParsingMode::DEFAULT,
    32→        QuirksMode::NoQuirks,
    33→        /* namespaces = */ Default::default(),
    34→        None,
    35→        None,
    36→    );
    37→    let mut parser = Parser::new(input);
    38→    f(&context, &mut parser)
    39→}
    40→
    41→// This is a macro so that the file/line information
    42→// is preserved in the panic

    44→    ($fun:expr, $string:expr) => {
    45→        ass

     1→/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public
     2→ * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this
     3→ * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */
     4→
     5→use serde::Serialize;
     6→use serde_json::{Map, Value};
     7→
     8→use crate::StreamId;
     9→use crate::actor::{Actor, ActorError, ActorRegistry};
    10→use crate::protocol::ClientRequest;
    11→
    12→#[derive(Serialize)]
    13→#[serde(rename_all = "camelCase")]
    14→struct GetStyleSheetsReply {
    15→    from: String,
    16→    style_sheets: Vec<u32>, // TODO: real JSON structure.
    17→}
    18→
    19→pub(crate) struct StyleSheetsActor {
    20→    name: String,
    21→}
    22→
    23→impl Actor for StyleSheetsActor {
    24→    fn name(&self) -> String {
    25→        self.name.clone()
    26→    }
    27→    fn handle_message(
    28→        &self,
    29→        request: ClientRequest,
    30→        _registry: &ActorRegistry,
    31→        msg_type: &str,
    32→        _msg: &Map<String, Value>,
    33→        _id: StreamId,
    34→    ) -> Result<(), ActorError> {
    35→        match msg_type {
    36→            "getStyleSheets" => {
    37→                let msg = GetStyleSheetsReply {
    38→                    from: self.name(),
    39→                    style_sheets: vec![],
    40→                };
    41→                request.reply_final(&msg)?
    42→            },
    43→
    44→            _ => return Err(ActorError::UnrecognizedPacketType),
    45→        };
    46→        Ok(())
    47→    }
    48→}
    49→
    50→impl StyleSheetsActor {
    51→    pub fn new(name: String) -> StyleSheetsActor {
    52→        StyleSheetsActor { name }
    53→    }
    54→}
    55→

<system-reminder>

serde_bytes = "0.11"
serde_core = "1.0.226"
serde_json = "1.0"
servo-media = { git = "https://github.com/servo/media", rev = "f384dbc4ff8b5c6f8db2c763306cbe2281d66391" }
--
servo_arc = { git = "https://github.com/servo/stylo", rev = "3b46f3ef27eb14ca276b4bf3ff041a6c43d93827" }
sha1 = "0.10"
sha2 = "0.10"
sha3 = "0.10"
skrifa = "0.37.0"
smallvec = { version = "1.15", features = ["serde", "union"] }
--
stylo = { git = "https://github.com/servo/stylo", rev = "3b46f3ef27eb14ca276b4bf3ff041a6c43d93827" }
stylo_atoms = { git = "https://github.com/servo/stylo", rev = "3b46f3ef27eb14ca276b4bf3ff041a6c43d93827" }
stylo_config = { git = "https://github.com/servo/stylo", rev = "3b46f3ef27eb14ca276b4bf3ff041a6c43d93827" }
stylo_dom = { git = "https://github.com/servo/stylo", rev = "3b46f3ef27eb14ca276b4bf3ff041a6c43d93827" }
stylo_malloc_size_of = { git = "https://github.com/servo/stylo", rev = "3b46f3ef27eb14ca276b4bf3ff041a6c43d93827" }
stylo_traits = { git = "https://github.com/servo/stylo", rev = "3b46f3ef27eb14ca276b4bf3ff041a6c43d93827" }
surfman = { version = "0.11.0", features = ["chains"] }
syn = { version = "2", default-features = false, features = ["clone-impls", "derive", "parsing"] }
synstructure = "0.13"
taffy = { version = "0.9.2", default-features = false, features = ["calc", "detailed_layout_info", "grid", "std"] }
tendril = { version = "0.5", features = ["encoding_rs"] }
--
# [patch."https://github.com/servo/stylo"]
# selectors = { path = "../stylo/selectors" }
# servo_arc = { path = "../stylo/servo_arc" }
# stylo = { path = "../stylo/style" }
251-usertool_resultAgent
Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings:

## Comprehensive Search Results: CSS Architecture in Servo

I've completed a thorough search of the Servo codebase. Here's what I found:

---

## 1. CSS PROPERTY DEFINITIONS AND PARSING

**Location**: Properties are defined and parsed through **Stylo** (external crate from GitHub)

### Key Files:
- **/workspace/components/script/css.rs** - CSS parser context factory
  - `parser_context_for_document()` - Creates ParserContext with document quirks mode and error reporting
  - `parser_context_for_document_with_reporter()` - Custom error reporter variant
  - `parser_context_for_anonymous_content()` - For parsing without a document

- **/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssstyledeclaration.rs** - Style declaration access
  - Handles property declaration blocks
  - Uses `PropertyDeclarationBlock`, `LonghandId`, `ShorthandId`, `PropertyId` from Stylo
  - Methods like `parse_one_declaration_into` for property parsing

- **/workspace/tests/unit/style/parsing/** - Unit test modules for properties:
  - `background.rs` - background properties
  - `border.rs` - border properties
  - `box_.rs` - box-related properties
  - `effects.rs` - visual effects
  - `image.rs` - image properties
  - Plus many others for specific property groups

### Property Architecture:
```
Stylo (GitHub: servo/stylo rev 3b46f3ef27eb14ca276b4bf3ff041a6c43d93827)
  ├── style::properties::ComputedValues (computed style)
  ├── style::properties::PropertyId (property identifiers)
  ├── style::properties::LonghandId (longhand properties)
  ├── style::properties::ShorthandId (shorthand properties)
  └── style::properties::PropertyDeclarationBlock (declaration collections)
```

---

## 2. AT-RULE PARSING

**Location**: 
... (truncated)

259-usertext-
Search for any existing container query implementation in Servo:
1. Look for "container" in CSS-related files (not visual containers)
2. Search for container rules in the DOM CSS files
3. Check if CSSContainerRule exists
4. Look for container type or container name properties
5. Check layout thread for container query evaluation

Search broadly in:
- components/script/dom/css/
- components/layout/
- Any references to container queries, @container, or CSSContainerRule

Be thorough as this will tell us what's already implemented.

    34→    fn new_inherited(parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet, mediarule: Arc<MediaRule>) -> CSSMediaRule {

    12→
    13→static PREFERENCES: RwLock<Preferences> = RwLock::new(Preferences::const_default());
    14→
    15→pub trait PreferencesObserver: Send + Sync {
    16→    fn prefs_changed(&self, _changes: &[(&'static str, PrefValue)]) {}
    17→}
    18→
    19→static OBSERVERS: RwLock<Vec<Box<dyn PreferencesObserver>>> = RwLock::new(Vec::new());
    20→
    21→#[inline]
    22→/// Get the current set of global preferences for Servo.
    23→pub fn get() -> RwLockReadGuard<'static, Preferences> {
    24→    PREFERENCES.read().unwrap()
    25→}
    26→

    28→    OBSERVERS.write().unwrap().push(observer);
    29→}

Perfect! Now let me compile a comprehensive report of my findings:

## Container Query Implementation Status in Servo

Based on my thorough search of the Servo codebase, here's what I found regarding container query implementation:

### **Current State: PARTIALLY STARTED BUT MOSTLY UNIMPLEMENTED**

---

## **Key Findings:**

### 1. **Container Rule Support in CSS Parser (Exists but Unimplemented)**
- **File:** `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssrule.rs` (lines 116, 208)
- **Status:** `StyleCssRule::Container(_) => unimplemented!()` - marked with TODO
- The style crate (Stylo) already has `StyleCssRule::Container` variant
- No corresponding `CSSContainerRule` DOM wrapper exists in Servo's script layer
- Missing WebIDL bindings for `CSSContainerRule`

### 2. **Configuration/Preference Flag**
- **File:** `/workspace/components/config/prefs.rs` (lines 49-50, 265, 470)
- **Field:** `layout_container_queries_enabled: bool`
- **Default:** `false` (disabled by default)
- **Integration:** Passed to Stylo via `stylo_config::set_bool("layout.container-queries.enabled", ...)`
- This means the infrastructure is ready for the feature to be toggled on once implemented

### 3. **Devtools Support**
- **File:** `/workspace/components/devtools/actors/inspector/node.rs` (line 58)
- **Field:** `container_type: Option<()>`
- Currently a stub field set to `None`
- Prepared for future container type information display

### 4. **Layout Query Infrastructure**
- **File:** `/workspace/components/layout/query.rs`
- Contains `process_scroll_container_query()` function
- Demonstrates a pattern for how container queries would be evaluated
- Shows the layout thread has mechanisms for processing container-related queries

### 5. **Container Size Query API (From Style Crate)**
- **File:** `/worksp
... (truncated)

      "text": "Excellent! Now let me create a comprehensive final report:\n\n## Summary of Container Query Test Files\n\nI've completed a thorough search of the codebase for container query test files. Here's what I found:\n\n### Test File Locations\n\n**Primary Container Query Test Directory:**\n- `/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/`\n\n**Subdirectories:**\n- `container-queries/` - 201 main test files\n- `scroll-state/` - 55 scroll-state specific tests  \n- `crashtests/` - 58 crashtest files\n- `support/` - Support utilities including `cq-testcommon.js`\n\n**Total Test Files: 314 HTML test files**\n\n### Test Support Infrastructure\n\n**Key Support File:**\n- `/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/support/cq-testcommon.js`\n  - Contains test helper functions:\n    - `assert_implements_size_container_queries()`\n    - `assert_implements_scroll_state_container_queries()`\n    - `assert_implements_style_container_queries()`\n    - `test_cq_condition_known()`, `test_cq_condition_unknown()`\n    - `test_cq_rule_valid()`, `test_cq_rule_invalid()`\n    - `test_container_name_valid()`, `test_container_name_invalid()`\n    - `test_cq_condition_serialization()`\n\n### Major Test Categories\n\n**1. Core Parsing & Serialization Tests:**\n- `at-container-parsing.html` - @container rule parsing validation\n- `at-container-serialization.html` - Serialization of @container rules\n- `at-container-style-parsing.html` - style() function parsing in @container\n- `at-container-style-serialization.html` - Serialization of style queries\n- `container-parsing.html` - container property parsing\n- `container-type-parsing.html` - container-type property parsing\n- `container-name-parsing.html` - container-name property parsing\n\n**2. Container Type Tests:**\n- `container-type-containment.html` - Containment behavior\n- `container-type-computed.html` - Computed values\n- `container-type-invalidation.html` - Invalidation on type changes\n- `container-type-layout-invalidation.html` - Layout invalidation\n\n**3. Container Selection & Querying:**\n- `container-selection.html` - Container name and implicit selection\n- `container-selection-unknown-features.html` - Unknown feature handling\n- `container-ident-function.html` - Container identifier functions\n- `conditional-container-status.html` - Container status evaluation\n\n**4. Size Query Features:**\n- `container-units-basic.html` - Container relative units (cqi, cqw, cqh, cqb, cqmin, cqmax)\n- `container-units-computational-independence.html` - Unit independence\n- `container-units-content-box.html` - Content-box sizing\n- `container-units-gradient.html` - CQ units in gradients\n- `container-units-svglength.html` - SVG length handling\n- `container-units-typed-om.html` - CSSOM TypedOM support\n- `size-feature-evaluation.html` - Size feature evaluation\n- `aspect-ratio-feature-evaluation.html` - Aspect-ratio queries\n\n**5. Style Query Tests:**\n- `at-container-style-parsing.html` & `at-container-style-serialization.html`\n- `style-container-for-shadow-dom.html` - Shadow DOM support\n- `style-container-invalidation-inheritance.html` - Invalidation\n- `style-query-document-element.html` - Root element queries\n- `style-query-registered-custom-*.html` - Custom property handling\n- `style-query-no-cycle.html` - Cycle prevention\n\n**6. Scroll State Query Tests (scroll-state/ directory):**\n- `at-container-scrollable-parsing.html`\n- `at-container-scrollable-serialization.html`\n- `at-container-scrolled-parsing.html`\n- `at-container-scrolled-serialization.html`\n- `at-container-snapped-parsing.html`\n- `at-container-stuck-parsing.html`\n- `scroll-state-scrollable-container-type-change.html`\n- And 48+ more scroll-state specific tests\n\n**7. Invalidation & Dynamic Tests:**\n- `container-size-invalidation.html` - Size change invalidation\n- `container-size-nested-invalidation.html` - Nested container invalidation\n- `container-size-shadow-invalidation.html` - Shadow DOM invalidation\n- `container-name-invalidation.html` - Name change invalidation\n- `animation-container-type-dynamic.html` - Dynamic type changes\n- `animation-container-size.html` - Size animation\n\n**8. Container Nested & Inheritance:**\n- `container-nested.html` - Nested container behavior\n- `container-inheritance.html` - Property inheritance\n- `container-name-tree-scoped.html` - Tree-scoped names\n- `nested-query-containers.html` - Nested query container handling\n\n**9. Element Type & Special Cases:**\n- `canvas-as-container-*.html` - Canvas element containers\n- `svg-root-size-container.html` - SVG container support\n- `svg-foreignobject-*.html` - ForeignObject support\n- `container-for-shadow-dom.html` - Shadow DOM containers\n- `container-for-cue.html` - VTT cue elements\n- `top-layer-dialog*.html` - Dialog and top-layer elements\n- `table-inside-container*.html` - Table inside containers\n- `multicol-*.html` - Multicol support\n\n**10. Edge Cases & Regression Tests:**\n- 58 crashtest files for stability\n- `chrome-legacy-skip-recalc.html` - Legacy compatibility\n- `unsupported-axis.html` - Invalid axis handling\n- `container-units-small-viewport-fallback.html` - Viewport fallback\n- `column-spanner-in-container.html` - Column spanner interaction\n- `change-display-in-container.html` - Display property changes\n- `size-container-writing-mode-change.html` - Writing mode changes\n\n**11. CSSOM & Computed Values:**\n- `container-rule-cssom.html` - CSSContainerRule interface\n- `container-computed.html` - Computed container property values\n- `container-name-computed.html` - Computed container-name values\n\n**12. Animation & Transitions:**\n- `container-longhand-animation-type.html` - Animation type support\n- `animation-nested-animation.html` - Nested animations\n- `animation-nested-transition.html` - Nested transitions\n- `transition-scrollbars.html` - Scrollbar transition handling\n\n### Key Testing Requirements\n\nBased on the test files, container queries must support:\n\n1. **@container rule parsing and serialization** - Full CSS syntax support\n2. **Size features:** width, height, aspect-ratio, orientation, inline-size, block-size\n3. **Size comparison operators:** <, <=, =, >=, >\n4. **Container-relative units:** cqi, cqw, cqh, cqb, cqmin, cqmax\n5. **Container naming:** container-name property, implicit and explicit selection\n6. **Container types:** normal, size, inline-size, and scroll-state variants\n7. **Style queries:** style() function for custom property queries\n8. **Scroll state queries:** scrollable, scrolled, snapped, stuck\n9. **Proper invalidation:** On size/type/name changes\n10. **Shadow DOM support:** Container queries in shadow trees\n11. **Dynamic behavior:** Animation, transition, and property change handling\n12. **Containment:** Proper layout/size/style/paint containment enforcement\n13. **Nesting:** Multiple containers with proper precedence\n14. **Edge cases:** SVG, Canvas, Tables, Dialogs, Multicolumn layout\n\nAll test files are located at:\n- **Absolute paths:** `/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/`",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],

</div>\n    84\u2192  </div>\n    85\u2192</main>\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192<main>\n    88\u2192  <div class=\"a-size\">\n    89\u2192    <div class=\"b-size\">\n    90\u2192      <div class=\"a-inline\">\n    91\u2192        <span></span>\n    92\u2192      </div>\n    93\u2192    </div>\n    94\u2192  </div>\n    95\u2192</main>\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192<main>\n    98\u2192  <div class=\"a-inline\">\n    99\u2192    <div class=\"b-size\">\n   100\u2192      <span></span>\n   101\u2192    </div>\n   102\u2192  </div>\n   103\u2192</main>\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192<main>\n   106\u2192  <div class=\"ab-size\">\n   107\u2192    <div class=\"size\">\n   108\u2192      <span></span>\n   109\u2192    </div>\n   110\u2192  </div>\n   111\u2192</main>\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192<script>\n   114\u2192  setup(() => assert_implements_size_container_queries());\n   115\u2192\n   116\u2192  function test_query(prelude, selector, expected) {\n   117\u2192    test(t => {\n   118\u2192      let elements = document.querySelectorAll(selector);\n   119\u2192      assert_equals(elements.length, 1);\n   120\u2192      let element = elements[0];\n   121\u2192\n   122\u2192      let style = document.createElement('style');\n   123\u2192      t.add_cleanup(() => { style.remove(); });\n   124\u2192      style.innerText = `@container ${prelude} { span { --match:true; } } `;\n   125\u2192      document.body.append(style);\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192      assert_equals(getComputedStyle(element).getPropertyValue('--match'), expected);\n   128\u2192    }, `${prelude} for ${selector}`);\n   129\u2192  }\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192  // Test that a given container query applies to the specified element.\n   132\u2192  // The provided selector must unique identify the element.\n   133\u2192  function test_applied(prelude, selector) {\n   134\u2192    test_query(prelude, selector, 'true');\n   135\u2192  }\n   136\u2192\n   137\u2192  function test_rejected(prelude, selector) {\n   138\u2192    test_query(prelude, selector, '');\n   139\u2192  }\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192  // For the following tests, the inner container has a size of 16x16px,\n   142\u2192  // and the outer container has a size of 32x32px.\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192  // Implicit selection:\n   145\u2192  test_applied('(width: 16px)',  '.size > .inline > span');\n   146\u2192  test_applied('(height: 16px)', '.inline > .size > span');\n   147\u2192  test_applied('(width: 16px)',  '.inline > .size > span');\n   148\u2192  test_applied('(height: 32px)', '.size > .inline > span');\n   149\u2192  test_rejected('(height: 16px)', '.size > .inline > span');\n   150\u2192\n   151\u2192  // Name selection:\n   152\u2192  test_applied('a (width: 32px)', '.a-size > .b-size > span');\n   153\u2192  test_applied('b (width: 16px)', '.a-size > .b-size > span');\n   154\u2192  test_rejected('c (width)', '.a-size > .b-size > span');\n   155\u2192  test_applied('a (width: 16px)', '.a-size > .a-size > span');\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192  // container-name alone does not establish a container:\n   158\u2192  test_applied('a (width: 32px)', '.a-size > .a > span');\n   159\u2192\n   160\u2192  // Can query container with multiple names:\n   161\u2192  test_applied('a (width: 32px)', '.ab-size > .size > span');\n   162\u2192  test_applied('b (width: 32px)', '.ab-size > .size > span');\n   163\u2192  test_rejected('c (width)', '.ab-size > .size > span');\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192  // The following tests have three containers:\n   166\u2192  //\n   167\u2192  //  outer  -> 32x32px\n   168\u2192  //  middle -> 16x16px\n   169\u2192  //  inner -> 8x8px\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192  // Combinations of name and implicit selection:\n   172\u2192  test_applied('a (width: 8px)', '.a-size > .b-size > .a-inline > span');\n   173\u2192  test_applied('b (width: 16px)', '.a-size > .b-size > .a-inline > span');\n   174\u2192  test_applied('a (height: 32px)', '.a-size > .b-size > .a-inline > span');\n   175\u2192  test_rejected('a (height)', '.a-inline > .b-size');\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192  // Same tests as above, but logical versions:\n   178\u2192  test_applied('a (inline-size: 8px)', '.a-size > .b-size > .a-inline > span');\n   179\u2192  test_applied('b (inline-size: 16px)', '.a-size > .b-size > .a-inline > span');\n   180\u2192  test_applied('a (block-size: 32px)', '.a-size > .b-size > .a-inline > span');\n   181\u2192  test_rejected('a (block-size)', '.a-inline > .b-size');\n   182\u2192\n   183\u2192</script>\n   184\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
13. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/container-units-basic.html"
}
Output
"     1\u2192<!doctype html>\n     2\u2192<title>Container Relative Units: cqi, cqb, etc</title>\n     3\u2192<link rel=\"help\" href=\"https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#container-lengths\">\n     4\u2192<script src=\"/resources/testharness.js\"></script>\n     5\u2192<script src=\"/resources/testharnessreport.js\"></script>\n     6\u2192<script src=\"support/cq-testcommon.js\"></script>\n     7\u2192<style>\n     8\u2192  .inline { container-type: inline-size; }\n     9\u2192  .size { container-type: size; }\n    10\u2192  .inline.outer { width: 500px; }\n    11\u2192  .size.outer { height: 400px; }\n    12\u2192  .inline.inner { width: 300px; }\n    13\u2192</style>\n    14\u2192<div id=ref></div>\n    15\u2192<div class=\"inline outer\">\n    16\u2192  <div class=\"size outer\">\n    17\u2192    <div class=\"inline inner\">\n    18\u2192      <div id=child>Test</div>\n    19\u2192    </div>\n    20\u2192  </div>\n    21\u2192</div>\n    22\u2192<script>\n    23\u2192  setup(() => assert_implements_size_container_queries());\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192  function assert_unit_equals(element, actual, expected) {\n    26\u2192    try {\n    27\u2192      element.style.padding = actual;\n    28\u2192      ref.style.padding = expected;\n    29\u2192      assert_equals(getComputedStyle(element).paddingLeft,\n    30\u2192                    getComputedStyle(ref).paddingLeft);\n    31\u2192    } finally {\n    32\u2192      element.style = '';\n    33\u2192      ref.style = '';\n    34\u2192    }\n    35\u2192  }\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192  test(function() {\n    38\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '0cqi', '0px');\n    39\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '1cqi', '3px');\n    40\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqi', '30px');\n    41\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqw', '30px');\n    42\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqb', '40px');\n    43\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqh', '40px');\n    44\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqmin', '30px');\n    45\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqmax', '40px');\n    46\u2192  }, 'Container relative units');\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192  test(function() {\n    49\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqi', '30px');\n    50\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqb', '40px');\n    51\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, 'calc(10cqi + 10cqb)', '70px');\n    52\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, 'max(10cqi, 10cqb)', '40px');\n    53\u2192  }, 'Container relative units in math functions');\n    54\u2192</script>\n    55\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
14. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/container-type-containment.html"
}
Output
"     1\u2192<!doctype html>\n     2\u2192<title>CSS Container Queries Test: applied containment for container-type</title>\n     3\u2192<link rel=\"help\" href=\"https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#container-type\">\n     4\u2192<script src=\"/resources/testharness.js\"></script>\n     5\u2192<script src=\"/resources/testharnessreport.js\"></script>\n     6\u2192<script src=\"support/cq-testcommon.js\"></script>\n     7\u2192<script>\n     8\u2192  setup(() => assert_implements_size_container_queries());\n     9\u2192</script>\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192<style>\n    12\u2192  /* Note: background colors have no impact on the test result. They are\n    13\u2192           present to make it easier to visually verify that the test\n    14\u2192           does the right thing. */\n    15\u2192  .square {\n    16\u2192    width: 50px;\n    17\u2192    height: 50px;\n    18\u2192    background: tomato;\n    19\u2192  }\n    20\u2192  .half {\n    21\u2192    width: 25px;\n    22\u2192    height: 50px;\n    23\u2192    background: red;\n    24\u2192  }\n    25\u2192  div > div:nth-of-type(1) { background: skyblue; }\n    26\u2192  div > div:nth-of-type(2) { background: hotpink; }\n    27\u2192</style>\n    28\u2192\n    29\u2192<div id=test1 class=square>\n    30\u2192  <div id=float1 class=half style=\"float:left\"></div>\n    31\u2192  <div id=child1 class=half style=\"container-type:inline-size\"></div>\n    32\u2192</div>\n    33\u2192<script>\n    34\u2192  test(() => {\n    35\u2192    assert_equals(child1.offsetLeft, test1.offsetLeft + float1.offsetWidth);\n    36\u2192  }, 'container-type:inline-size turns on layout containment');\n    37\u2192</script>\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192<hr>\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192<div id=test2 class=square>\n    42\u2192  <div id=ref2 style=\"float:left\">A</div>\n    43\u2192  <div id=child2 style=\"float:left; container-type:inline-size\">A</div>\n    44\u2192</div>\n    45\u2192<script>\n    46\u2192  test(() => {\n    47\u2192    assert_equals(child2.offsetWidth, 0);\n    48\u2192    assert_equals(child2.offsetHeight, ref2.offsetHeight);\n    49\u2192  }, 'container-type:inline-size turns on inline-size containment');\n    50\u2192</script>\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192<hr>\n    53\u2192\n    54\u2192<div id=test3 class=square>\n    55\u2192  <div id=child3 style=\"float:left; container-type:size\">A</div>\n    56\u2192</div>\n    57\u2192<script>\n    58\u2192  test(() => {\n    59\u2192    assert_equals(child3.offsetHeight, 0);\n    60\u2192    assert_equals(child3.offsetWidth, 0);\n    61\u2192  }, 'container-type:size turns on full size containment');\n    62\u2192</script>\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192<hr>\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192<style>\n    67\u2192  #ref4::before, #child4::before {\n    68\u2192    content: counter(foo);\n    69\u2192  }\n    70\u2192</style>\n    71\u2192<div id=test4 class=square style=\"counter-set: foo 5\">\n    72\u2192  <div id=ref4 style=\"float:left\"></div>\n    73\u2192  <section style=\"container-type:inline-size\">\n    74\u2192    <span style=\"counter-increment: foo 1000;\"></span>\n    75\u2192  </section>\n    76\u2192  <section style=\"container-type:size\">\n    77\u2192    <span style=\"counter-increment: foo 1000;\"></span>\n    78\u2192  </section>\n    79\u2192  <div id=child4 style=\"float:left\"></div>\n    80\u2192</div>\n    81\u2192<script>\n    82\u2192  test(() => {\n    83\u2192    assert_equals(child4.offsetWidth, ref4.offsetWidth);\n    84\u2192  }, 'container-type:inline/size turns on style containment');\n    85\u2192</script>\n    86\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
15. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/support/cq-testcommon.js"
}
Output
"     1\u2192function assert_implements_size_container_queries() {\n     2\u2192  assert_implements(CSS.supports(\"container-type:size\"),\n     3\u2192                    \"Basic support for size container queries required\");\n     4\u2192}\n     5\u2192\n     6\u2192function assert_implements_scroll_state_container_queries() {\n     7\u2192  assert_implements(CSS.supports(\"container-type:scroll-state\"),\n     8\u2192                    \"Basic support for scroll-state container queries required\");\n     9\u2192}\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192function assert_implements_style_container_queries() {\n    12\u2192  // TODO: Replace with CSS.supports() when/if this can be expressed with at-rule().\n    13\u2192  const sheet = new CSSStyleSheet();\n    14\u2192  // No support means the style() function is <general-enclosed> which should\n    15\u2192  // affect serialization. Although serialization for <general-enclosed> is not\n    16\u2192  // specified[1], unknown function names are unlikely to be resolved to be\n    17\u2192  // serialized lower-case. Also, keeping the case is currently interoperable.\n    18\u2192  //\n    19\u2192  // [1] https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/7266\n    20\u2192  sheet.replaceSync('@container STYLE(--foo: bar){}');\n    21\u2192  assert_implements(sheet.cssRules[0].containerQuery === \"style(--foo: bar)\",\n    22\u2192                    \"Basic support for style container queries required\");\n    23\u2192}\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192function cleanup_container_query_main() {\n    26\u2192  const main = document.querySelector(\"#cq-main\");\n    27\u2192  while (main.firstChild)\n    28\u2192    main.firstChild.remove();\n    29\u2192}\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192function set_container_query_style(text) {\n    32\u2192  let style = document.createElement('style');\n    33\u2192  style.innerText = text;\n    34\u2192  document.querySelector(\"#cq-main\").append(style);\n    35\u2192  return style;\n    36\u2192}\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192function test_cq_rule_invalid(query) {\n    39\u2192  const ruleText = `@container ${query} {}`;\n    40\u2192  test(t => {\n    41\u2192    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    42\u2192    let style = set_container_query_style(ruleText);\n    43\u2192    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 0);\n    44\u2192  }, `@container rule should be invalid: ${ruleText} {}`);\n    45\u2192}\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192function test_cq_rule_valid(query) {\n    48\u2192  const ruleText = `@container ${query} {}`;\n    49\u2192  test(t => {\n    50\u2192    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    51\u2192    let style = set_container_query_style(`@container ${query} {}`);\n    52\u2192    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 1);\n    53\u2192  }, `@container rule should be valid: ${ruleText} {}`);\n    54\u2192}\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192function test_cq_condition_invalid(condition) {\n    57\u2192  test(t => {\n    58\u2192    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    59\u2192    let style = set_container_query_style(`@container name ${condition} {}`);\n    60\u2192    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 0);\n    61\u2192  }, `Query condition should be invalid: ${condition}`);\n    62\u2192}\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192// Tests that 1) the condition parses, and 2) is either \"unknown\" or not, as\n    65\u2192// specified.\n    66\u2192function test_cq_condition_valid(condition, unknown) {\n    67\u2192  test(t => {\n    68\u2192    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    69\u2192    let style = set_container_query_style(`\n    70\u2192      @container name ${condition} {}\n    71\u2192      @container name (${condition}) or (not (${condition})) { main { --match:true; } }\n    72\u2192    `);\n    73\u2192    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 2);\n    74\u2192    const expected = unknown ? '' : 'true';\n    75\u2192    assert_equals(getComputedStyle(document.querySelector(\"#cq-main\")).getPropertyValue('--match'), expected);\n    76\u2192  }, `Query condition should be valid${unknown ? ' but unknown' : ''}: ${condition}`);\n    77\u2192}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192function test_cq_condition_known(condition) {\n    80\u2192  test_cq_condition_valid(condition, false /* unknown */);\n    81\u2192}\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192function test_cq_condition_unknown(condition) {\n    84\u2192  test_cq_condition_valid(condition, true /* unknown */);\n    85\u2192}\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192function test_container_name_invalid(container_name) {\n    88\u2192  test(t => {\n    89\u2192    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    90\u2192    let style = set_container_query_style(`@container ${container_name} not (width) {}`);\n    91\u2192    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 0);\n    92\u2192  }, `Container name: ${container_name}`);\n    93\u2192}\n    94\u2192\n    95\u2192function test_container_name_valid(container_name) {\n    96\u2192  test(t => {\n    97\u2192    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    98\u2192    let style = set_container_query_style(`@container ${container_name} not (width) {}`);\n    99\u2192    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 1);\n   100\u2192  }, `Container name: ${container_name}`);\n   101\u2192}\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192function test_cq_condition_serialization(query, expected) {\n   104\u2192  test(t => {\n   105\u2192    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n   106\u2192    let style = set_container_query_style(`@container ${query} {}`);\n   107\u2192    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 1);\n   108\u2192    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules[0].conditionText, expected);\n   109\u2192  }, `@container conditionText serialization: ${query}`);\n   110\u2192}\n   111\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

pace/tests/wpt/tests/conformance-checkers/html-its/locqualityissue/xml/locqualityissue12xml.xml"

Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/container-parsing.html"
}
Output
"     1\u2192<!doctype html>\n     2\u2192<meta charset=\"utf-8\">\n     3\u2192<title>CSS Conditional Test: Parsing of container</title>\n     4\u2192<link rel=\"help\" href=\"https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#container-name\">\n     5\u2192<link rel=\"help\" href=\"https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#container-type\">\n     6\u2192<script src=\"/resources/testharness.js\"></script>\n     7\u2192<script src=\"/resources/testharnessreport.js\"></script>\n     8\u2192<script src=\"/css/support/parsing-testcommon.js\"></script>\n     9\u2192<script src=\"support/cq-testcommon.js\"></script>\n    10\u2192<div id=\"target\"></div>\n    11\u2192<script>\n    12\u2192setup(() => assert_implements_size_container_queries());\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'initial');\n    15\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'inherit');\n    16\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'unset');\n    17\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'revert');\n    18\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'none');\n    19\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'none / normal', 'none');\n    20\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'inline-size');\n    21\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'none / inline-size', 'none / inline-size');\n    22\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'size');\n    23\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'block-size / size');\n    24\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'inline-size / inline-size');\n    25\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'size / size');\n    26\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'foo');\n    27\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'foo / normal', 'foo');\n    28\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'foo bar / size');\n    29\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'foo bar / normal', 'foo bar');\n    30\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'FOO / size');\n    31\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'FOO/size', 'FOO / size');\n    32\u2192test_valid_value('container', '  FOO  /size', 'FOO / size');\n    33\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'normal / size');\n    34\u2192test_valid_value('container', 'auto / size');\n    35\u2192\n    36\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none none');\n    37\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none inline-size');\n    38\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none / inline-size none');\n    39\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none / inline-size normal');\n    40\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none / inline-size inline-size');\n    41\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none / inline-size block-size unknown');\n    42\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none / inline-size block-size');\n    43\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none / size block-size');\n    44\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none, none');\n    45\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none, normal');\n    46\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none / none');\n    47\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none / auto');\n    48\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none / foo');\n    49\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none / foo, bar');\n    50\u2192test_invalid_value('container', '#fff');\n    51\u2192test_invalid_value('container', '1px');\n    52\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'default');\n    53\u2192test_invalid_value('container', '10px / inline-size');\n    54\u2192test_invalid_value('container', '#fefefe / inline-size');\n    55\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'calc(3px) / inline-size');\n    56\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'size 1 / name');\n    57\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none / block-size');\n    58\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'name / block-size');\n    59\u2192test_invalid_value('container', ' NAME  / block-size', 'NAME / block-size');\n    60\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'NAME/block-size','NAME / block-size');\n    61\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'block-size / block-size');\n    62\u2192test_invalid_value('container', 'none / size style');\n    63\u2192</script>\n    64\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Output
"     1\u2192<!doctype html>\n     2\u2192<meta charset=\"utf-8\">\n     3\u2192<title>CSS Conditional Test: Parsing of container-type</title>\n     4\u2192<link rel=\"help\" href=\"https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#container-type\">\n     5\u2192<script src=\"/resources/testharness.js\"></script>\n     6\u2192<script src=\"/resources/testharnessreport.js\"></script>\n     7\u2192<script src=\"/css/support/parsing-testcommon.js\"></script>\n     8\u2192<script src=\"support/cq-testcommon.js\"></script>\n     9\u2192<div id=\"target\"></div>\n    10\u2192<script>\n    11\u2192setup(() => assert_implements_size_container_queries());\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192test_valid_value('container-type', 'initial');\n    14\u2192test_valid_value('container-type', 'inherit');\n    15\u2192test_valid_value('container-type', 'unset');\n    16\u2192test_valid_value('container-type', 'revert');\n    17\u2192test_valid_value('container-type', 'normal');\n    18\u2192test_valid_value('container-type', 'size');\n    19\u2192test_valid_value('container-type', 'inline-size');\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'none');\n    22\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'auto');\n    23\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'block-size');\n    24\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'normal normal');\n    25\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'normal inline-size');\n    26\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'inline-size normal');\n    27\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'inline-size inline-size');\n    28\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'inline-size block-size');\n    29\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'block-size inline-size');\n    30\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'size inline-size');\n    31\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'inline-size size');\n    32\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'normal, normal');\n    33\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'foo');\n    34\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', '\"foo\"');\n    35\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'foo, bar');\n    36\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', '#fff');\n    37\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', '1px');\n    38\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'default');\n    39\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'size nonsense');\n    40\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'style');\n    41\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'inline-size style', 'style inline-size');\n    42\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'style inline-size');\n    43\u2192test_invalid_value('container-type', 'style size');\n    44\u2192</script>\n    45\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Output
"     1\u2192<!DOCTYPE html>\n     2\u2192<title>CSS Conditional Test: @container scrollable query parsing</title>\n     3\u2192<link rel=\"help\" href=\"https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#container-rule\">\n     4\u2192<link rel=\"help\" href=\"https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#scrollable\">\n     5\u2192<script src=\"/resources/testharness.js\"></script>\n     6\u2192<script src=\"/resources/testharnessreport.js\"></script>\n     7\u2192<script src=\"/css/css-conditional/container-queries/support/cq-testcommon.js\"></script>\n     8\u2192<div style=\"container-name:name;container-type:scroll-state\">\n     9\u2192  <main id=\"cq-main\"></main>\n    10\u2192</div>\n    11\u2192<script>\n    12\u2192  setup(() => assert_implements_scroll_state_container_queries());\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(scrollable)');\n    15\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(scrollable: none)');\n    16\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(scrollable: top)');\n    17\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(scrollable: left)');\n    18\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(scrollable: bottom)');\n    19\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(scrollable: right)');\n    20\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(scrollable: inline-start)');\n    21\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(scrollable: inline-end)');\n    22\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(scrollable: block-start)');\n    23\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(scrollable: block-end)');\n    24\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(scrollable: block)');\n    25\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(scrollable: x)');\n    26\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(scrollable: y)');\n    27\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(scrollable: inline)');\n    28\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('(scroll-state(scrollable: inline-end))');\n    29\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state((scrollable: left))');\n    30\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state(not ((scrollable: bottom) and (scrollable: right)))');\n    31\u2192  test_cq_condition_known('scroll-state((scrollable: left) or (scrollable: top))');\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192  test_cq_condition_unknown('scroll-state(scrollable: auto)');\n    34\u2192  test_cq_condition_unknown('scroll-state(scrollable: true)');\n    35\u2192  test_cq_condition_unknown('scroll-state(style(scrollable: left))');\n    36\u2192  test_cq_condition_unknown('style(scroll-state(scrollable: left))');\n    37\u2192  test_cq_condition_unknown('scroll-state(scrollable:)');\n    38\u2192</script>\n    39\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
44. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/container-units-basic.html",
  "limit": 50
}
Output
"     1\u2192<!doctype html>\n     2\u2192<title>Container Relative Units: cqi, cqb, etc</title>\n     3\u2192<link rel=\"help\" href=\"https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#container-lengths\">\n     4\u2192<script src=\"/resources/testharness.js\"></script>\n     5\u2192<script src=\"/resources/testharnessreport.js\"></script>\n     6\u2192<script src=\"support/cq-testcommon.js\"></script>\n     7\u2192<style>\n     8\u2192  .inline { container-type: inline-size; }\n     9\u2192  .size { container-type: size; }\n    10\u2192  .inline.outer { width: 500px; }\n    11\u2192  .size.outer { height: 400px; }\n    12\u2192  .inline.inner { width: 300px; }\n    13\u2192</style>\n    14\u2192<div id=ref></div>\n    15\u2192<div class=\"inline outer\">\n    16\u2192  <div class=\"size outer\">\n    17\u2192    <div class=\"inline inner\">\n    18\u2192      <div id=child>Test</div>\n    19\u2192    </div>\n    20\u2192  </div>\n    21\u2192</div>\n    22\u2192<script>\n    23\u2192  setup(() => assert_implements_size_container_queries());\n    24\u2192\n    25\u2192  function assert_unit_equals(element, actual, expected) {\n    26\u2192    try {\n    27\u2192      element.style.padding = actual;\n    28\u2192      ref.style.padding = expected;\n    29\u2192      assert_equals(getComputedStyle(element).paddingLeft,\n    30\u2192                    getComputedStyle(ref).paddingLeft);\n    31\u2192    } finally {\n    32\u2192      element.style = '';\n    33\u2192      ref.style = '';\n    34\u2192    }\n    35\u2192  }\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192  test(function() {\n    38\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '0cqi', '0px');\n    39\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '1cqi', '3px');\n    40\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqi', '30px');\n    41\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqw', '30px');\n    42\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqb', '40px');\n    43\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqh', '40px');\n    44\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqmin', '30px');\n    45\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqmax', '40px');\n    46\u2192  }, 'Container relative units');\n    47\u2192\n    48\u2192  test(function() {\n    49\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqi', '30px');\n    50\u2192    assert_unit_equals(child, '10cqb', '40px');\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

54. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/Cargo.toml"
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "[workspace]\nresolver = \"2\"\nmembers = [\n    \"components/xpath\",\n    \"ports/servoshell\",\n    \"tests/unit/*\",\n]\ndefault-members = [\"ports/servoshell\"]\nexclude = [\".cargo\", \"support/crown\"]\n\n[workspace.package]\nversion = \"0.0.1\"\nauthors = [\"The Servo Project Developers\"]\nlicense = \"MPL-2.0\"\nedition = \"2024\"\npublish = false\n# We have yet to decide on a policy for updating the minimum supported Rust version.\n# Before increasing this, please open a discussion on Zulip explaining the reason\n# why we should consider increasing our minimum supported Rust version.\n# Please also note, that the **default** Rust version in rust-toolchain.toml may be\n# bumped freely.\n# Skip 1.90 which causes mis-compilation: https://github.com/rust-lang/rust/issues/147265\nrust-version = \"1.86.0\"\n\n[workspace.dependencies]\naccesskit = { version = \"0.21.1\", features = [\"serde\"] }\naccountable-refcell = \"0.2.2\"\naes = \"0.8.4\"\naes-gcm = \"0.10.3\"\naes-kw = { version = \"0.2.1\", features = [\"alloc\"] }\napp_units = \"0.7\"\narboard = \"3\"\nargon2 = { version = \"0.5\", features = [\"alloc\"] }\narrayvec = \"0.7\"\nasync-tungstenite = { version = \"0.32\", features = [\"tokio-rustls-webpki-roots\"] }\natomic_refcell = \"0.1.13\"\naws-lc-rs = { version = \"1.15\", default-features = false, features = [\"aws-lc-sys\"] }\nbackground_hang_monitor_api = { path = \"components/shared/background_hang_monitor\" }\nbacktrace = \"0.3\"\nbase = { path = \"components/shared/base\" }\nbase64 = \"0.22.1\"\nbase64ct = { version = \"1.8\", features = [\"alloc\"] }\nbitflags = \"2.10\"\nbluetooth_traits = { path = \"components/shared/bluetooth\" }\nbrotli = \"8.0.2\"\nbytemuck = \"1\"\nbyteorder = \"1.5\"\ncanvas_traits = { path = \"components/shared/canvas\" }\ncbc = \"0.1.2\"\ncfg-if = \"1.0.4\"\nchacha20poly1305 = \"0.10\"\nchardetng = \"0.1\"\nchrono = { version = \"0.4\", features = [\"serde\"] }\ncipher = { version = \"0.4.4\", features = [\"alloc\"] }\nconstellation_traits = { path = \"components/shared/constellation\" }\ncontent-security-policy = { version = \"0.6.0\", features = [\"serde\"] }\ncookie = { package = \"cookie\", version = \"0.18\" }\ncrossbeam-channel = \"0.5\"\ncssparser = { version = \"0.36\", features = [\"serde\"] }\nctr =
