\"0.1\"\ntokio-util = { version = \"0.7.18\", default-features = false }\ntower = { version = \"0.5\", default-features = false, features = [\"util\"] }\ntracing = \"0.1.44\"\ntracing-perfetto = \"0.1.5\"\ntracing-subscriber = \"0.3.22\"\ntungstenite = \"0.28\"\nunicode-bidi = \"0.3.18\"\nunicode-properties = { version = \"0.1.4\", features = [\"emoji\"] }\nunicode-script = \"0.5\"\nunicode-segmentation = \"1.12.0\"\nurl = \"2.5\"\nurlpattern = \"0.3\"\nutf-8 = \"0.7\"\nuuid = { version = \"1.20.0\", features = [\"v4\", \"v5\"] }\nvello = \"0.6\"\nvello_cpu = \"0.0.4\"\nwebdriver = \"0.53.0\"\nwebgpu_traits = { path = \"components/shared/webgpu\" }\nwebpki-roots = \"1.0\"\nwebrender = { version = \"0.68\", features = [\"capture\"] }\nwebrender_api = \"0.68\"\nwebxr-api = { path = \"components/shared/webxr\" }\nwgpu-core = \"26\"\nwgpu-types = \"26\"\nwinapi = \"0.3\"\nwindows-sys = \"0.61\"\nwinit = \"0.30.12\"\nwio = \"0.2\"\nwr_malloc_size_of = \"0.2.2\"\nx25519-dalek = { version = \"2.0.1\", features = [\"static_secrets\"] }\nxi-unicode = \"0.3.0\"\nxml5ever = \"0.38\"\nxpath = { path = \"components/xpath\" }\n\n# RSA key generation could be very slow without compilation\n# optimizations, in development mode. Without optimizations, WPT might\n# consider RSA key generation tests fail due to timeout.\n# Building with higher optimization levels can speed up the key\n# generation process to avoid false negative result in those tests.\n# More details: <https://docs.rs/rsa/0.9.8/rsa/index.html#example>\n[profile.dev.package.num-bigint-dig]\nopt-level = 3\n\n# Force tikv-jemalloc-sys to build with at least -O1.\n# This can help with build environments that force _FORTIFY_SOURCE, such as Nix.\n# More details: <https://github.com/tikv/jemallocator/issues/108>\n[profile.dev.package.tikv-jemalloc-sys]\nopt-level = 1\n\n[profile.release]\nopt-level = 3\ndebug-assertions = true\n\n# Disk-space is limited in CI, so we disabled features that are not needed for code coverage.\n# Locally using the default `dev` profile is perfectly fine.\n[profile.coverage]\ninherits = \"dev\"\nincremental = false\ndebug = false\n\n# A profile between `dev` and `release` which aims to offer a compromise between\n# fast incremental rebuilds and runtime speed.\n[profile.medium]\ninherits = \"release\"\nopt-level = 2\nincremental = true\ndebug = \"line-tables-only\"\n\n[profile.production]\ninherits = \"release\"\ndebug-assertions = false\nlto = true\ncodegen-units = 1\nopt-level = \"s\"\n\n[profile.production-stripped]\ninherits = \"production\"\nstrip = true\n\n[profile.profiling]\ninherits = \"release\"\ndebug-assertions = false\ndebug = true\nlto = \"thin\"\ncodegen-units = 1\n\n[patch.crates-io]\n# If you need to temporarily test Servo with a local fork of some upstream\n# crate, add that here. Use the form:\n#\n#     <crate> = { path = \"/path/to/local/checkout\" }\n#\n# For html5ever:\n#\n# html5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/html5ever\" }\n# markup5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/markup5ever\" }\n# web_atoms = { path = \"../html5ever/web_atoms\" }\n# xml5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/xml5ever\" }\n# tendril = { path = \"../html5ever/tendril\" }\n#\n# Or for Stylo:\n#\n# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/stylo\"]\n# selectors = { path = \"../stylo/selectors\" }\n# servo_arc = { path = \"../stylo/servo_arc\" }\n# stylo = { path = \"../stylo/style\" }\n# stylo_atoms = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_atoms\" }\n# stylo_config = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_config\" }\n# stylo_dom = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_dom\" }\n# stylo_malloc_size_of = { path = \"../stylo/malloc_size_of\" }\n# stylo_traits = { path = \"../stylo/style_traits\" }\n#\n# Or for WebRender:\n#\n# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/webrender\"]\n# webrender = { path = \"../webrender/webrender\" }\n# webrender_api = { path = \"../webrender/webrender_api\" }\n# wr_malloc_size_of = { path = \"../webrender/wr_malloc_size_of\" }\n#\n# Or for another Git dependency:\n#\n# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/<repository>\"]\n# <crate> = { path = \"/path/to/local/checkout\" }\n#\n# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/rust-content-security-policy\"]\n# content-security-policy = { path = \"../rust-content-security-policy\" }\n#\n# Or for mozjs:\n#\n# mozjs = { path = \"../mozjs/mozjs\" }\n# mozjs_sys = { path = \"../mozjs/mozjs-sys\" }\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/Cargo.toml",

57. Bash @ -
Input
{
  "command": "head -50 /workspace/mach",
  "description": "Check the Servo build script"
}
Output
{
  "interrupted": false,
  "isImage": false,
  "noOutputExpected": false,
  "stderr": "",
  "stdout": "#!/bin/sh\n# This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n# License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n# file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/.\n\n# The beginning of this script is both valid shell and valid python, such that\n# the script starts with the shell and is reexecuted with `uv run`. This\n# ensures that the Python provided by the virtual environment (in the .venv\n# directory) is used. If the virtual environment does not exist, `uv run` will\n# still use the correct version of Python given in `.python-version` and\n# python/mach_bootstrap.py will provision a new environment that will be used\n# for the subsequent runs.\n''':' && {\n    MACH_DIR=$(dirname \"$0\");\n    run_in_nix_if_needed() {\n        if { [ -f /etc/NIXOS ] || [ -n \"${MACH_USE_NIX}\" ]; } && [ -z \"${IN_NIX_SHELL}\" ]; then\n            EXTRA_NIX_ARGS=${SERVO_ANDROID_BUILD:+'--arg buildAndroid true'}\n\n            # `nix-shell` needs the whole command passed as a single argument, so the arguments need\n            # to be shell-quoted. Rotate through the arguments, replacing them with quoted versions.\n            for arg in \"$@\"; do\n                set -- \"$@\" \"$(printf \\%q \"$1\")\"\n                shift\n            done\n\n            echo \"NOTE: Entering nix-shell ${MACH_DIR}/shell.nix\"\n            exec nix-shell \"${MACH_DIR}/shell.nix\" $EXTRA_NIX_ARGS --run \"$*\"\n        else\n            exec \"$@\"\n        fi\n    }\n\n    run_in_nix_if_needed uv run --frozen python ${MACH_DIR}/mach \"$@\"\n}\n'''\n\nimport os\nimport sys\n\n# On NixOS, prepend nix-provided binary paths so they take precedence over .venv/bin\nif 'SERVO_NIX_BIN_DIR' in os.environ:\n    os.environ['PATH'] = os.environ['SERVO_NIX_BIN_DIR'] + os.pathsep + os.environ.get('PATH', '')\n\ndef main(args):\n    topdir = os.path.abspath(os.path.dirname(sys.argv[0]))\n    sys.path.insert(0, os.path.join(topdir, \"python\"))\n    import mach_bootstrap\n    if len(sys.argv) > 1 and sys.argv[1] == \"bootstrap\":\n        sys.exit(mach_bootstrap.bootstrap_command_only(topdir))\n    else:"
}

  "file": {
    "content": "<!doctype html>\n<meta charset=\"utf-8\">\n<title>CSS Conditional Test: Parsing of container</title>\n<link rel=\"help\" href=\"https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#container-name\">\n<link rel=\"help\" href=\"https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-5/#container-type\">\n<script src=\"/resources/testharness.js\"></script>\n<script src=\"/resources/testharnessreport.js\"></script>\n<script src=\"/css/support/parsing-testcommon.js\"></script>\n<script src=\"support/cq-testcommon.js\"></script>\n<div id=\"target\"></div>\n<script>\nsetup(() => assert_implements_size_container_queries());\n\ntest_valid_value('container', 'initial');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'inherit');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'unset');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'revert');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'none');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'none / normal', 'none');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'inline-size');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'none / inline-size', 'none / inline-size');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'size');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'block-size / size');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'inline-size / inline-size');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'size / size');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'foo');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'foo / normal', 'foo');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'foo bar / size');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'foo bar / normal', 'foo bar');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'FOO / size');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'FOO/size', 'FOO / size');\ntest_valid_value('container', '  FOO  /size', 'FOO / size');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'normal / size');\ntest_valid_value('container', 'auto / size');\n\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none none');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none inline-size');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none / inline-size none');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none / inline-size normal');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none / inline-size inline-size');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none / inline-size block-size unknown');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none / inline-size block-size');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none / size block-size');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none, none');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none, normal');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none / none');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none / auto');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none / foo');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none / foo, bar');\ntest_invalid_value('container', '#fff');\ntest_invalid_value('container', '1px');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'default');\ntest_invalid_value('container', '10px / inline-size');\ntest_invalid_value('container', '#fefefe / inline-size');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'calc(3px) / inline-size');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'size 1 / name');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none / block-size');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'name / block-size');\ntest_invalid_value('container', ' NAME  / block-size', 'NAME / block-size');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'NAME/block-size','NAME / block-size');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'block-size / block-size');\ntest_invalid_value('container', 'none / size style');\n</script>\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/container-parsing.html",
    "numLines": 64,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 64

src=\"/resources/testharness.js\"></script>\n<script src=\"/resources/testharnessreport.js\"></script>\n<script src=\"support/cq-testcommon.js\"></script>\n<div style=\"container-name:name;container-type:size; width:100px; height:100px\">\n  <main id=\"cq-main\"></main>\n</div>\n<script>\n  setup(() => assert_implements_size_container_queries());\n\n  test_cq_condition_known('(width)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(min-width: 0px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(max-width: 0px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(height)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(min-height: 0px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(max-height: 0px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(aspect-ratio)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(min-aspect-ratio: 1/2)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(max-aspect-ratio: 1/2)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(orientation: portrait)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(inline-size)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(min-inline-size: 0px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(max-inline-size: 0px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(block-size)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(min-block-size: 0px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(max-block-size: 0px)');\n\n  test_cq_condition_known('(width: 100px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('((width: 100px))');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(not (width: 100px))');\n  test_cq_condition_known('((width: 100px) and (height: 100px))');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(((width: 40px) or (width: 50px)) and (height: 100px))');\n  test_cq_condition_known('((width: 100px) and 50px)))');\n  test_cq_condition_known('((width: 100px) and (not (height: 100px)))');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(width < 100px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(width <= 100px)');\n test_cq_condition_known('(100px <= width)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(100px = width)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(100px > width)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(100px >= width)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(100px < width < 200px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(100px < width <= 200px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(100px <= width < 200px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(100px > width > 200px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(100px > width >= 200px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(100px >= width > 200px)');\n\n  test_cq_condition_known('(width: calc(10px))');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(width: calc(10em))');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(width: calc(10px + 10em))');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(width < calc(10px + 10em))');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(width < max(10px, 10em))');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(calc(10px + 10em) < width)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(calc(10px + 10em) < width < max(30px, 30em))');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(width: 100px) and (height: 100px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('(width: 100px) or (height: 100px)');\n  test_cq_condition_known('not (width: 100px)');\n\n  test_cq_condition_unknown('foo(width)');\n 62. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/support/cq-testcommon.js",
  "limit": 150
}
Output
{
  "file": {
    "content": "function assert_implements_size_container_queries() {\n  assert_implements(CSS.supports(\"container-type:size\"),\n                    \"Basic support for size container queries required\");\n}\n\nfunction assert_implements_scroll_state_container_queries() {\n  assert_implements(CSS.supports(\"container-type:scroll-state\"),\n                    \"Basic support for scroll-state container queries required\");\n}\n\nfunction assert_implements_style_container_queries() {\n  // TODO: Replace with CSS.supports() when/if this can be expressed with at-rule().\n  const sheet = new CSSStyleSheet();\n  // No support means the style() function is <general-enclosed> which should\n  // affect serialization. Although serialization for <general-enclosed> is not\n  // specified[1], unknown function names are unlikely to be resolved to be\n  // serialized lower-case. Also, keeping the case is currently interoperable.\n  //\n  // [1] https://github.com/w3c/csswg-drafts/issues/7266\n  sheet.replaceSync('@container STYLE(--foo: bar){}');\n  assert_implements(sheet.cssRules[0].containerQuery === \"style(--foo: bar)\",\n                    \"Basic support for style container queries required\");\n}\n\nfunction cleanup_container_query_main() {\n  const main = document.querySelector(\"#cq-main\");\n  while (main.firstChild)\n    main.firstChild.remove();\n}\n\nfunction set_container_query_style(text) {\n  let style = document.createElement('style');\n  style.innerText = text;\n  document.querySelector(\"#cq-main\").append(style);\n  return style;\n}\n\nfunction test_cq_rule_invalid(query) {\n  const ruleText = `@container ${query} {}`;\n  test(t => {\n    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    let style = set_container_query_style(ruleText);\n    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 0);\n  }, `@container rule should be invalid: ${ruleText} {}`);\n}\n\nfunction test_cq_rule_valid(query) {\n  const ruleText = `@container ${query} {}`;\n  test(t => {\n    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    let style = set_container_query_style(`@container ${query} {}`);\n    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 1);\n  }, `@container rule should be valid: ${ruleText} {}`);\n}\n\nfunction test_cq_condition_invalid(condition) {\n  test(t => {\n    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    let style = set_container_query_style(`@container name ${condition} {}`);\n    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 0);\n  }, `Query condition should be invalid: ${condition}`);\n}\n\n// Tests that 1) the condition parses, and 2) is either \"unknown\" or not, as\n// specified.\nfunction test_cq_condition_valid(condition, unknown) {\n  test(t => {\n    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    let style = set_container_query_style(`\n      @container name ${condition} {}\n      @container name (${condition}) or (not (${condition})) { main { --match:true; } }\n    `);\n    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 2);\n    const expected = unknown ? '' : 'true';\n    assert_equals(getComputedStyle(document.querySelector(\"#cq-main\")).getPropertyValue('--match'), expected);\n  }, `Query condition should be valid${unknown ? ' but unknown' : ''}: ${condition}`);\n}\n\nfunction test_cq_condition_known(condition) {\n  test_cq_condition_valid(condition, false /* unknown */);\n}\n\nfunction test_cq_condition_unknown(condition) {\n  test_cq_condition_valid(condition, true /* unknown */);\n}\n\nfunction test_container_name_invalid(container_name) {\n  test(t => {\n    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    let style = set_container_query_style(`@container ${container_name} not (width) {}`);\n    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 0);\n  }, `Container name: ${container_name}`);\n}\n\nfunction test_container_name_valid(container_name) {\n  test(t => {\n    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    let style = set_container_query_style(`@container ${container_name} not (width) {}`);\n    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 1);\n  }, `Container name: ${container_name}`);\n}\n\nfunction test_cq_condition_serialization(query, expected) {\n  test(t => {\n    t.add_cleanup(cleanup_container_query_main);\n    let style = set_container_query_style(`@container ${query} {}`);\n    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules.length, 1);\n    assert_equals(style.sheet.rules[0].conditionText, expected);\n  }, `@container conditionText serialization: ${query}`);\n}\n",
    "filePath": "/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/support/cq-testcommon.js",
    "numLines": 111,
    "startLine": 1,
    "totalLines": 111
  },
  "type": "text"
}
63. Write @ -
Input
{
  "content": "# Container Queries Implementation Progress\n\n## Project Context\n- Implementing CSS Container Queries in Servo browser\n- Tests are in `/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/`\n- Over 300+ test files covering various aspects\n\n## Architecture\n- Servo uses **Stylo** as its CSS engine (external crate from https://github.com/servo/stylo)\n- Stylo is imported as a git dependency in Cargo.toml (line 178)\n- Style system is NOT in workspace components but in external stylo crate\n\n## Required Features to Implement\n1. `container-type` CSS property (values: normal, size, inline-size, scroll-state)\n2. `container-name` CSS property (values: none or custom idents)\n3. `@container` at-rule parsing with conditions\n4. Size query evaluation (min-width, max-width, width, height, etc.)\n5. Comparison operators: <, <=, =, >=, >\n6. Container relative units: cqi, cqw, cqh, cqb, cqmin, cqmax\n7. Style queries with style() function\n8. Scroll state queries\n\n## Test Files\n- **Parsing tests:** container-parsing.html, container-type-parsing.html\n- **At-rule tests:** at-container-parsing.html, at-container-serialization.html\n- **Size features:** size-feature-evaluation.html, aspect-ratio-feature-evaluation.html\n- **Units:** container-units-basic.html\n- **Invalidation:** Multiple invalidation tests\n- **Shadow DOM:** container-for-shadow-dom.html\n- **Total:** 314 test files\n\n## Testing Infrastructure\n- Uses testharness.js (JavaScript test framework)\n- Key functions from cq-testcommon.js:\n  - `assert_implements_size_container_queries()` - checks CSS.supports(\"container-type:size\")\n  - `test_cq_rule_valid/invalid()` - validates @container parsing\n  - `test_cq_condition_known/unknown()` - validates conditions\n  - `getComputedStyle()` - checks computed values\n\n## Next Steps\n1. Understand Stylo crate structure\n2. Check if container queries already partially implemented\n3. Implement missing parts in Stylo\n4. Run tests to verify implementation\n",
  "file_path": "/logs/agent/sessions/projects/-workspace/memory/MEMORY.md"
}
Output
{
  "content": "# Container Queries Implementation Progress\n\n## Project Context\n- Implementing CSS Container Queries in Servo browser\n- Tests are in `/workspace/tests/wpt/tests/css/css-conditional/container-queries/`\n- Over 300+ test files covering various aspects\n\n## Architecture\n- Servo uses **Stylo** as its CSS engine (external crate from https://github.com/servo/stylo)\n- Stylo is imported as a git dependency in Cargo.toml (line 178)\n- Style system is NOT in workspace components but in external stylo crate\n\n## Required Features to Implement\n1. `container-type` CSS property (values: normal, size, inline-size, scroll-state)\n2. `container-name` CSS property (values: none or custom idents)\n3. `@container` at-rule parsing with conditions\n4. Size query evaluation (min-width, max-width, width, height, etc.)\n5. Comparison operators: <, <=, =, >=, >\n6. Container relative units: cqi, cqw, cqh, cqb, cqmin, cqmax\n7. Style queries with style() function\n8. Scroll state queries\n\n## Test Files\n- **Parsing tests:** container-parsing.html, container-type-parsing.html\n- **At-rule tests:** at-container-parsing.html, at-container-serialization.html\n- **Size features:** size-feature-evaluation.html, aspect-ratio-feature-evaluation.html\n- **Units:** container-units-basic.html\n- **Invalidation:** Multiple invalidation tests\n- **Shadow DOM:** container-for-shadow-dom.html\n- **Total:** 314 test files\n\n## Testing Infrastructure\n- Uses testharness.js (JavaScript test framework)\n- Key functions from cq-testcommon.js:\n  - `assert_implements_size_container_queries()` - checks CSS.supports(\"container-type:size\")\n  - `test_cq_rule_valid/invalid()` - validates @container parsing\n  - `test_cq_condition_known/unknown()` - validates conditions\n  - `getComputedStyle()` - checks computed values\n\n## Next Steps\n1. Understand Stylo crate structure\n2. Check if container queries already partially implemented\n3. Implement missing parts in Stylo\n4. Run tests to verify implementation\n",

65. Agent @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Find where CSS properties and rules are defined in Servo",
  "prompt": "Search the Servo codebase to find:\n1. Where CSS property definitions are (look for longhand, shorthand, properties)\n2. Where at-rule parsing is handled (look for RuleParser, AtRuleParser, @media, @supports)\n3. Where style matching/cascade logic is implemented\n4. How media queries are implemented as a reference for container queries\n\nCheck in all components directories, especially layout, script, and shared components. Be comprehensive in searching for any CSS-related code patterns.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "a4a79757a1ea0ed40",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary of my findings:\n\n## Comprehensive Search Results: CSS Architecture in Servo\n\nI've completed a thorough search of the Servo codebase. Here's what I found:\n\n---\n\n## 1. CSS PROPERTY DEFINITIONS AND PARSING\n\n**Location**: Properties are defined and parsed through **Stylo** (external crate from GitHub)\n\n### Key Files:\n- **/workspace/components/script/css.rs** - CSS parser context factory\n  - `parser_context_for_document()` - Creates ParserContext with document quirks mode and error reporting\n  - `parser_context_for_document_with_reporter()` - Custom error reporter variant\n  - `parser_context_for_anonymous_content()` - For parsing without a document\n\n- **/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssstyledeclaration.rs** - Style declaration access\n  - Handles property declaration blocks\n  - Uses `PropertyDeclarationBlock`, `LonghandId`, `ShorthandId`, `PropertyId` from Stylo\n  - Methods like `parse_one_declaration_into` for property parsing\n\n- **/workspace/tests/unit/style/parsing/** - Unit test modules for properties:\n  - `background.rs` - background properties\n  - `border.rs` - border properties\n  - `box_.rs` - box-related properties\n  - `effects.rs` - visual effects\n  - `image.rs` - image properties\n  - Plus many others for specific property groups\n\n### Property Architecture:\n```\nStylo (GitHub: servo/stylo rev 3b46f3ef27eb14ca276b4bf3ff041a6c43d93827)\n  \u251c\u2500\u2500 style::properties::ComputedValues (computed style)\n  \u251c\u2500\u2500 style::properties::PropertyId (property identifiers)\n  \u251c\u2500\u2500 style::properties::LonghandId (longhand properties)\n  \u251c\u2500\u2500 style::properties::ShorthandId (shorthand properties)\n  \u2514\u2500\u2500 style::properties::PropertyDeclarationBlock (declaration collections)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 2. AT-RULE PARSING\n\n**Location**: At-rule parsing is primarily handled in DOM bindings with Stylo doing the actual parsing\n\n### Key At-Rule Files in `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/`:\n\n1. **Media Queries** (@media):\n   - `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssmediarule.rs`\n   - `/workspace/components/script/dom/media/medialist.rs`\n   - Uses `style::media_queries::MediaList` and `MediaQuery`\n   - Evaluation happens via `MediaList::evaluate()` with device context\n\n2. **@supports**:\n   - `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/csssupportsrule.rs`\n   - Uses `style::stylesheets::supports_rule::SupportsCondition`\n\n3. **Condition Rules** (base for @media/@supports):\n   - `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssconditionrule.rs`\n   - Handles condition text and rule collections\n\n4. **Other At-Rules**:\n   - `cssimportrule.rs` - @import rules\n   - `csskeyframesrule.rs` - @keyframes\n   - `cssfontfacerule.rs` - @font-face\n   - `cssnamespacerule.rs` - @namespace\n   - `csslayerblockrule.rs` - @layer (block form)\n   - `csslayerstatementrule.rs` - @layer (statement form)\n   - `csspropertyrule.rs` - @property (custom properties)\n\n5. **Rule Parsing Dispatch**:\n   - `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssrule.rs`\n   - Maps `StyleCssRule` (from Stylo) to DOM rule types via `new_specific()` method\n   - Supports: Import, Style, FontFace, Keyframes, Media, Namespace, Supports, LayerBlock, LayerStatement, Property, NestedDeclarations\n\n---\n\n## 3. STYLE MATCHING AND CASCADE LOGIC\n\n**Location**: Primarily in Stylo, with integration points in Servo's layout component\n\n### Key Files:\n\n**In `/workspace/components/layout/`**:\n- **layout_impl.rs** - Main layout thread\n  - Uses `style::stylist::Stylist` for \"CSS selector matching and style resolution\"\n  - Maintains `style::context::SharedStyleContext` for matching context\n  - Loads stylesheets into stylist via UserAgentStylesheets\n  - Contains stylesheet files:\n    - `stylesheets/user-agent.css`\n    - `stylesheets/servo.css`\n    - `stylesheets/presentational-hints.css`\n    - `stylesheets/quirks-mode.css`\n\n- **context.rs** - Layout context\n  - `struct LayoutContext` wraps `SharedStyleContext`\n  - Provides context for style resolution during layout\n\n- **query.rs** - Style query interface\n  - Uses `style::traversal::resolve_style` for computing styles\n  - Handles style queries for element matching\n\n**Stylo Integration** (from `Cargo.toml`):\n```rust\nstylo = { git = \"https://github.com/servo/stylo\", ... }\n  \u251c\u2500\u2500 style::stylist::Stylist - Matcher and resolver\n  \u251c\u2500\u2500 style::context::SharedStyleContext - Shared matching state\n  \u251c\u2500\u2500 style::driver - Style system entry points\n  \u2514\u2500\u2500 style::traversal - DOM traversal for styling\n```\n\n---\n\n## 4. MEDIA QUERY IMPLEMENTATION\n\n**Location**: Servo leverages Stylo's media query system\n\n### Key Components:\n\n**Media Query Parsing and Evaluation**:\n- **File**: `/workspace/components/script/dom/media/medialist.rs`\n- **Key Methods**:\n  - `StyleMediaList::parse()` - Parses media query strings\n  - `StyleMediaList::evaluate()` - Evaluates media queries against device\n  - Uses `Device` context to check media features\n\n**Media Query List for JavaScript**:\n- **File**: `/workspace/components/script/dom/media/mediaquerylist.rs`\n- Implements DOM `MediaQueryList` interface\n- Tracks match state changes for event firing\n- Key method: `evaluate()` which calls:\n  ```rust\n  self.media_query_list.evaluate(\n      self.document.window().layout().device(),\n      quirks_mode,\n      &mut CustomMediaEvaluator::none(),\n  )\n  ```\n\n**Media Query Matching in @media Rules**:\n- **File**: `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssmediarule.rs`\n- Wraps `Arc<MediaRule>` from Stylo\n- Accesses media queries via `mediarule.media_queries`\n\n**Device Context**:\n- Located in Stylo: `style::media_queries::Device`\n- Passed from layout: `self.document.window().layout().device()`\n- Contains viewport size, media type, and feature values\n\n**Custom Media Support**:\n- Uses `CustomMediaEvaluator` for evaluating custom media definitions\n- Currently defaults to `CustomMediaEvaluator::none()` in Servo\n\n---\n\n## 5. STYLESHEET LOADING AND MANAGEMENT\n\n### Key Files:\n\n- **/workspace/components/script/stylesheet_loader.rs**\n  - Asynchronous stylesheet loading\n  - Handles network fetches via traits like `StylesheetOwner`\n  - Creates stylesheets via `Stylesheet::from_bytes()`\n  - Manages media, import rules, layer syntax\n\n- **/workspace/components/script/stylesheet_set.rs**\n  - `StylesheetSetRef` enum (Author/Document variants)\n  - Methods: `append_stylesheet()`, `insert_stylesheet_before()`, `remove_stylesheet()`\n  - Uses `Device` for invalidation tracking\n\n- **/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssstylesheet.rs**\n  - DOM `CSSStyleSheet` interface\n  - Wraps Stylo's `Stylesheet` in `Arc<StyleStyleSheet>`\n  - Maintains `SharedRwLock` for thread-safe access\n  - Tracks origin-clean flag and modification state\n\n---\n\n## 6. PROPERTY REGISTRATION (@property)\n\n**Location**: `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/csspropertyrule.rs`\n\nHandled via:\n- `style::properties_and_values::rule` (from Stylo)\n- `PropertyRegistration` with syntax and inheritance info\n- `PropertyRegistrationError` for validation\n\n---\n\n## 7. COMPLETE CSS RULE HIERARCHY\n\nAll 27 CSS-related DOM files in `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/`:\n\n```\ncssrule.rs (base dispatcher)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cssstylesheet.rs (container)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cssrulelist.rs (collection)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cssgroupingrule.rs (base for nested rules)\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 cssmediarule.rs\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 csssupportsrule.rs\n\u2502   \u251c\u2500\u2500 csslayerblockrule.rs\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 cssnesteddeclarations.rs\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cssstyledeclaration.rs (style properties)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cssstylerule.rs (style rules with selectors)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cssimportrule.rs (@import)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cssfontfacerule.rs (@font-face)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 csskeyframesrule.rs (@keyframes)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 csskeyframerule.rs (@keyframe single)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cssnamespacerule.rs (@namespace)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 csspropertyrule.rs (@property)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 csslayerstatementrule.rs (@layer statement)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cssconditionrule.rs (base for @media/@supports)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 stylesheet.rs (base stylesheet interface)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 stylesheetlist.rs (collection)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 stylesheetcontentscache.rs (caching)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 fontface.rs (@font-face implementation)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 fontfaceset.rs (collection)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cssstylevalue.rs (CSS value representation)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 stylepropertymapreadonly.rs (computed styles map)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 cssconditional rule.rs\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 css.rs (module root)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. STYLE CONTEXT AND PARSING MODES\n\nFrom `/workspace/components/script/css.rs`:\n- **ParserContext** creation respects:\n  - Document quirks mode\n  - CSS error reporter\n  - Rule type (CssRuleType::Media, CssRuleType::Style, etc.)\n  - Parsing mode (DEFAULT, all(), etc.)\n  - URL extra data for resolving URLs\n\n---\n\n## 9. ARCHITECTURE DIAGRAM\n\n```\nJavaScript DOM API\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 CSSStyleSheet \u2192 Stylo's Arc<Stylesheet>\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 CSSRule (dispatch) \u2192 Specific rule types\n\u2502   \u2514\u2500\u2500 CSSMediaRule \u2192 MediaList.evaluate()\n\u2502       \u2514\u2500\u2500 Device context from layout thread\n\u2502           \u2514\u2500\u2500 Viewport features, media type\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 CSSStyleDeclaration \u2192 PropertyDeclarationBlock\n\nLayout Thread (layout_impl.rs)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 Stylist (style matching engine)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 SharedStyleContext (matching state)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 Device (viewport + features)\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 DocumentStyleSheet + AuthorStylesheets\n\nStylo (External, GitHub: servo/stylo)\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 style::parser::ParserContext\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 style::media_queries::{MediaList, MediaQuery, Device}\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 style::stylesheets::{Stylesheet, CssRule, Rule types}\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 style::properties::{PropertyId, ComputedValues, ...}\n\u251c\u2500\u2500 style::stylist::Stylist\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 style::traversal (DOM style computation)\n\ncssparser (External: v0.36)\n\u2514\u2500\u2500 For low-level token parsing\n```\n\n---\n\n## Summary\n\nServo's CSS architecture is built upon **Stylo**, Mozilla's shared style system. Key architectural points:\n\n1. **Property definitions** are in Stylo's `style::properties` module with longhand/shorthand support\n2. **At-rule parsing** uses Stylo's parser with DOM wrappers for JavaScript access\n3. **Style matching/cascade** is performed by Stylo's `Stylist` with device context from layout\n4. **Media queries** are evaluated by `MediaList::evaluate()` against device features\n5. **All CSS rules** are parsed by Stylo and wrapped with DOM interfaces in script component\n6. **Property registration** for custom properties (@property) is supported via Stylo's properties_and_values module\n\nThe separation allows Servo to benefit from Stylo's CSS engine while providing standard DOM APIs for JavaScript.",
      "type": "text"
    }
  ],
  "prompt": "Search the Servo codebase to find:\n1. Where CSS property definitions are (look for longhand, shorthand, properties)\n2. Where at-rule parsing is handled (look for RuleParser, AtRuleParser, @media, @supports)\n3. Where style matching/cascade logic is implemented\n4. How media queries are implemented as a reference for container queries\n\nCheck in all components directories, especially layout, script, and shared components. Be comprehensive in searching for any CSS-related code patterns.",

default-features = false, features = [\"util\"] }\n   197\u2192tracing = \"0.1.44\"\n   198\u2192tracing-perfetto = \"0.1.5\"\n   199\u2192tracing-subscriber = \"0.3.22\"\n   200\u2192tungstenite = \"0.28\"\n   201\u2192unicode-bidi = \"0.3.18\"\n   202\u2192unicode-properties = { version = \"0.1.4\", features = [\"emoji\"] }\n   203\u2192unicode-script = \"0.5\"\n   204\u2192unicode-segmentation = \"1.12.0\"\n   205\u2192url = \"2.5\"\n   206\u2192urlpattern = \"0.3\"\n   207\u2192utf-8 = \"0.7\"\n   208\u2192uuid = { version = \"1.20.0\", features = [\"v4\", \"v5\"] }\n   209\u2192vello = \"0.6\"\n   210\u2192vello_cpu = \"0.0.4\"\n   211\u2192webdriver = \"0.53.0\"\n   212\u2192webgpu_traits = { path = \"components/shared/webgpu\" }\n   213\u2192webpki-roots = \"1.0\"\n   214\u2192webrender = { version = \"0.68\", features = [\"capture\"] }\n   215\u2192webrender_api = \"0.68\"\n   216\u2192webxr-api = { path = \"components/shared/webxr\" }\n   217\u2192wgpu-core = \"26\"\n   218\u2192wgpu-types = \"26\"\n   219\u2192winapi = \"0.3\"\n   220\u2192windows-sys = \"0.61\"\n   221\u2192winit = \"0.30.12\"\n   222\u2192wio = \"0.2\"\n   223\u2192wr_malloc_size_of = \"0.2.2\"\n   224\u2192x25519-dalek = { version = \"2.0.1\", features = [\"static_secrets\"] }\n   225\u2192xi-unicode = \"0.3.0\"\n   226\u2192xml5ever = \"0.38\"\n   227\u2192xpath = { path = \"components/xpath\" }\n   228\u2192\n   229\u2192# RSA key generation could be very slow without compilation\n   230\u2192# optimizations, in development mode. Without optimizations, WPT might\n   231\u2192# consider RSA key generation tests fail due to timeout.\n   232\u2192# Building with higher optimization levels can speed up the key\n   233\u2192# generation process to avoid false negative result in those tests.\n   234\u2192# More details: <https://docs.rs/rsa/0.9.8/rsa/index.html#example>\n   235\u2192[profile.dev.package.num-bigint-dig]\n   236\u2192opt-level = 3\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192# Force tikv-jemalloc-sys to build with at least -O1.\n   239\u2192# This can help with build environments that force _FORTIFY_SOURCE, such as Nix.\n   240\u2192# More details: <https://github.com/tikv/jemallocator/issues/108>\n   241\u2192[profile.dev.package.tikv-jemalloc-sys]\n   242\u2192opt-level = 1\n   243\u2192\n   244\u2192[profile.release]\n   245\u2192opt-level = 3\n   246\u2192debug-assertions = true\n   247\u2192\n   248\u2192# Disk-space is limited in CI, so we disabled features that are not needed for code coverage.\n   249\u2192# Locally using the default `dev` profile is perfectly fine.\n   250\u2192[profile.coverage]\n   251\u2192inherits = \"dev\"\n   252\u2192incremental = false\n   253\u2192debug = false\n   254\u2192\n   255\u2192# A profile between `dev` and `release` which aims to offer a compromise between\n   256\u2192# fast incremental rebuilds and runtime speed.\n   257\u2192[profile.medium]\n   258\u2192inherits = \"release\"\n   259\u2192opt-level = 2\n   260\u2192incremental = true\n   261\u2192debug = \"line-tables-only\"\n   262\u2192\n   263\u2192[profile.production]\n   264\u2192inherits = \"release\"\n   265\u2192debug-assertions = false\n   266\u2192lto = true\n   267\u2192codegen-units = 1\n   268\u2192opt-level = \"s\"\n   269\u2192\n   270\u2192[profile.production-stripped]\n   271\u2192inherits = \"production\"\n   272\u2192strip = true\n   273\u2192\n   274\u2192[profile.profiling]\n   275\u2192inherits = \"release\"\n   276\u2192debug-assertions = false\n   277\u2192debug = true\n   278\u2192lto = \"thin\"\n   279\u2192codegen-units = 1\n   280\u2192\n   281\u2192[patch.crates-io]\n   282\u2192# If you need to temporarily test Servo with a local fork of some upstream\n   283\u2192# crate, add that here. Use the form:\n   284\u2192#\n   285\u2192#     <crate> = { path = \"/path/to/local/checkout\" }\n   286\u2192#\n   287\u2192# For html5ever:\n   288\u2192#\n   289\u2192# html5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/html5ever\" }\n   290\u2192# markup5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/markup5ever\" }\n   291\u2192# web_atoms = { path = \"../html5ever/web_atoms\" }\n   292\u2192# xml5ever = { path = \"../html5ever/xml5ever\" }\n   293\u2192# tendril = { path = \"../html5ever/tendril\" }\n   294\u2192#\n   295\u2192# Or for Stylo:\n   296\u2192#\n   297\u2192# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/stylo\"]\n   298\u2192# selectors = { path = \"../stylo/selectors\" }\n   299\u2192# servo_arc = { path = \"../stylo/servo_arc\" }\n   300\u2192# stylo = { path = \"../stylo/style\" }\n   301\u2192# stylo_atoms = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_atoms\" }\n   302\u2192# stylo_config = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_config\" }\n   303\u2192# stylo_dom = { path = \"../stylo/stylo_dom\" }\n   304\u2192# stylo_malloc_size_of = { path = \"../stylo/malloc_size_of\" }\n   305\u2192# stylo_traits = { path = \"../stylo/style_traits\" }\n   306\u2192#\n   307\u2192# Or for WebRender:\n   308\u2192#\n   309\u2192# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/webrender\"]\n   310\u2192# webrender = { path = \"../webrender/webrender\" }\n   311\u2192# webrender_api = { path = \"../webrender/webrender_api\" }\n   312\u2192# wr_malloc_size_of = { path = \"../webrender/wr_malloc_size_of\" }\n   313\u2192#\n   314\u2192# Or for another Git dependency:\n   315\u2192#\n   316\u2192# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/<repository>\"]\n   317\u2192# <crate> = { path = \"/path/to/local/checkout\" }\n   318\u2192#\n   319\u2192# [patch.\"https://github.com/servo/rust-content-security-policy\"]\n   320\u2192# content-security-policy = { path = \"../rust-content-security-policy\" }\n   321\u2192#\n   322\u2192# Or for mozjs:\n   323\u2192#\n   324\u2192# mozjs = { path = \"../mozjs/mozjs\" }\n   325\u2192# mozjs_sys = { path = \"../mozjs/mozjs-sys\" }\n   326\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Output
"     1\u2192/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n     2\u2192 * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3\u2192 * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192use std::cell::RefCell;\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192use dom_struct::dom_struct;\n     8\u2192use servo_arc::Arc;\n     9\u2192use style::shared_lock::{SharedRwLockReadGuard, ToCssWithGuard};\n    10\u2192use style::stylesheets::{CssRuleType, MediaRule};\n    11\u2192use style_traits::ToCss;\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192use super::cssconditionrule::CSSConditionRule;\n    14\u2192use super::cssrule::SpecificCSSRule;\n    15\u2192use super::cssstylesheet::CSSStyleSheet;\n    16\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::CSSMediaRuleBinding::CSSMediaRuleMethods;\n    17\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::reflector::{DomGlobal, reflect_dom_object};\n    18\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::root::{DomRoot, MutNullableDom};\n    19\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\n    20\u2192use crate::dom::medialist::MediaList;\n    21\u2192use crate::dom::window::Window;\n    22\u2192use crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192#[dom_struct]\n    25\u2192pub(crate) struct CSSMediaRule {\n    26\u2192    cssconditionrule: CSSConditionRule,\n    27\u2192    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"Stylo\"]\n    28\u2192    #[no_trace]\n    29\u2192    mediarule: RefCell<Arc<MediaRule>>,\n    30\u2192    medialist: MutNullableDom<MediaList>,\n    31\u2192}\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192impl CSSMediaRule {\n    34\u2192    fn new_inherited(parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet, mediarule: Arc<MediaRule>) -> CSSMediaRule {\n    35\u2192        let list = mediarule.rules.clone();\n    36\u2192        CSSMediaRule {\n    37\u2192            cssconditionrule: CSSConditionRule::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet, list),\n    38\u2192            mediarule: RefCell::new(mediarule),\n    39\u2192            medialist: MutNullableDom::new(None),\n    40\u2192        }\n    41\u2192    }\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192    pub(crate) fn new(\n    44\u2192        window: &Window,\n    45\u2192        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n    46\u2192        mediarule: Arc<MediaRule>,\n    47\u2192        can_gc: CanGc,\n    48\u2192    ) -> DomRoot<CSSMediaRule> {\n    49\u2192        reflect_dom_object(\n    50\u2192            Box::new(CSSMediaRule::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet, mediarule)),\n    51\u2192            window,\n    52\u2192            can_gc,\n    53\u2192        )\n    54\u2192    }\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192    fn medialist(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<MediaList> {\n    57\u2192        self.medialist.or_init(|| {\n    58\u2192            MediaList::new(\n    59\u2192                self.global().as_window(),\n    60\u2192                self.cssconditionrule.parent_stylesheet(),\n    61\u2192                self.mediarule.borrow().media_queries.clone(),\n    62\u2192                can_gc,\n    63\u2192            )\n    64\u2192        })\n    65\u2192    }\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#the-cssmediarule-interface>\n    68\u2192    pub(crate) fn get_condition_text(&self) -> DOMString {\n    69\u2192        let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\n    70\u2192        self.mediarule\n    71\u2192            .borrow()\n    72\u2192            .media_queries\n    73\u2192            .read_with(&guard)\n    74\u2192            .to_css_string()\n    75\u2192            .into()\n    76\u2192    }\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192    pub(crate) fn update_rule(&self, mediarule: Arc<MediaRule>, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) {\n    79\u2192        self.cssconditionrule\n    80\u2192            .update_rules(mediarule.rules.clone(), guard);\n    81\u2192        if let Some(medialist) = self.medialist.get() {\n    82\u2192            medialist.update_media_list(mediarule.media_queries.clone());\n    83\u2192        }\n    84\u2192        *self.mediarule.borrow_mut() = mediarule;\n    85\u2192    }\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192impl SpecificCSSRule for CSSMediaRule {\n    89\u2192    fn ty(&self) -> CssRuleType {\n    90\u2192        CssRuleType::Media\n    91\u2192    }\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192    fn get_css(&self) -> DOMString {\n    94\u2192        let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\n    95\u2192        self.mediarule.borrow().to_css_string(&guard).into()\n    96\u2192    }\n    97\u2192}\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192impl CSSMediaRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSMediaRule {\n   100\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssgroupingrule-media>\n   101\u2192    fn Media(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<MediaList> {\n   102\u2192        self.medialist(can_gc)\n   103\u2192    }\n   104\u2192}\n   105\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

7\u2192use dom_struct::dom_struct;\n     8\u2192use servo_arc::Arc;\n     9\u2192use style::shared_lock::{SharedRwLockReadGuard, ToCssWithGuard};\n    10\u2192use style::stylesheets::{CssRuleType, SupportsRule};\n    11\u2192use style_traits::ToCss;\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192use super::cssconditionrule::CSSConditionRule;\n    14\u2192use super::cssrule::SpecificCSSRule;\n    15\u2192use super::cssstylesheet::CSSStyleSheet;\n    16\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::reflector::reflect_dom_object;\n    17\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::root::DomRoot;\n    18\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\n    19\u2192use crate::dom::window::Window;\n    20\u2192use crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192#[dom_struct]\n    23\u2192pub(crate) struct CSSSupportsRule {\n    24\u2192    cssconditionrule: CSSConditionRule,\n    25\u2192    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"Stylo\"]\n    26\u2192    #[no_trace]\n    27\u2192    supportsrule: RefCell<Arc<SupportsRule>>,\n    28\u2192}\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192impl CSSSupportsRule {\n    31\u2192    fn new_inherited(\n    32\u2192        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n    33\u2192        supportsrule: Arc<SupportsRule>,\n    34\u2192    ) -> CSSSupportsRule {\n    35\u2192        let list = supportsrule.rules.clone();\n    36\u2192        CSSSupportsRule {\n    37\u2192            cssconditionrule: CSSConditionRule::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet, list),\n    38\u2192            supportsrule: RefCell::new(supportsrule),\n    39\u2192        }\n    40\u2192    }\n    41\u2192\n    42\u2192    pub(crate) fn new(\n    43\u2192        window: &Window,\n    44\u2192        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n    45\u2192        supportsrule: Arc<SupportsRule>,\n    46\u2192        can_gc: CanGc,\n    47\u2192    ) -> DomRoot<CSSSupportsRule> {\n    48\u2192        reflect_dom_object(\n    49\u2192            Box::new(CSSSupportsRule::new_inherited(\n    50\u2192                parent_stylesheet,\n    51\u2192                supportsrule,\n    52\u2192            )),\n    53\u2192            window,\n    54\u2192            can_gc,\n    55\u2192        )\n    56\u2192    }\n    57\u2192\n    58\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#the-csssupportsrule-interface>\n    59\u2192    pub(crate) fn get_condition_text(&self) -> DOMString {\n    60\u2192        self.supportsrule.borrow().condition.to_css_string().into()\n    61\u2192    }\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192    pub(crate) fn update_rule(\n    64\u2192        &self,\n    65\u2192        supportsrule: Arc<SupportsRule>,\n    66\u2192        guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,\n    67\u2192    ) {\n    68\u2192        self.cssconditionrule\n    69\u2192            .update_rules(supportsrule.rules.clone(), guard);\n    70\u2192        *self.supportsrule.borrow_mut() = supportsrule;\n    71\u2192    }\n    72\u2192}\n    73\u2192\n    74\u2192impl SpecificCSSRule for CSSSupportsRule {\n    75\u2192    fn ty(&self) -> CssRuleType {\n    76\u2192        CssRuleType::Supports\n    77\u2192    }\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192    fn get_css(&self) -> DOMString {\n    80\u2192        let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\n    81\u2192        self.supportsrule.borrow().to_css_string(&guard).into()\n    82\u2192    }\n    83\u2192}\n    84\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. "     1\u2192/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n     2\u2192 * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3\u2192 * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192use std::cell::RefCell;\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192use dom_struct::dom_struct;\n     8\u2192use servo_arc::Arc;\n     9\u2192use style::shared_lock::{Locked, SharedRwLock, SharedRwLockReadGuard};\n    10\u2192use style::stylesheets::CssRules as StyleCssRules;\n    11\u2192\n    12\u2192use super::cssgroupingrule::CSSGroupingRule;\n    13\u2192use super::cssmediarule::CSSMediaRule;\n    14\u2192use super::cssstylesheet::CSSStyleSheet;\n    15\u2192use super::csssupportsrule::CSSSupportsRule;\n    16\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::CSSConditionRuleBinding::CSSConditionRuleMethods;\n    17\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::inheritance::Castable;\n    18\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\n    19\u2192\n    20\u2192#[dom_struct]\n    21\u2192pub(crate) struct CSSConditionRule {\n    22\u2192    cssgroupingrule: CSSGroupingRule,\n    23\u2192    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"Stylo\"]\n    24\u2192    #[no_trace]\n    25\u2192    rules: RefCell<Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>>>,\n    26\u2192}\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192impl CSSConditionRule {\n    29\u2192    pub(crate) fn new_inherited(\n    30\u2192        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n    31\u2192        rules: Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>>,\n    32\u2192    ) -> CSSConditionRule {\n    33\u2192        CSSConditionRule {\n    34\u2192            cssgroupingrule: CSSGroupingRule::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet),\n    35\u2192            rules: RefCell::new(rules),\n    36\u2192        }\n    37\u2192    }\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192    pub(crate) fn parent_stylesheet(&self) -> &CSSStyleSheet {\n    40\u2192        self.cssgroupingrule.parent_stylesheet()\n    41\u2192    }\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192    pub(crate) fn shared_lock(&self) -> &SharedRwLock {\n    44\u2192        self.cssgroupingrule.shared_lock()\n    45\u2192    }\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192    pub(crate) fn clone_rules(&self) -> Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>> {\n    48\u2192        self.rules.borrow().clone()\n    49\u2192    }\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192    pub(crate) fn update_rules(\n    52\u2192        &self,\n    53\u2192        rules: Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>>,\n    54\u2192        guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,\n    55\u2192    ) {\n    56\u2192        self.cssgroupingrule.update_rules(&rules, guard);\n    57\u2192        *self.rules.borrow_mut() = rules;\n    58\u2192    }\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192impl CSSConditionRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSConditionRule {\n    62\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#dom-cssconditionrule-conditiontext>\n    63\u2192    fn ConditionText(&self) -> DOMString {\n    64\u2192        if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\n    65\u2192            rule.get_condition_text()\n    66\u2192        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\n    67\u2192            rule.get_condition_text()\n    68\u2192        } else {\n    69\u2192            unreachable!()\n    70\u2192        }\n    71\u2192    }\n    72\u2192}\n    73\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

{\n    78\u2192    LinkElement,\n    79\u2192    Import(Arc<Locked<ImportRule>>),\n    80\u2192}\n    81\u2192\n    82\u2192/// The context required for asynchronously loading an external stylesheet.\n    83\u2192struct StylesheetContext {\n    84\u2192    /// The element that initiated the request.\n    85\u2192    element: Trusted<HTMLElement>,\n    86\u2192    source: StylesheetContextSource,\n    87\u2192    media: Arc<Locked<MediaList>>,\n    88\u2192    url: ServoUrl,\n    89\u2192    metadata: Option<Metadata>,\n    90\u2192    /// The response body received to date.\n    91\u2192    data: Vec<u8>,\n    92\u2192    /// The node document for elem when the load was initiated.\n    93\u2192    document: Trusted<Document>,\n    94\u2192    shadow_root: Option<Trusted<ShadowRoot>>,\n    95\u2192    origin_clean: bool,\n    96\u2192    /// A token which must match the generation id of the `HTMLLinkElement` for it to load the stylesheet.\n    97\u2192    /// This is ignored for `HTMLStyleElement` and imports.\n    98\u2192    request_generation_id: Option<RequestGenerationId>,\n    99\u2192    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#contributes-a-script-blocking-style-sheet>\n   100\u2192    is_script_blocking: bool,\n   101\u2192    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#render-blocking>\n   102\u2192    is_render_blocking: bool,\n   103\u2192}\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192impl StylesheetContext {\n   106\u2192    fn unminify_css(&mut self, file_url: ServoUrl) {\n   107\u2192        let Some(unminified_dir) = self.document.root().window().unminified_css_dir() else {\n   108\u2192            return;\n   109\u2192        };\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192        let mut style_content = std::mem::take(&mut self.data);\n   112\u2192        if let Some((input, mut output)) = create_temp_files() {\n   113\u2192            if execute_js_beautify(\n   114\u2192                input.path(),\n   115\u2192                output.try_clone().unwrap(),\n   116\u2192                BeautifyFileType::Css,\n   117\u2192            ) {\n   118\u2192                output.seek(std::io::SeekFrom::Start(0)).unwrap();\n   119\u2192                output.read_to_end(&mut style_content).unwrap();\n   120\u2192            }\n   121\u2192        }\n   122\u2192        match create_output_file(unminified_dir, &file_url, None) {\n   123\u2192            Ok(mut file) => {\n   124\u2192                file.write_all(&style_content).unwrap();\n   125\u2192            },\n   126\u2192            Err(why) => {\n   127\u2192                log::warn!(\"Could not store script {:?}\", why);\n   128\u2192            },\n   129\u2192        }\n   130\u2192\n   131\u2192        self.data = style_content;\n   132\u2192    }\n   133\u2192\n   134\u2192    fn empty_stylesheet(&self, document: &Document) -> Arc<Stylesheet> {\n   135\u2192        let shared_lock = document.style_shared_lock().clone();\n   136\u2192        let quirks_mode = document.quirks_mode();\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192        Arc::new(Stylesheet::from_bytes(\n   139\u2192            &[],\n   140\u2192            UrlExtraData(self.url.get_arc()),\n   141\u2192            None,\n   142\u2192            None,\n   143\u2192            Origin::Author,\n   144\u2192            self.media.clone(),\n   145\u2192            shared_lock,\n   146\u2192            None,\n   147\u2192            None,\n   148\u2192            quirks_mode,\n   149\u2192        ))\n   150\u2192    }\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

StylesheetInDocument};\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192/// Functionality common to DocumentStylesheetSet and AuthorStylesheetSet.\n    11\u2192pub(crate) enum StylesheetSetRef<'a, S>\n    12\u2192where\n    13\u2192    S: StylesheetInDocument + PartialEq + 'static,\n    14\u2192{\n    15\u2192    /// Author stylesheet set.\n    16\u2192    Author(&'a mut AuthorStylesheetSet<S>),\n    17\u2192    /// Document stylesheet set.\n    18\u2192    Document(&'a mut DocumentStylesheetSet<S>),\n    19\u2192}\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192impl<S> StylesheetSetRef<'_, S>\n    22\u2192where\n    23\u2192    S: StylesheetInDocument + PartialEq + 'static,\n    24\u2192{\n    25\u2192    /// Appends a new stylesheet to the current set.\n    26\u2192    ///\n    27\u2192    /// No device implies not computing invalidations.\n    28\u2192    pub(crate) fn append_stylesheet(\n    29\u2192        &mut self,\n    30\u2192        device: Option<&Device>,\n    31\u2192        sheet: S,\n    32\u2192        guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,\n    33\u2192    ) {\n    34\u2192        let custom_media = &CustomMediaMap::default();\n    35\u2192        match self {\n    36\u2192            StylesheetSetRef::Author(set) => {\n    37\u2192                set.append_stylesheet(device, custom_media, sheet, guard)\n    38\u2192            },\n    39\u2192            StylesheetSetRef::Document(set) => {\n    40\u2192                set.append_stylesheet(device, custom_media, sheet, guard)\n    41\u2192            },\n    42\u2192        }\n    43\u2192    }\n    44\u2192\n    45\u2192    /// Insert a given stylesheet before another stylesheet in the document.\n    46\u2192    pub(crate) fn insert_stylesheet_before(\n    47\u2192        &mut self,\n    48\u2192        device: Option<&Device>,\n    49\u2192        sheet: S,\n    50\u2192        before_sheet: S,\n    51\u2192        guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,\n    52\u2192    ) {\n    53\u2192        let custom_media = &CustomMediaMap::default();\n    54\u2192        match self {\n    55\u2192            StylesheetSetRef::Author(set) => {\n    56\u2192                set.insert_stylesheet_before(device, custom_media, sheet, before_sheet, guard)\n    57\u2192            },\n    58\u2192            StylesheetSetRef::Document(set) => {\n    59\u2192                set.insert_stylesheet_before(device, custom_media, sheet, before_sheet, guard)\n    60\u2192            },\n    61\u2192        }\n    62\u2192    }\n    63\u2192\n    64\u2192    /// Remove a given stylesheet from the set.\n    65\u2192    pub(crate) fn remove_stylesheet(\n    66\u2192        &mut self,\n    67\u2192        device: Option<&Device>,\n    68\u2192        sheet: S,\n    69\u2192        guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,\n    70\u2192    ) {\n    71\u2192        let custom_media = &CustomMediaMap::default();\n    72\u2192        match self {\n    73\u2192 MediaQuery};\n    11\u2192use style::parser::ParserContext;\n    12\u2192use style::shared_lock::{Locked, SharedRwLock};\n    13\u2192use style::stylesheets::{CssRuleType, CustomMediaEvaluator, Origin, UrlExtraData};\n    14\u2192use style_traits::{ParseError, ParsingMode, ToCss};\n    15\u2192\n    16\u2192use crate::css::parser_context_for_document;\n    17\u2192use crate::dom::window::Window;\n    26\u2192use crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\n    27\u2192\n    28\u2192#[dom_struct]\n    29\u2192pub(crate) struct MediaList {\n    30\u2192    reflector_: Reflector,\n    31\u2192    parent_stylesheet: Dom<CSSStyleSheet>,\n    32\u2192    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"Stylo\"]\n    33\u2192    #[no_trace]\n    34\u2192    media_queries:    40\u2192        media_queries: Arc<Locked<StyleMediaList>>,\n    41\u2192    ) -> MediaList {\n    42\u2192        MediaList {\n    43\u2192            parent_stylesheet: Dom::from_ref(parent_stylesheet),\n    44\u2192            reflector_: Reflector::new(),\n    45\u2192            media_queries: RefCell::new(media_queries),\n    46\u2192        }\n    47\u2192    }\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192    pub(crate) fn new(\n    50\u2192        window: &Window,\n    51\u2192        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n    52\u2192        media_queries: Arc<Locked<StyleMediaList>>,\n    53\u2192        can_gc: CanGc,\n    54\u2192    ) -> DomRoot<MediaList> {\n    55\u2192        reflect_dom_object(\n    56\u2192            Box::new(MediaList::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet, media_queries)),\n    57\u2192            window,\n    58\u2192            can_gc,\n    59\u2192        )\n    60\u2192    }\n    61\u2192\n    62\u2192    fn shared_lock(&self) -> &SharedRwLock {\n    63\u2192        self.parent_stylesheet.shared_lock()\n    64\u2192    }\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#parse-a-media-query-list>\n    67\u2192    pub(crate) fn parse_media_list(value: &str, window: &Window) -> StyleMediaList {\n    68\u2192        if value.is_empty() {\n    69\u2192            return StyleMediaList::empty();\n    70\u2192        }\n    71\u2192        let mut input = ParserInput::new(value);\n    72\u2192        let mut parser = Parser::new(&mut input);\n    73\u2192        let document = window.Document();\n    74\u2192        let url_data = UrlExtraData(document.owner_global().api_base_url().get_arc());\n    75\u2192        // FIXME(emilio): This looks somewhat fishy, since we use the context\n    76\u2192        // only to parse the media query list, CssRuleType::Media doesn't make\n    77\u2192        // much sense.\n    78\u2192        let context = parser_context_for_document(\n    79\u2192            &document,\n    80\u2192            CssRuleType::Media,\n    81\u2192            ParsingMode::DEFAULT,\n    82\u2192            &url_data,\n    83\u2192        );\n    84\u2192        StyleMediaList::parse(&context, &mut parser)\n    85\u2192    }\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#parse-a-media-query>\n    88\u2192    pub(crate) fn parse_media_query<'i>(\n    89\u2192        value: &'i str,\n    90\u2192        window: &Window,\n    91\u2192    ) -> Result<MediaQuery, ParseError<'i>> {\n    92\u2192        let mut input = ParserInput::new(value);\n    93\u2192        let mut parser = Parser::new(&mut input);\n    94\u2192        let document = window.Document();\n    95\u2192        let url_data = UrlExtraData(document.owner_global().api_base_url().get_arc());\n    96\u2192        let context = parser_context_for_document(\n    97\u2192            &document,\n    98\u2192            CssRuleType::Media,\n    99\u2192            ParsingMode::DEFAULT,\n   100\u2192            &url_data,\n   101\u2192        );\n   102\u2192        MediaQuery::parse(&context, &mut parser)\n   103\u2192    }\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192    pub(crate) fn clone_media_list(&self) -> StyleMediaList {\n   106\u2192        let guard = self.shared_lock().read();\n   107\u2192        self.media_queries.borrow().read_with(&guard).clone()\n   108\u2192    }\n   109\u2192\n   110\u2192    pub(crate) fn update_media_list(&self, media_queries: Arc<Locked<StyleMediaList>>) {\n   111\u2192        *self.media_queries.borrow_mut() = media_queries;\n   112\u2192    }\n   113\u2192\n   114\u2192    /// <https://html.spec.whatwg.org/multipage/#matches-the-environment>\n   115\u2192    pub(crate) fn matches_environment(document: &Document, media_query: &str) -> bool {\n   116\u2192        let quirks_mode = document.quirks_mode();\n   117\u2192        let url_data = UrlExtraData(document.owner_global().api_base_url().get_arc());\n   118\u2192        // FIXME(emilio): This should do the same that we do for other media\n   119\u2192        // lists regarding the rule type and such, though it doesn't really\n   120\u2192        // matter right now...\n   121\u2192        //\n   122\u2192        // Also, ParsingMode::all() is wrong, and should be DEFAULT.\n   123\u2192        let context = ParserContext::new(\n   124\u2192            Origin::Author,\n   125\u2192            &url_data,\n   126\u2192            Some(CssRuleType::Style),\n   127\u2192            ParsingMode::all(),\n   128\u2192            quirks_mode,\n   129\u2192            /* namespaces = */ Default::default(),\n   130\u2192            None,\n   131\u2192            None,\n   132\u2192        );\n   133\u2192        let mut parser_input = ParserInput::new(media_query);\n   134\u2192        let mut parser = Parser::new(&mut parser_input);\n   135\u2192        let media_list = StyleMediaList::parse(&context, &mut parser);\n   136\u2192        media_list.evaluate(\n   137\u2192            document.window().layout().device(),\n   138\u2192            quirks_mode,\n   139\u2192            &mut CustomMediaEvaluator::none(),\n   140\u2192        )\n   141\u2192    }\n   142\u2192}\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192impl MediaListMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for MediaList {\n   145\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-medialist-mediatext>\n   146\u2192    fn MediaText(&self) -> DOMString {\n   147\u2192        let guard = self.shared_lock().read();\n   148\u2192        DOMString::from(\n   149\u2192            self.media_queries\n   150\u2192                .borrow()\n   151\u2192                .read_with(&guard)\n   152\u2192                .to_css_string(),\n   153\u2192        )\n   154\u2192    }\n   155\u2192\n   156\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-medialist-mediatext>\n   157\u2192    fn SetMediaText(&self, value: DOMString) {\n   158\u2192        self.parent_stylesheet.will_modify();\n   159\u2192        let global = self.global();\n   160\u2192        let mut guard = self.shared_lock().write();\n   161\u2192        let media_queries_borrowed = self.media_queries.borrow();\n   162\u2192        let media_queries = media_queries_borrowed.write_with(&mut guard);\n   163\u2192        *media_queries = Self::parse_media_list(&value.str(), global.as_window());\n   164\u2192        self.parent_stylesheet.notify_invalidations();\n   165\u2192    }\n   166\u2192\n   167\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-medialist-length>\n   168\u2192    fn Length(&self) -> u32 {\n   169\u2192        let guard = self.shared_lock().read();\n   170\u2192        self.media_queries\n   171\u2192            .borrow()\n   172\u2192            .read_with(&guard)\n   173\u2192            .media_queries\n   174\u2192            .len() as u32\n   175\u2192    }\n   176\u2192\n   177\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-medialist-item>\n   178\u2192    fn Item(&self, index: u32) -> Option<DOMString> {\n   179\u2192        let guard = self.shared_lock().read();\n   180\u2192        self.media_queries\n   181\u2192            .borrow()\n   182\u2192            .read_with(&guard)\n   183\u2192            .media_queries\n   184\u2192            .get(index as usize)\n   185\u2192            .map(|query| query.to_css_string().into())\n   186\u2192    }\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-medialist-item>\n   189\u2192    fn IndexedGetter(&self, index: u32) -> Option<DOMString> {\n   190\u2192        self.Item(index)\n   191\u2192    }\n   192\u2192\n   193\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-medialist-appendmedium>\n   194\u2192    fn AppendMedium(&self, medium: DOMString) {\n   195\u2192        // Step 1\n   196\u2192        let global = self.global();\n   197\u2192        let medium = medium.str();\n   198\u2192        let m = Self::parse_media_query(&medium, global.as_window());\n   199\u2192        // Step 2\n   200\u2192        if m.is_err() {\n   201\u2192            return;\n   202\u2192        }\n   203\u2192        // Step 3\n   204\u2192        {\n   205\u2192            let m_serialized = m.clone().unwrap().to_css_string();\n   206\u2192            let guard = self.shared_lock().read();\n   207\u2192            let any = self\n   208\u2192                .media_queries\n   209\u2192                .borrow()\n   210\u2192                .read_with(&guard)\n   211\u2192                .media_queries\n   212\u2192                .iter()\n   213\u2192                .any(|q| m_serialized == q.to_css_string());\n   214\u2192            if any {\n   215\u2192                return;\n   216\u2192            }\n   217\u2192        }\n   218\u2192        // Step 4\n   219\u2192        self.parent_stylesheet.will_modify();\n   220\u2192        let mut guard = self.shared_lock().write();\n   221\u2192        self.media_queries\n   222\u2192            .borrow()\n   223\u2192            .write_with(&mut guard)\n   224\u2192            .media_queries\n   225\u2192            .push(m.unwrap());\n   226\u2192        self.parent_stylesheet.notify_invalidations();\n   227\u2192    }\n   228\u2192\n   229\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-medialist-deletemedium>\n   230\u2192    fn DeleteMedium(&self, medium: DOMString) {\n   231\u2192        // Step 1\n   232\u2192        let global = self.global();\n   233\u2192        let medium = medium.str();\n   234\u2192        let m = Self::parse_media_query(&medium, global.as_window());\n   235\u2192        // Step 2\n   236\u2192        if m.is_err() {\n   237\u2192            return;\n   238\u2192        }\n   239\u2192        // Step 3\n   240\u2192        self.parent_stylesheet.will_modify();\n   241\u2192        let m_serialized = m.unwrap().to_css_string();\n   242\u2192        let mut guard = self.shared_lock().write();\n   243\u2192        let media_queries_borrowed = self.media_queries.borrow();\n   244\u2192        let media_list = media_queries_borrowed.write_with(&mut guard);\n   245\u2192        let new_vec = media_list\n   246\u2192            .media_queries\n   247\u2192            .drain(..)\n   248\u2192            .filter(|q| m_serialized != q.to_css_string())\n   249\u2192            .collect();\n   250\u2192        media_list.media_queries = new_vec;\n   251\u2192        self.parent_stylesheet.notify_invalidations();\n   252\u2192    }\n   253\u2192}\n   254\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. std::rc::Rc;\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192use dom_struct::dom_struct;\n     9\u2192use style::media_queries::MediaList;\n    10\u2192use style::stylesheets::CustomMediaEvaluator;\n    11\u2192use style_traits::ToCss;\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::EventListenerBinding::EventListener;\n    14\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::EventTargetBinding::{\n    15\u2192    AddEventListenerOptions, EventListenerOptions,\n    16\u2192};\n    17\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::MediaQueryListBinding::MediaQueryListMethods;\n    18\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::inheritance::Castable;\n    19\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::reflector::reflect_dom_object;\n    20\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::root::{Dom, DomRoot};\n    21\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\n    22\u2192use crate::dom::document::Document;\n    23\u2192use crate::dom::eventtarget::EventTarget;\n    24\u2192use crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\n    25\u2192\n    26\u2192pub(crate) enum MediaQueryListMatchState {\n    27\u2192    Same,\n    28\u2192    Changed,\n    29\u2192}\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192#[dom_struct]\n    32\u2192pub(crate) struct MediaQueryList {\n    33\u2192    eventtarget: EventTarget,\n    34\u2192    document: Dom<Document>,\n    35\u2192    #[no_trace]\n    36\u2192    media_query_list: MediaList,\n    37\u2192    last_match_state: Cell<Option<bool>>,\n    38\u2192}\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192impl MediaQueryList {\n    41\u2192    fn new_inherited(document: &Document, media_query_list: MediaList) -> MediaQueryList {\n    42\u2192        MediaQueryList {\n    43\u2192            eventtarget: EventTarget::new_inherited(),\n    44\u2192            document: Dom::from_ref(document),\n    45\u2192            media_query_list,\n    46\u2192            last_match_state: Cell::new(None),\n    47\u2192        }\n    48\u2192    }\n    49\u2192\n    50\u2192    pub(crate) fn new(\n    51\u2192        document: &Document,\n    52\u2192        media_query_list: MediaList,\n    53\u2192        can_gc: CanGc,\n    54\u2192    ) -> DomRoot<MediaQueryList> {\n    55\u2192        reflect_dom_object(\n    56\u2192            Box::new(MediaQueryList::new_inherited(document, media_query_list)),\n    57\u2192            document.window(),\n    58\u2192            can_gc,\n    59\u2192        )\n    60\u2192    }\n    61\u2192}\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192impl MediaQueryList {\n    64\u2192    pub(crate) fn evaluate_changes(&self) -> MediaQueryListMatchState {\n    65\u2192        let matches = self.evaluate();\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192        let result = if let Some(old_matches) = self.last_match_state.get() {\n    68\u2192            if old_matches == matches {\n    69\u2192                MediaQueryListMatchState::Same\n    70\u2192            } else {\n    71\u2192                MediaQueryListMatchState::Changed\n    72\u2192            }\n    73\u2192        } else {\n    74\u2192            MediaQueryListMatchState::Changed\n    75\u2192        };\n    76\u2192\n    77\u2192        self.last_match_state.set(Some(matches));\n    78\u2192        result\n    79\u2192    }\n    80\u2192\n    81\u2192    pub(crate) fn evaluate(&self) -> bool {\n    82\u2192        let quirks_mode = self.document.quirks_mode();\n    83\u2192        self.media_query_list.evaluate(\n    84\u2192            self.document.window().layout().device(),\n    85\u2192            quirks_mode,\n    86\u2192            &mut CustomMediaEvaluator::none(),\n    87\u2192        )\n    88\u2192    }\n    89\u2192}\n    90\u2192\n    91\u2192impl MediaQueryListMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for MediaQueryList {\n    92\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#dom-mediaquerylist-media>\n    93\u2192    fn Media(&self) -> DOMString {\n    94\u2192        self.media_query_list.to_css_string().into()\n    95\u2192    }\n    96\u2192\n    97\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#dom-mediaquerylist-matches>\n    98\u2192    fn Matches(&self) -> bool {\n    99\u2192        match self.last_match_state.get() {\n   100\u2192            None => self.evaluate(),\n   101\u2192            Some(state) => state,\n   102\u2192        }\n   103\u2192    }\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#dom-mediaquerylist-addlistener>\n   106\u2192    fn AddListener(&self, listener: Option<Rc<EventListener>>) {\n   107\u2192        self.upcast::<EventTarget>().add_event_listener(\n   108\u2192            DOMString::from_string(\"change\".to_owned()),\n   109\u2192            listener,\n   110\u2192            AddEventListenerOptions {\n   111\u2192                parent: EventListenerOptions { capture: false },\n   112\u2192                once: false,\n   113\u2192                passive: None,\n   114\u2192                signal: None,\n   115\u2192            },\n   116\u2192        );\n   117\u2192    }\n   118\u2192\n   119\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#dom-mediaquerylist-removelistener>\n   120\u2192    fn RemoveListener(&self, listener: Option<Rc<EventListener>>) {\n   121\u2192        self.upcast::<EventTarget>().remove_event_listener(\n   122\u2192            DOMString::from_string(\"change\".to_owned()),\n   123\u2192            &listener,\n   124\u2192            &EventListenerOptions { capture: false },\n   125\u2192        );\n   126\u2192    }\n   127\u2192\n   128\u2192    // https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom-view/#dom-mediaquerylist-onchange\n   129\u2192    event_handler!(change, GetOnchange, SetOnchange);\n   130\u2192}\n   131\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
