Result<CachedImage, ResolveImageError>;\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192pub(crate) struct LayoutContext<'a> {\n    32\u2192    pub use_rayon: bool,\n    33\u2192\n    34\u2192    /// Bits shared by the layout and style system.\n    35\u2192    pub style_context: SharedStyleContext<'a>,\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192    /// A FontContext to be used during layout.\n    38\u2192    pub font_context: Arc<FontContext>,\n    39\u2192\n    40\u2192    /// A collection of `<iframe>` sizes to send back to script.\n    41\u2192    pub iframe_sizes: Mutex<IFrameSizes>,\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192    /// An [`ImageResolver`] used for resolving images during box and fragment\n    44\u2192    /// tree construction. Later passed to display list construction.\n    45\u2192    pub image_resolver: Arc<ImageResolver>,\n    46\u2192\n    47\u2192    /// The [`PainterId`] that identifies which `RenderingContext` that this layout targets.\n    48\u2192    pub painter_id: PainterId,\n    49\u2192}\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192pub enum ResolvedImage<'a> {\n    52\u2192    Gradient(&'a Gradient),\n    53\u2192    // The size is tracked explicitly as image-set images can specify their\n    54\u2192    // natural resolution which affects the final size for raster images.\n    55\u2192    Image {\n    56\u2192        image: CachedImage,\n    57\u2192        size: DeviceSize,\n    58\u2192    },\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]\n    62\u2192pub enum ResolveImageError {\n    63\u2192    LoadError,\n    64\u2192    ImagePending,\n    65\u2192    OnlyMetadata,\n    66\u2192    InvalidUrl,\n    67\u2192    MissingNode,\n    68\u2192    ImageMissingFromImageSet,\n    69\u2192    NotImplementedYet,\n    70\u2192    None,\n    71\u2192}\n    72\u2192\n    73\u2192pub(crate) enum LayoutImageCacheResult {\n    74\u2192    Pending,\n    75\u2192    DataAvailable(ImageOrMetadataAvailable),\n    76\u2192    LoadError,\n    77\u2192}\n    78\u2192\n    79\u2192pub(crate) struct ImageResolver {\n    80\u2192    /// The origin of the `Document` that this [`ImageResolver`] resolves images for.\n    81\u2192    pub origin: ImmutableOrigin,\n    82\u2192\n    83\u2192    /// Reference to the script thread image cache.\n    84\u2192    pub image_cache: Arc<dyn ImageCache>,\n    85\u2192\n    86\u2192    /// A list of in-progress image loads to be shared with the script thread.\n    87\u2192    pub pending_images: Mutex<Vec<PendingImage>>,\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192    /// A list of fully loaded vector images that need to be rasterized to a specific\n    90\u2192    /// size determined by layout. This will be shared with the script thread.\n    91\u2192    pub pending_rasterization_images: Mutex<Vec<PendingRasterizationImage>>,\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192    /// A list of `SVGSVGElement`s encountered during layout that are not\n    94\u2192    /// serialized yet. This is needed to support inline SVGs as they are treated\n    95\u2192    /// as replaced elements and the layout is responsible for triggering the\n    96\u2192    /// network load for the corresponding serialized data: urls (similar to\n    97\u2192    /// background images).\n    98\u2192    pub pending_svg_elements_for_serialization: Mutex<Vec<UntrustedNodeAddress>>,\n    99\u2192\n   100\u2192    /// A shared reference to script's map of DOM nodes with animated images. This is used\n   101\u2192    /// to manage image animations in script and inform the script about newly animating\n   102\u2192    /// nodes.\n   103\u2192    pub animating_images: Arc<RwLock<AnimatingImages>>,\n   104\u2192\n   105\u2192    // A cache that maps image resources used in CSS (e.g as the `url()` value\n   106\u2192    // for `background-image` or `content` property) to the final resolved image data.\n   107\u2192    pub resolved_images_cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<ServoUrl, CachedImageOrError>>>,\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192    /// The current animation timeline value used to properly initialize animating images.\n   110\u2192    pub animation_timeline_value: f64,\n   111\u2192}\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192impl Drop for ImageResolver {\n   114\u2192    fn drop(&mut self) {\n   115\u2192        if !std::thread::panicking() {\n   116\u2192            assert!(self.pending_images.lock().is_empty());\n   117\u2192            assert!(self.pending_rasterization_images.lock().is_empty());\n   118\u2192            assert!(\n   119\u2192                self.pending_svg_elements_for_serialization\n   120\u2192                    .lock()\n   121\u2192                    .is_empty()\n   122\u2192            );\n   123\u2192        }\n   124\u2192    }\n   125\u2192}\n   126\u2192\n   127\u2192impl ImageResolver {\n   128\u2192    pub(crate) fn get_or_request_image_or_meta(\n   129\u2192        &self,\n   130\u2192        node: OpaqueNode,\n   131\u2192        url: ServoUrl,\n   132\u2192        destination: LayoutImageDestination,\n   133\u2192    ) -> LayoutImageCacheResult {\n   134\u2192        // Check for available image or start tracking.\n   135\u2192        let cache_result =\n   136\u2192            self.image_cache\n   137\u2192                .get_cached_image_status(url.clone(), self.origin.clone(), None);\n   138\u2192\n   139\u2192        match cache_result {\n   140\u2192            ImageCacheResult::Available(img_or_meta) => {\n   141\u2192                LayoutImageCacheResult::DataAvailable(img_or_meta)\n   142\u2192            },\n   143\u2192            // Image has been requested, is still pending. Return no image for this paint loop.\n   144\u2192            // When the image loads it will trigger a reflow and/or repaint.\n   145\u2192            ImageCacheResult::Pending(id) => {\n   146\u2192                let image = PendingImage {\n   147\u2192                    state: PendingImageState::PendingResponse,\n   148\u2192                    node: node.into(),\n   149\u2192                    id,\n   150\u2192                    origin: self.origin.clone(),\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

Helpers for CSS value parsing.\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192use style::context::QuirksMode;\n     8\u2192use style::error_reporting::ParseErrorReporter;\n     9\u2192use style::parser::ParserContext;\n    10\u2192use style::stylesheets::{CssRuleType, Origin, UrlExtraData};\n    11\u2192use style_traits::ParsingMode;\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192use crate::dom::document::Document;\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192/// Creates a `ParserContext` from the given document.\n    16\u2192///\n    17\u2192/// Automatically configures quirks mode and error reporter from the document.\n    18\u2192pub(crate) fn parser_context_for_document<'a>(\n    19\u2192    document: &'a Document,\n    20\u2192    rule_type: CssRuleType,\n    21\u2192    parsing_mode: ParsingMode,\n    22\u2192    url_data: &'a UrlExtraData,\n    23\u2192) -> ParserContext<'a> {\n    24\u2192    let quirks_mode = document.quirks_mode();\n    25\u2192    let error_reporter = document.window().css_error_reporter();\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192    ParserContext::new(\n    28\u2192        Origin::Author,\n    29\u2192        url_data,\n    30\u2192        Some(rule_type),\n    31\u2192        parsing_mode,\n    32\u2192        quirks_mode,\n    33\u2192        /* namespaces = */ Default::default(),\n    34\u2192        Some(error_reporter),\n    35\u2192        None,\n    36\u2192    )\n    37\u2192}\n    38\u2192\n    39\u2192/// Like [`parser_context_for_document`], but with a custom error reporter.\n    40\u2192pub(crate) fn parser_context_for_document_with_reporter<'a>(\n    41\u2192    document: &'a Document,\n    42\u2192    rule_type: CssRuleType,\n    43\u2192    parsing_mode: ParsingMode,\n    44\u2192    url_data: &'a UrlExtraData,\n    45\u2192    error_reporter: &'a dyn ParseErrorReporter,\n    46\u2192) -> ParserContext<'a> {\n    47\u2192    let quirks_mode = document.quirks_mode();\n    48\u2192\n    49\u2192    ParserContext::new(\n    50\u2192        Origin::Author,\n    51\u2192        url_data,\n    52\u2192        Some(rule_type),\n    53\u2192        parsing_mode,\n    54\u2192        quirks_mode,\n    55\u2192        /* namespaces = */ Default::default(),\n    56\u2192        Some(error_reporter),\n    57\u2192        None,\n    58\u2192    )\n    59\u2192}\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192/// Creates a `ParserContext` without a document, using no quirks mode\n    62\u2192/// and no error reporter.\n    63\u2192pub(crate) fn parser_context_for_anonymous_content<'a>(\n    64\u2192    rule_type: CssRuleType,\n    65\u2192    parsing_mode: ParsingMode,\n    66\u2192    url_data: &'a UrlExtraData,\n    67\u2192) -> ParserContext<'a> {\n    68\u2192    ParserContext::new(\n    69\u2192        Origin::Author,\n    70\u2192        url_data,\n    71\u2192        Some(rule_type),\n    72\u2192        parsing_mode,\n    73\u2192        QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n    74\u2192        /* namespaces = */ Default::default(),\n    75\u2192        None,\n    76\u2192        None,\n    77\u2192    )\n    78\u2192}\n    79\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

super::cssmediarule::CSSMediaRule;\n    18\u2192use super::cssnamespacerule::CSSNamespaceRule;\n    19\u2192use super::cssnesteddeclarations::CSSNestedDeclarations;\n    20\u2192use super::csspropertyrule::CSSPropertyRule;\n    21\u2192use super::cssstylerule::CSSStyleRule;\n    22\u2192use super::cssstylesheet::CSSStyleSheet;\n    23\u2192use super::csssupportsrule::CSSSupportsRule;\n    24\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::CSSRuleBinding::CSSRuleMethods;\n    25\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::inheritance::Castable;\n crate::dom::window::Window;\n    30\u2192use crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\n    31\u2192\n    32\u2192#[dom_struct]\n    33\u2192pub(crate) struct CSSRule {\n    34\u2192    reflector_: Reflector,\n    35\u2192    parent_stylesheet: Dom<CSSStyleSheet>,\n    36\u2192\n    37\u2192    /// Whether the parentStyleSheet attribute should return null.\n    38\u2192    /// We keep parent_stylesheet in that case because insertRule needs it\n    39\u2192    /// for the stylesheet\u2019s base URL and namespace prefixes.\n    40\u2192    parent_stylesheet_removed: Cell<bool>,\n    41\u2192}\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192impl CSSRule {\n    44\u2192    pub(crate) fn new_inherited(parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet) -> CSSRule {\n    45\u2192        CSSRule {\n    46\u2192            reflector_: Reflector::new(),\n    47\u2192            parent_stylesheet: Dom::from_ref(parent_stylesheet),\n    48\u2192            parent_stylesheet_removed: Cell::new(false),\n    49\u2192        }\n    50\u2192    }\n    51\u2192\n    52\u2192    pub(crate) fn as_specific(&self) -> &dyn SpecificCSSRule {\n    53\u2192        if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\n    54\u2192            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    55\u2192        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSFontFaceRule>() {\n    56\u2192            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    57\u2192        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframesRule>() {\n    58\u2192            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    59\u2192        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\n    60\u2192            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    61\u2192        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNamespaceRule>() {\n    62\u2192            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    63\u2192        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSKeyframeRule>() {\n    64\u2192            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    65\u2192        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSImportRule>() {\n    66\u2192            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    67\u2192        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\n    68\u2192            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    69\u2192        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerBlockRule>() {\n    70\u2192            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    71\u2192        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerStatementRule>() {\n    72\u2192            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    73\u2192        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSNestedDeclarations>() {\n    74\u2192            rule as &dyn SpecificCSSRule\n    75\u2192        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSPropertyRule>() {\n    76\u2192 StyleCssRule, create a new instance of a derived class of\n    83\u2192    // CSSRule based on which rule it is\n    84\u2192    pub(crate) fn new_specific(\n    85\u2192        window: &Window,\n    86\u2192        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n    87\u2192        rule: StyleCssRule,\n    88\u2192        can_gc: CanGc,\n    89\u2192    ) -> DomRoot<CSSRule> {\n    90\u2192        // be sure to update the match in as_specific when this is updated\n    91\u2192        match rule {\n    92\u2192            StyleCssRule::Import(s) => {\n    93\u2192                DomRoot::upcast(CSSImportRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n    94\u2192            },\n    95\u2192            StyleCssRule::Style(s) => {\n    96\u2192                DomRoot::upcast(CSSStyleRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n    97\u2192            },\n    98\u2192            StyleCssRule::FontFace(s) => {\n    99\u2192                DomRoot::upcast(CSSFontFaceRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n   100\u2192            },\n   101\u2192            StyleCssRule::FontFeatureValues(_) => unimplemented!(),\n   102\u2192            StyleCssRule::CounterStyle(_) => unimplemented!(),\n   103\u2192            StyleCssRule::Keyframes(s) => {\n   104\u2192                DomRoot::upcast(CSSKeyframesRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n   105\u2192            },\n   106\u2192            StyleCssRule::Media(s) => {\n   107\u2192                DomRoot::upcast(CSSMediaRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n   108\u2192            },\n   109\u2192            StyleCssRule::Namespace(s) => {\n   110\u2192                DomRoot::upcast(CSSNamespaceRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n   111\u2192            },\n   112\u2192            StyleCssRule::Supports(s) => {\n   113\u2192                DomRoot::upcast(CSSSupportsRule::new(window, parent_stylesheet, s, can_gc))\n   114\u2192            },\n   115\u2192            StyleCssRule::Page(_) => unreachable!(),\n   116\u2192            StyleCssRule::Container(_) => unimplemented!(), // DomRoot::upcast(CSSNestedDeclarations::new(\n   137\u2192                window,\n   138\u2192                parent_stylesheet,\n   139\u2192                s,\n   140\u2192                can_gc,\n   141\u2192            )),\n   142\u2192        }\n   143\u2192    }\n   144\u2192\n   145\u2192    /// Sets owner sheet/rule to null\n   146\u2192    pub(crate) fn detach(&self) {\n   147\u2192        self.deparent();\n   148\u2192        // should set parent rule to None when we add parent rule support\n   149\u2192    }\n   150\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
108. Read @ -
Input
{
  "file_path": "/workspace/tests/unit/style/parsing/mod.rs"
}
Output
"     1\u2192/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n     2\u2192 * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3\u2192 * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192//! Tests for parsing and serialization of values/properties\n     6\u2192\n     7\u2192use cssparser::{Parser, ParserInput};\n     8\u2192use style::context::QuirksMode;\n     9\u2192use style::parser::ParserContext;\n    10\u2192use style::stylesheets::{CssRuleType, Origin};\n    11\u2192use style_traits::{ParseError, ParsingMode};\n    12\u2192use url::Url;\n    13\u2192\n    14\u2192fn parse<T, F>(f: F, s: &'static str) -> Result<T, ParseError<'static>>\n    15\u2192where\n    16\u2192    F: for<'t> Fn(&ParserContext, &mut Parser<'static, 't>) -> Result<T, ParseError<'static>>,\n    17\u2192{\n    18\u2192    let mut input = ParserInput::new(s);\n    19\u2192    parse_input(f, &mut input)\n    20\u2192}\n    21\u2192\n    22\u2192fn parse_input<'i: 't, 't, T, F>(f: F, input: &'t mut ParserInput<'i>) -> Result<T, ParseError<'i>>\n    23\u2192where\n    24\u2192    F: Fn(&ParserContext, &mut Parser<'i, 't>) -> Result<T, ParseError<'i>>,\n    25\u2192{\n    26\u2192    let url_data = Url::parse(\"http://localhost\").unwrap().into();\n    27\u2192    let context = ParserContext::new(\n    28\u2192        Origin::Author,\n    29\u2192        &url_data,\n    30\u2192        Some(CssRuleType::Style),\n    31\u2192        ParsingMode::DEFAULT,\n    32\u2192        QuirksMode::NoQuirks,\n    33\u2192        /* namespaces = */ Default::default(),\n    34\u2192        None,\n    35\u2192        None,\n    36\u2192    );\n    37\u2192    let mut parser = Parser::new(input);\n    38\u2192    f(&context, &mut parser)\n    39\u2192}\n    40\u2192\n    41\u2192// This is a macro so that the file/line information\n    42\u2192// is preserved in the panic\n    43\u2192macro_rules! assert_roundtrip_with_context {\n    44\u2192    ($fun:expr, $string:expr) => {\n    45\u2192        assert_roundtrip_with_context!($fun, $string, $string);\n    46\u2192    };\n    47\u2192    ($fun:expr, $input:expr, $output:expr) => {{\n    48\u2192        let mut input = ::cssparser::ParserInput::new($input);\n    49\u2192        let serialized = super::parse_input(\n    50\u2192            |context, i| {\n    51\u2192                let parsed = $fun(context, i).expect(&format!(\"Failed to parse {}\", $input));\n    52\u2192                let serialized = ToCss::to_css_string(&parsed);\n    53\u2192                assert_eq!(serialized, $output);\n    54\u2192                Ok(serialized)\n    55\u2192            },\n    56\u2192            &mut input,\n    57\u2192        )\n    58\u2192        .unwrap();\n    59\u2192\n    60\u2192        let mut input = ::cssparser::ParserInput::new(&serialized);\n    61\u2192        let unwrapped = super::parse_input(\n    62\u2192            |context, i| {\n    63\u2192                let re_parsed =\n    64\u2192                    $fun(context, i).expect(&format!(\"Failed to parse serialization {}\", $input));\n    65\u2192                let re_serialized = ToCss::to_css_string(&re_parsed);\n    66\u2192                assert_eq!(serialized, re_serialized);\n    67\u2192                Ok(())\n    68\u2192            },\n    69\u2192            &mut input,\n    70\u2192        )\n    71\u2192        .unwrap();\n    72\u2192        unwrapped\n    73\u2192    }};\n    74\u2192}\n    75\u2192\n    76\u2192macro_rules! assert_roundtrip {\n    77\u2192    ($fun:expr, $string:expr) => {\n    78\u2192        assert_roundtrip!($fun, $string, $string);\n    79\u2192    };\n    80\u2192    ($fun:expr, $input:expr, $output:expr) => {\n    81\u2192        let mut input = ParserInput::new($input);\n    82\u2192        let mut parser = Parser::new(&mut input);\n    83\u2192        let parsed = $fun(&mut parser).expect(&format!(\"Failed to parse {}\", $input));\n    84\u2192        let serialized = ToCss::to_css_string(&parsed);\n    85\u2192        assert_eq!(serialized, $output);\n    86\u2192\n    87\u2192        let mut input = ParserInput::new(&serialized);\n    88\u2192        let mut parser = Parser::new(&mut input);\n    89\u2192        let re_parsed =\n    90\u2192            $fun(&mut parser).expect(&format!(\"Failed to parse serialization {}\", $input));\n    91\u2192        let re_serialized = ToCss::to_css_string(&re_parsed);\n    92\u2192        assert_eq!(serialized, re_serialized)\n    93\u2192    };\n    94\u2192}\n    95\u2192\n    96\u2192macro_rules! assert_parser_exhausted {\n    97\u2192    ($fun:expr, $string:expr, $should_exhausted:expr) => {{\n    98\u2192        parse(\n    99\u2192            |context, input| {\n   100\u2192                let parsed = $fun(context, input);\n   101\u2192                assert_eq!(parsed.is_ok(), true);\n   102\u2192                assert_eq!(input.is_exhausted(), $should_exhausted);\n   103\u2192                Ok(())\n   104\u2192            },\n   105\u2192            $string,\n   106\u2192        )\n   107\u2192        .unwrap()\n   108\u2192    }};\n   109\u2192}\n   110\u2192\n   111\u2192macro_rules! parse_longhand {\n   112\u2192    ($name:ident, $s:expr) => {\n   113\u2192        parse($name::parse, $s).unwrap()\n   114\u2192    };\n   115\u2192}\n   116\u2192\n   117\u2192mod background;\n   118\u2192mod border;\n   119\u2192mod box_;\n   120\u2192mod column;\n   121\u2192mod effects;\n   122\u2192mod image;\n   123\u2192mod inherited_text;\n   124\u2192mod outline;\n   125\u2192mod selectors;\n   126\u2192mod supports;\n   127\u2192mod text_overflow;\n   128\u2192mod transition_duration;\n   129\u2192mod transition_timing_function;\n   130\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

compute_damage_and_repair_style};\n    99\u2192use crate::{BoxTree, FragmentTree};\n   100\u2192\n   101\u2192// This mutex is necessary due to syncronisation issues between two different types of thread-local storage\n   102\u2192// which manifest themselves when the layout thread tries to layout iframes in parallel with the main page\n   103\u2192//\n   104\u2192// See: https://github.com/servo/servo/pull/29792\n   105\u2192// And: https://gist.github.com/mukilan/ed57eb61b83237a05fbf6360ec5e33b0\n   106\u2192static STYLE_THREAD_POOL: Mutex<&LazyLock<style::global_style_data::StyleThreadPool>> =\n   107\u2192    Mutex::new(&style::global_style_data::STYLE_THREAD_POOL);\n   108\u2192\n   109\u2192/// A CSS file to style the user agent stylesheet.\n   110\u2192static USER_AGENT_CSS: &[u8] = include_bytes!(\"./stylesheets/user-agent.css\");\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192/// A CSS file to style the Servo browser.\n   113\u2192static SERVO_CSS: &[u8] = include_bytes!(\"./stylesheets/servo.css\");\n   114\u2192\n   115\u2192/// A CSS file to style the presentational hints.\n   116\u2192static PRESENTATIONAL_HINTS_CSS: &[u8] = include_bytes!(\"./stylesheets/presentational-hints.css\");\n   117\u2192\n   118\u2192/// A CSS file to style the quirks mode.\n   119\u2192static QUIRKS_MODE_CSS: &[u8] = include_bytes!(\"./stylesheets/quirks-mode.css\");\n   120\u2192\n   121\u2192/// Information needed by layout.\n   122\u2192pub struct LayoutThread {\n   123\u2192    /// The ID of the pipeline that we belong to.\n   124\u2192    id: PipelineId,\n   125\u2192\n   126\u2192    /// The webview that contains the pipeline we belong to.\n   127\u2192    webview_id: WebViewId,\n   128\u2192\n   129\u2192    /// The URL of the pipeline that we belong to.\n   130\u2192    url: ServoUrl,\n   131\u2192\n   132\u2192    /// Performs CSS selector matching and style resolution.\n   133\u2192    stylist: Stylist,\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192    /// Is the current reflow of an iframe, as opposed to a root window?\n   136\u2192    is_iframe: bool,\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192    /// The channel on which messages can be sent to the script thread.\n   139\u2192    script_chan: GenericSender<ScriptThreadMessage>,\n   140\u2192\n   141\u2192    /// The channel on which messages can be sent to the time profiler.\n   142\u2192    time_profiler_chan: profile_time::ProfilerChan,\n   143\u2192\n   144\u2192    /// Reference to the script thread image cache.\n   145\u2192    image_cache: Arc<dyn ImageCache>,\n   146\u2192\n   147\u2192    /// A FontContext to be used during layout.\n   148\u2192    font_context: Arc<FontContext>,\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192    /// Whether or not user agent stylesheets have been added to the Stylist or not.\n   151\u2192    have_added_user_agent_stylesheets: bool,\n   152\u2192\n   153\u2192    // A vector of parsed `DocumentStyleSheet`s representing the corresponding `UserStyleSheet`s\n   154\u2192    // associated with the `WebView` to which this `Layout` belongs. The `DocumentStylesheet`s might\n   155\u2192    // be shared with `Layout`s in the same `ScriptThread`.\n   156\u2192    user_stylesheets: Rc<Vec<DocumentStyleSheet>>,\n   157\u2192\n   158\u2192    /// Whether or not this [`LayoutImpl`]'s [`Device`] has changed since the last restyle.\n   159\u2192    /// If it has, a restyle is pending.\n   160\u2192    device_has_changed: bool,\n   161\u2192\n   162\u2192    /// Is this the first reflow in this LayoutThread?\n   163\u2192    have_ever_generated_display_list: Cell<bool>,\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192    /// Whether a new overflow calculation needs to happen due to changes to the fragment\n   166\u2192    /// tree. This is set to true every time a restyle requests overflow calculation.\n   167\u2192    need_overflow_calculation: Cell<bool>,\n   168\u2192\n   169\u2192    /// Whether a new display list is necessary due to changes to layout or stacking\n   170\u2192    /// contexts. This is set to true every time layout changes, even when a display list\n   171\u2192    /// isn't requested for this layout, such as for layout queries. The next time a\n   172\u2192    /// layout requests a display list, it is produced unconditionally, even when the\n   173\u2192    /// layout trees remain the same.\n   174\u2192    need_new_display_list: Cell<bool>,\n   175\u2192\n   176\u2192    /// Whether or not the existing stacking context tree is dirty and needs to be\n   177\u2192    /// rebuilt. This happens after a relayout or overflow update. The reason that we\n   178\u2192    /// don't simply clear the stacking context tree when it becomes dirty is that we need\n   179\u2192    /// to preserve scroll offsets from the old tree to the new one.\n   180\u2192    need_new_stacking_context_tree: Cell<bool>,\n   181\u2192\n   182\u2192    /// The box tree.\n   183\u2192    box_tree: RefCell<Option<Arc<BoxTree>>>,\n   184\u2192\n   185\u2192    /// The fragment tree.\n   186\u2192    fragment_tree: RefCell<Option<Rc<FragmentTree>>>,\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192    /// The [`StackingContextTree`] cached from previous layouts.\n   189\u2192    stacking_context_tree: RefCell<Option<StackingContextTree>>,\n   190\u2192\n   191\u2192    // A cache that maps image resources specified in CSS (e.g as the `url()` value\n   192\u2192    // for `background-image` or `content` properties) to either the final resolved\n   193\u2192    // image data, or an error if the image cache failed to load/decode the image.\n   194\u2192    resolved_images_cache: Arc<RwLock<HashMap<ServoUrl, CachedImageOrError>>>,\n   195\u2192\n   196\u2192    /// The executors for paint worklets.\n   197\u2192    registered_painters: RegisteredPaintersImpl,\n   198\u2192\n   199\u2192    /// Cross-process access to the `Paint` API.\n   200\u2192    paint_api: CrossProcessPaintApi,\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

crate::flow::inline::construct::{TextTransformation, WhitespaceCollapse, capitalize_string};\n    50\u2192use crate::fragment_tree::{\n    51\u2192    BoxFragment, Fragment, FragmentFlags, 58\u2192fn root_transform_for_layout_node(\n    59\u2192    scroll_tree: &ScrollTree,\n    60\u2192    node: ServoThreadSafeLayoutNode<'_>,\n    61\u2192) -> Option<FastLayoutTransform> {\n 123. Agent @ -
Input
{
  "description": "Search for container query support in Servo",
  "prompt": "Search for any existing container query implementation in Servo:\n1. Look for \"container\" in CSS-related files (not visual containers)\n2. Search for container rules in the DOM CSS files\n3. Check if CSSContainerRule exists\n4. Look for container type or container name properties\n5. Check layout thread for container query evaluation\n\nSearch broadly in:\n- components/script/dom/css/\n- components/layout/\n- Any references to container queries, @container, or CSSContainerRule\n\nBe thorough as this will tell us what's already implemented.",
  "subagent_type": "Explore"
}
Output
{
  "agentId": "af6734cca346def2e",
  "content": [
    {
      "text": "Perfect! Now let me compile a comprehensive report of my findings:\n\n## Container Query Implementation Status in Servo\n\nBased on my thorough search of the Servo codebase, here's what I found regarding container query implementation:\n\n### **Current State: PARTIALLY STARTED BUT MOSTLY UNIMPLEMENTED**\n\n---\n\n## **Key Findings:**\n\n### 1. **Container Rule Support in CSS Parser (Exists but Unimplemented)**\n- **File:** `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssrule.rs` (lines 116, 208)\n- **Status:** `StyleCssRule::Container(_) => unimplemented!()` - marked with TODO\n- The style crate (Stylo) already has `StyleCssRule::Container` variant\n- No corresponding `CSSContainerRule` DOM wrapper exists in Servo's script layer\n- Missing WebIDL bindings for `CSSContainerRule`\n\n### 2. **Configuration/Preference Flag**\n- **File:** `/workspace/components/config/prefs.rs` (lines 49-50, 265, 470)\n- **Field:** `layout_container_queries_enabled: bool`\n- **Default:** `false` (disabled by default)\n- **Integration:** Passed to Stylo via `stylo_config::set_bool(\"layout.container-queries.enabled\", ...)`\n- This means the infrastructure is ready for the feature to be toggled on once implemented\n\n### 3. **Devtools Support**\n- **File:** `/workspace/components/devtools/actors/inspector/node.rs` (line 58)\n- **Field:** `container_type: Option<()>`\n- Currently a stub field set to `None`\n- Prepared for future container type information display\n\n### 4. **Layout Query Infrastructure**\n- **File:** `/workspace/components/layout/query.rs`\n- Contains `process_scroll_container_query()` function\n- Demonstrates a pattern for how container queries would be evaluated\n- Shows the layout thread has mechanisms for processing container-related queries\n\n### 5. **Container Size Query API (From Style Crate)**\n- **File:** `/workspace/components/layout/replaced.rs` (lines 25, 237)\n- Uses `style::stylesheets::container_rule::ContainerSizeQuery`\n- Called with `ContainerSizeQuery::none()` - meaning it's not actually evaluating container queries yet\n- Stylo/rustyle has the data structures ready but they're not being used\n\n### 6. **Missing Implementation: query_container_size Trait Method**\n- **File:** `/workspace/components/script/layout_dom/element.rs` (lines 548-552)\n- **Trait:** `style::dom::TElement`\n- **Status:** Marked with `todo!()`\n- This is where elements should return their container size information for query evaluation\n- Currently unimplemented in ServoLayoutElement\n\n---\n\n## **What's Missing:**\n\n1. **CSSContainerRule DOM Implementation** - No struct like CSSMediaRule or CSSSupportsRule exists\n2. **WebIDL Bindings** - No CSSContainerRule.webidl file\n3. **Container Dimension Tracking** - Layout doesn't track which elements are containers and their sizes\n4. **Container Query Evaluation** - No logic to evaluate `@container` conditions during style matching\n5. **query_container_size Implementation** - The bridge between layout and style querying\n6. **Container Type Properties** - No handling of `container-type` and `container-name` CSS properties\n7. **Selector Matching** - No support for container query conditions in selector matching\n\n---\n\n## **Architecture Ready For Implementation:**\n\n- Style crate (Stylo) has `StyleCssRule::Container` variant defined\n- Layout query infrastructure exists (pattern from scroll container queries)\n- Preference system is in place to enable/disable the feature\n- DOM structure matches pattern of similar grouping rules (MediaRule, SupportsRule)\n- ContainerSizeQuery type is available from Stylo but unused\n\n---\n\n## **File Summary:**\n\n**Files with existing container references:**\n- `/workspace/components/config/prefs.rs` - Feature flag\n- `/workspace/components/script/dom/css/cssrule.rs` - Unimplemented rule handling\n- `/workspace/components/script/layout_dom/element.rs` - Missing query_container_size method\n- `/workspace/components/layout/replaced.rs` - Unused ContainerSizeQuery import\n- `/workspace/components/devtools/actors/inspector/node.rs` - Stubbed container_type field\n- `/workspace/components/layout/query.rs` - Query infrastructure pattern\n- `/workspace/components/layout/style_ext.rs` - CSSRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSRule {\n   241\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-type>\n   242\u2192    fn Type(&self) -> u16 {\n   243\u2192        let rule_type = self.as_specific().ty() as u16;\n   244\u2192        // Per https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-type for constants > 15\n   245\u2192        // we return 0.\n   246\u2192        if rule_type > 15 { 0 } else { rule_type }\n   247\u2192    }\n   248\u2192\n   249\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssrule-parentstylesheet>\n   250\u2192    fn GetParentStyleSheet(&self) -> 264\u2192    fn SetCssText(&self, _: DOMString) {\n   265\u2192        // do nothing\n   266\u2192    }\n   267\u2192}\n   268\u2192\n   269\u2192pub(crate) trait SpecificCSSRule {\n   270\u2192    fn ty(&self) -> CssRuleType;\n   271\u2192    fn get_css(&self) -> DOMString;\n   272\u2192    /// Remove parentStylesheet from all transitive children\n   273\u2192    fn deparent_children(&self) {\n "     1\u2192/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n     2\u2192 * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3\u2192 * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192use dom_struct::dom_struct;\n     6\u2192use servo_arc::Arc;\n     7\u2192use style::shared_lock::{Locked, SharedRwLock, SharedRwLockReadGuard};\n     8\u2192use style::stylesheets::{CssRuleType, CssRuleTypes, CssRules};\n     9\u2192\n    10\u2192use super::cssconditionrule::CSSConditionRule;\n    11\u2192use super::csslayerblockrule::CSSLayerBlockRule;\n    12\u2192use super::cssrule::CSSRule;\n    13\u2192use super::cssrulelist::{CSSRuleList, RulesSource};\n    14\u2192use super::cssstylerule::CSSStyleRule;\n    15\u2192use super::cssstylesheet::CSSStyleSheet;\n    16\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::CSSGroupingRuleBinding::CSSGroupingRuleMethods;\n    17\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::error::{ErrorResult, Fallible};\n    18\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::inheritance::Castable;\n    19\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::reflector::DomGlobal;\n    20\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::root::{DomRoot, MutNullableDom};\n    21\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\n    22\u2192use crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192#[dom_struct]\n    25\u2192pub(crate) struct CSSGroupingRule {\n    26\u2192    cssrule: CSSRule,\n    27\u2192    rulelist: MutNullableDom<CSSRuleList>,\n    28\u2192}\n    29\u2192\n    30\u2192impl CSSGroupingRule {\n    31\u2192    pub(crate) fn new_inherited(parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet) -> CSSGroupingRule {\n    32\u2192        CSSGroupingRule {\n    33\u2192            cssrule: CSSRule::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet),\n    34\u2192            rulelist: MutNullableDom::new(None),\n    35\u2192        }\n    36\u2192    }\n    37\u2192\n    38\u2192    fn rulelist(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<CSSRuleList> {\n    39\u2192        let parent_stylesheet = self.upcast::<CSSRule>().parent_stylesheet();\n    40\u2192        self.rulelist.or_init(|| {\n    41\u2192            let rules = if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSConditionRule>() {\n    42\u2192                rule.clone_rules()\n    43\u2192            } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSLayerBlockRule>() {\n    44\u2192                rule.clone_rules()\n    45\u2192            } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSStyleRule>() {\n    46\u2192                rule.ensure_rules()\n    47\u2192            } else {\n    48\u2192                unreachable!()\n    49\u2192            };\n    50\u2192            CSSRuleList::new(\n    51\u2192                self.global().as_window(),\n    52\u2192                parent_stylesheet,\n    53\u2192                RulesSource::Rules(rules),\n    54\u2192                can_gc,\n    55\u2192            )\n    56\u2192        })\n    57\u2192    }\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192    pub(crate) fn parent_stylesheet(&self) -> &CSSStyleSheet {\n    60\u2192        self.cssrule.parent_stylesheet()\n    61\u2192    }\n    62\u2192\n    63\u2192    pub(crate) fn shared_lock(&self) -> &SharedRwLock {\n    64\u2192        self.cssrule.shared_lock()\n    65\u2192    }\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192    pub(crate) fn update_rules(\n    68\u2192        &self,\n    69\u2192        rules: &Arc<Locked<CssRules>>,\n    70\u2192        guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard,\n    71\u2192    ) {\n    72\u2192        if let Some(rulelist) = self.rulelist.get() {\n    73\u2192            rulelist.update_rules(RulesSource::Rules(rules.clone()), guard);\n    74\u2192        }\n    75\u2192    }\n    76\u2192}\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192impl CSSGroupingRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSGroupingRule {\n    79\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssgroupingrule-cssrules>\n    80\u2192    fn CssRules(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<CSSRuleList> {\n    81\u2192        // XXXManishearth check origin clean flag\n    82\u2192        self.rulelist(can_gc)\n    83\u2192    }\n    84\u2192\n    85\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssgroupingrule-insertrule>\n    86\u2192    fn InsertRule(&self, rule: DOMString, index: u32, can_gc: CanGc) -> Fallible<u32> {\n    87\u2192        // TODO: this should accumulate the rule types of all ancestors.\n    88\u2192        let rule_type = self.cssrule.as_specific().ty();\n    89\u2192        let containing_rule_types = CssRuleTypes::from(rule_type);\n    90\u2192        let parse_relative_rule_type = match rule_type {\n    91\u2192            CssRuleType::Style | CssRuleType::Scope => Some(rule_type),\n    92\u2192            _ => None,\n    93\u2192        };\n    94\u2192        self.rulelist(can_gc).insert_rule(\n    95\u2192            &rule,\n    96\u2192            index,\n    97\u2192            containing_rule_types,\n    98\u2192            parse_relative_rule_type,\n    99\u2192            can_gc,\n   100\u2192        )\n   101\u2192    }\n   102\u2192\n   103\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssgroupingrule-deleterule>\n   104\u2192    fn DeleteRule(&self, index: u32, can_gc: CanGc) -> ErrorResult {\n   105\u2192        self.rulelist(can_gc).remove_rule(index)\n   106\u2192    }\n   107\u2192}\n   108\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

       self.rules.borrow().clone()\n    49\u2192    }\n    50\u2192\n    51\u2192    pub(crate) fn update_rules(\n    52\u2192        &self,\n    53\u2192        rules: Arc<Locked<StyleCssRules>>,\n self.downcast::<CSSMediaRule>() {\n    65\u2192            rule.get_condition_text()\n    66\u2192        } else if let Some(rule) = self.downcast::<CSSSupportsRule>() {\n    67\u2192 MediaRule};\n    11\u2192use style_traits::ToCss;\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192use super::cssconditionrule::CSSConditionRule;\n    14\u2192use super::cssrule::SpecificCSSRule;\n    15\u2192use super::cssstylesheet::CSSStyleSheet;\n    16\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::codegen::Bindings::CSSMediaRuleBinding::CSSMediaRuleMethods;\n    17\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::reflector::{DomGlobal, reflect_dom_object};\n    18\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::root::{DomRoot, MutNullableDom};\n    19\u2192use crate::dom::bindings::str::DOMString;\n    20\u2192use crate::dom::medialist::MediaList;\n    21\u2192use crate::dom::window::Window;\n    22\u2192use crate::script_runtime::CanGc;\n    23\u2192\n    24\u2192#[dom_struct]\n    25\u2192pub(crate) struct CSSMediaRule {\n    26\u2192    cssconditionrule: CSSConditionRule,\n    27\u2192    #[ignore_malloc_size_of = \"Stylo\"]\n    28\u2192    #[no_trace]\n    29\u2192    mediarule: RefCell<Arc<MediaRule>>,\n    30\u2192    medialist: MutNullableDom<MediaList>,\n    31\u2192}\n    32\u2192\n    33\u2192impl CSSMediaRule {\n    34\u2192    fn new_inherited(parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet, mediarule: Arc<MediaRule>) -> CSSMediaRule {\n    35\u2192        let list = mediarule.rules.clone();\n    36\u2192        CSSMediaRule {\n    37\u2192            cssconditionrule: CSSConditionRule::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet, list),\n    38\u2192            mediarule: RefCell::new(mediarule),\n    39\u2192            medialist: MutNullableDom::new(None),\n    40\u2192        }\n    41\u2192    }\n    42\u2192\n    43\u2192    pub(crate) fn new(\n    44\u2192        window: &Window,\n    45\u2192        parent_stylesheet: &CSSStyleSheet,\n    46\u2192        mediarule: Arc<MediaRule>,\n    47\u2192        can_gc: CanGc,\n    48\u2192    ) -> DomRoot<CSSMediaRule> {\n    49\u2192        reflect_dom_object(\n    50\u2192            Box::new(CSSMediaRule::new_inherited(parent_stylesheet, mediarule)),\n    51\u2192            window,\n    52\u2192            can_gc,\n    53\u2192        )\n    54\u2192    }\n    55\u2192\n    56\u2192    fn medialist(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<MediaList> {\n    57\u2192        self.medialist.or_init(|| {\n    58\u2192            MediaList::new(\n    59\u2192                self.global().as_window(),\n    60\u2192                self.cssconditionrule.parent_stylesheet(),\n    61\u2192                self.mediarule.borrow().media_queries.clone(),\n    62\u2192                can_gc,\n    63\u2192            )\n    64\u2192        })\n    65\u2192    }\n    66\u2192\n    67\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-conditional-3/#the-cssmediarule-interface>\n    68\u2192    pub(crate) fn get_condition_text(&self) -> DOMString {\n    69\u2192        let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\n    70\u2192        self.mediarule\n    71\u2192            .borrow()\n    72\u2192            .media_queries\n    73\u2192            .read_with(&guard)\n    74\u2192            .to_css_string()\n    75\u2192            .into()\n    76\u2192    }\n    77\u2192\n    78\u2192    pub(crate) fn update_rule(&self, mediarule: Arc<MediaRule>, guard: &SharedRwLockReadGuard) {\n    79\u2192        self.cssconditionrule\n    80\u2192            .update_rules(mediarule.rules.clone(), guard);\n    81\u2192        if let Some(medialist) = self.medialist.get() {\n    82\u2192            medialist.update_media_list(mediarule.media_queries.clone());\n    83\u2192        }\n    84\u2192        *self.mediarule.borrow_mut() = mediarule;\n    85\u2192    }\n    86\u2192}\n    87\u2192\n    88\u2192impl SpecificCSSRule for CSSMediaRule {\n    89\u2192    fn ty(&self) -> CssRuleType {\n    90\u2192        CssRuleType::Media\n    91\u2192    }\n    92\u2192\n    93\u2192    fn get_css(&self) -> DOMString {\n    94\u2192        let guard = self.cssconditionrule.shared_lock().read();\n    95\u2192        self.mediarule.borrow().to_css_string(&guard).into()\n    96\u2192    }\n    97\u2192}\n    98\u2192\n    99\u2192impl CSSMediaRuleMethods<crate::DomTypeHolder> for CSSMediaRule {\n   100\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/cssom/#dom-cssgroupingrule-media>\n   101\u2192    fn Media(&self, can_gc: CanGc) -> DomRoot<MediaList> {\n   102\u2192        self.medialist(can_gc)\n   103\u2192    }\n   104\u2192}\n   105\u2192\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. 64\u2192}\n    65\u2192impl DisplayGeneratingBox {\n    66\u2192    pub(crate) fn display_inside(&self) -> DisplayInside {\n    67\u2192        match *self {\n    68\u2192            DisplayGeneratingBox::OutsideInside { inside, .. } => inside,\n    69\u2192            DisplayGeneratingBox::LayoutInternal(layout_internal) => {\n    70\u2192                layout_internal.display_inside()\n    71\u2192            },\n    72\u2192        }\n    73\u2192    }\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192    pub(crate) fn used_value_for_contents(&self, contents: &Contents) -> Self {\n    76\u2192        // From <https://www.w3.org/TR/css-display-3/#layout-specific-display>:\n    77\u2192        // > When the display property of a replaced element computes to one of\n    78\u2192        // > the layout-internal values, it is handled as having a used value of\n    79\u2192        // > inline.\n    80\u2192        if matches!(self, Self::LayoutInternal(_)) && contents.is_replaced() {\n    81\u2192            Self::OutsideInside {\n    82\u2192                outside: DisplayOutside::Inline,\n    83\u2192                inside: DisplayInside::Flow {\n    84\u2192                    is_list_item: false,\n    85\u2192                },\n    86\u2192            }\n    87\u2192        } else if matches!(contents, Contents::Widget(_)) {\n    88\u2192 is_list_item: false,\n    96\u2192                    },\n    97\u2192                }\n    98\u2192            } else {\n    99\u2192                *self\n   100\u2192            }\n   101\u2192        } else {\n   102\u2192            *self\n   103\u2192        }\n   104\u2192    }\n   105\u2192}\n   106\u2192\n   107\u2192#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]\n   108\u2192pub(crate) enum DisplayOutside {\n   109\u2192    Block,\n   110\u2192    Inline,\n   111\u2192}\n   112\u2192\n   113\u2192#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]\n   114\u2192pub(crate) enum DisplayInside {\n   115\u2192    // \u201clist-items are limited to the Flow Layout display types\u201d\n   116\u2192    // <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-display/#list-items>\n   117\u2192    Flow { is_list_item: bool },\n   118\u2192    FlowRoot { is_list_item: bool },\n   119\u2192    Flex,\n   120\u2192    Grid,\n   121\u2192    Table,\n   122\u2192}\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug, Eq, PartialEq)]\n   125\u2192#[expect(clippy::enum_variant_names)]\n   126\u2192/// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-display-3/#layout-specific-display>\n   127\u2192pub(crate) enum DisplayLayoutInternal {\n   128\u2192    TableCaption,\n   129\u2192    TableCell,\n   130\u2192    TableColumn,\n   131\u2192    TableColumnGroup,\n   132\u2192    TableFooterGroup,\n   133\u2192    TableHeaderGroup,\n   134\u2192    TableRow,\n   135\u2192    TableRowGroup,\n   136\u2192}\n   137\u2192\n   138\u2192impl DisplayLayoutInternal {\n   139\u2192    /// <https://drafts.csswg.org/css-display-3/#layout-specific-displa>\n   140\u2192    pub(crate) fn display_inside(&self) -> DisplayInside {\n   141\u2192        // When we add ruby, the display_inside of ruby must be Flow.\n   142\u2192        // TODO: this should be unreachable for everything but\n   143\u2192        // table cell and caption, once we have box tree fixups.\n   144\u2192        DisplayInside::FlowRoot {\n   145\u2192            is_list_item: false,\n   146\u2192        }\n   147\u2192    }\n   148\u2192}\n   149\u2192\n   150\u2192/// Percentages resolved but not `auto` margins\n   151\u2192#[derive(Clone, Debug)]\n   152\u2192pub(crate) struct PaddingBorderMargin {\n   153\u2192    pub padding: LogicalSides<Au>,\n   154\u2192    pub border: LogicalSides<Au>,\n   155\u2192    pub margin: LogicalSides<AuOrAuto>,\n   156\u2192\n   157\u2192    /// Pre-computed sums in each axis\n   158\u2192    pub padding_border_sums: LogicalVec2<Au>,\n   159\u2192}\n   160\u2192\n   161\u2192impl PaddingBorderMargin {\n   162\u2192    pub(crate) fn zero() -> Self {\n   163\u2192        Self {\n   164\u2192            padding: LogicalSides::zero(),\n   165\u2192            border: LogicalSides::zero(),\n   166\u2192            margin: LogicalSides::zero(),\n   167\u2192            padding_border_sums: LogicalVec2::zero(),\n   168\u2192        }\n   169\u2192    }\n   170\u2192\n   171\u2192    pub(crate) fn sums_auto_is_zero(\n   172\u2192        &self,\n   173\u2192        ignore_block_margins: LogicalSides1D<bool>,\n   174\u2192    ) -> LogicalVec2<Au> {\n   175\u2192        let margin = self.margin.auto_is(Au::zero);\n   176\u2192        let mut sums = self.padding_border_sums;\n   177\u2192        sums.inline += margin.inline_sum();\n   178\u2192        if !ignore_block_margins.start {\n   179\u2192            sums.block += margin.block_start;\n   180\u2192        }\n   181\u2192        if !ignore_block_margins.end {\n   182\u2192            sums.block += margin.block_end;\n   183\u2192        }\n   184\u2192        sums\n   185\u2192    }\n   186\u2192}\n   187\u2192\n   188\u2192/// Resolved `aspect-ratio` property with respect to a specific element. Depends\n   189\u2192/// on that element's `box-sizing` (and padding and border, if that `box-sizing`\n   190\u2192/// is `border-box`).\n   191\u2192#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]\n   192\u2192pub(crate) struct AspectRatio {\n   193\u2192    /// If the element that this aspect ratio belongs to uses box-sizing:\n   194\u2192    /// border-box, and the aspect-ratio property does not contain \"auto\", then\n   195\u2192    /// the aspect ratio is in respect to the border box. This will then contain\n   196\u2192    /// the summed sizes of the padding and border. Otherwise, it's 0.\n   197\u2192    box_sizing_adjustment: LogicalVec2<Au>,\n   198\u2192    /// The ratio itself (inline over block).\n   199\u2192    i_over_b: CSSFloat,\n   200\u2192}\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

"     1\u2192/* This Source Code Form is subject to the terms of the Mozilla Public\n     2\u2192 * License, v. 2.0. If a copy of the MPL was not distributed with this\n     3\u2192 * file, You can obtain one at https://mozilla.org/MPL/2.0/. */\n     4\u2192\n     5\u2192use std::env::consts::ARCH;\n     6\u2192use std::sync::{RwLock, RwLockReadGuard};\n     7\u2192\n     8\u2192use serde::{Deserialize, Serialize};\n     9\u2192use servo_config_macro::ServoPreferences;\n    10\u2192\n    11\u2192pub use crate::pref_util::PrefValue;\n    12\u2192\n    13\u2192static PREFERENCES: RwLock<Preferences> = RwLock::new(Preferences::const_default());\n    14\u2192\n    15\u2192pub trait PreferencesObserver: Send + Sync {\n    16\u2192    fn prefs_changed(&self, _changes: &[(&'static str, PrefValue)]) {}\n    17\u2192}\n    18\u2192\n    19\u2192static OBSERVERS: RwLock<Vec<Box<dyn PreferencesObserver>>> = RwLock::new(Vec::new());\n    20\u2192\n    21\u2192#[inline]\n    22\u2192/// Get the current set of global preferences for Servo.\n    23\u2192pub fn get() -> RwLockReadGuard<'static, Preferences> {\n    24\u2192    PREFERENCES.read().unwrap()\n    25\u2192}\n    26\u2192\n    27\u2192pub fn add_observer(observer: Box<dyn PreferencesObserver>) {\n    28\u2192    OBSERVERS.write().unwrap().push(observer);\n    29\u2192}\n    30\u2192\n    31\u2192pub fn set(preferences: Preferences) {\n    32\u2192    // Map between Stylo preference names and Servo preference names as the This should be\n    33\u2192    // kept in sync with components/script/dom/bindings/codegen/run.py which generates the\n    34\u2192    // DOM CSS style accessors.\n    35\u2192    stylo_config::set_bool(\"layout.unimplemented\", preferences.layout_unimplemented);\n    36\u2192    stylo_config::set_i32(\"layout.threads\", preferences.layout_threads as i32);\n    37\u2192    stylo_config::set_bool(\"layout.flexbox.enabled\", preferences.layout_flexbox_enabled);\n    38\u2192    stylo_config::set_bool(\"layout.columns.enabled\", preferences.layout_columns_enabled);\n    39\u2192    stylo_config::set_bool(\"layout.grid.enabled\", preferences.layout_grid_enabled);\n    40\u2192    stylo_config::set_bool(\n    41\u2192        \"layout.css.transition-behavior.enabled\",\n    42\u2192        preferences.layout_css_transition_behavior_enabled,\n    43\u2192    );\n    44\u2192    stylo_config::set_bool(\n    45\u2192        \"layout.writing-mode.enabled\",\n    46\u2192        preferences.layout_writing_mode_enabled,\n    47\u2192    );\n    48\u2192    stylo_config::set_bool(\n    49\u2192        \"layout.container-queries.enabled\",\n    50\u2192        preferences.layout_container_queries_enabled,\n    51\u2192    );\n    52\u2192    stylo_config::set_bool(\n    53\u2192        \"layout.variable_fonts.enabled\",\n    54\u2192        preferences.layout_variable_fonts_enabled,\n    55\u2192    );\n    56\u2192\n    57\u2192    let changed = preferences.diff(&PREFERENCES.read().unwrap());\n    58\u2192\n    59\u2192    *PREFERENCES.write().unwrap() = preferences;\n    60\u2192\n    61\u2192    for observer in &*OBSERVERS.read().unwrap() {\n    62\u2192        observer.prefs_changed(&changed);\n    63\u2192    }\n    64\u2192}\n    65\u2192\n    66\u2192/// A convenience macro for accessing a preference value using its static path.\n    67\u2192/// Passing an invalid path is a compile-time error.\n    68\u2192#[macro_export]\n    69\u2192macro_rules! pref {\n    70\u2192    ($name: ident) => {\n    71\u2192        $crate::prefs::get().$name.clone()\n    72\u2192    };\n    73\u2192}\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192#[derive(Clone, Deserialize, Serialize, ServoPreferences)]\n    76\u2192pub struct Preferences {\n    77\u2192    pub fonts_default: String,\n    78\u2192    pub fonts_serif: String,\n    79\u2192    pub fonts_sans_serif: String,\n    80\u2192    pub fonts_monospace: String,\n    81\u2192    pub fonts_default_size: i64,\n    82\u2192    pub fonts_default_monospace_size: i64,\n    83\u2192    pub css_animations_testing_enabled: bool,\n    84\u2192    /// Start the devtools server at startup\n    85\u2192    pub devtools_server_enabled: bool,\n    86\u2192    /// Port number to start a server to listen to remote Firefox devtools connections.\n    87\u2192    /// 0 for random port.\n    88\u2192    pub devtools_server_port: i64,\n    89\u2192    // feature: WebGPU | #24706 | Web/API/WebGPU_API\n    90\u2192    pub dom_webgpu_enabled: bool,\n    91\u2192    /// List of comma-separated backends to be used by wgpu.\n    92\u2192    pub dom_webgpu_wgpu_backend: String,\n    93\u2192    // feature: AbortController | #34866 | Web/API/AbortController\n    94\u2192    pub dom_abort_controller_enabled: bool,\n    95\u2192    // feature: Adopted Stylesheet | #38132 | Web/API/Document/adoptedStyleSheets\n    96\u2192    pub dom_adoptedstylesheet_enabled: bool,\n    97\u2192    // feature: Clipboard API | #36084 | Web/API/Clipboard_API\n    98\u2192    pub dom_async_clipboard_enabled: bool,\n    99\u2192    pub dom_bluetooth_enabled: bool,\n   100\u2192    pub dom_bluetooth_testing_enabled: bool,\n   101\u2192    pub dom_allow_scripts_to_close_windows: bool,\n   102\u2192    // feature: Media Capture and Streams API | #26861 | Web/API/Media_Capture_and_Streams_API\n   103\u2192    pub dom_canvas_capture_enabled: bool,\n   104\u2192    pub dom_canvas_text_enabled: bool,\n   105\u2192    /// Selects canvas backend\n   106\u2192    ///\n   107\u2192    /// Available values:\n   108\u2192    /// - ` `/`auto`\n   109\u2192    /// - vello\n   110\u2192    /// - vello_cpu\n   111\u2192    pub dom_canvas_backend: String,\n   112\u2192    pub dom_clipboardevent_enabled: bool,\n   113\u2192    pub dom_command_invokers_enabled: bool,\n   114\u2192    pub dom_composition_event_enabled: bool,\n   115\u2192    // #29376 | Web/API/CSS_Font_Loading_API\n   124\u2192    pub dom_fontface_enabled: bool,\n   125\u2192    pub dom_fullscreen_test: bool,\n   126\u2192    // feature: Gamepad API | #10977 | Web/API/Gamepad_API\n   127\u2192    pub dom_gamepad_enabled: bool,\n   128\u2192    // feature: Geolocation API | #38903 | Web/API/Geolocation_API\n   129\u2192    pub dom_geolocation_enabled: bool,\n   130\u2192    // feature: IndexedDB | #6963 | Web/API/IndexedDB_API\n   131\u2192    pub dom_indexeddb_enabled: bool,\n   132\u2192    // feature: IntersectionObserver | #35767 | dom_uievent_which_enabled: bool,\n   136\u2192    // feature: MutationObserver | #6633 | Web/API/MutationObserver\n   137\u2192    pub dom_mutation_observer_enabled: bool,\n   138\u2192    // feature: wrapper around elements that ensures layout can only ever access safe properties.\n    59\u2192#[derive(Clone, Copy, Eq, Hash, PartialEq)]\n    60\u2192pub struct ServoLayoutElement<'dom> {\n    61\u2192    /// The wrapped private DOM Element.\n    62\u2192    element: LayoutDom<'dom, Element>,\n    63\u2192}\n    64\u2192\n    65\u2192impl fmt::Debug for ServoLayoutElement<'_> {\n    66\u2192    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter) -> fmt::Result {\n    67\u2192        write!(f, \"<{}\", self.element.local_name())?;\n    68\u2192        if let Some(id) = self.id() {\n    69\u2192            write!(f, \" id={}\", id)?;\n    70\u2192        }\n    71\u2192        write!(f, \"> ({:#x})\", self.as_node().opaque().0)\n    72\u2192    }\n    73\u2192}\n    74\u2192\n    75\u2192impl<'dom> ServoLayoutElement<'dom> {\n    76\u2192    pub(super) fn from_layout_js(el: LayoutDom<'dom, Element>) -> Self {\n    77\u2192        ServoLayoutElement { element: el }\n    78\u2192    }\n    79\u2192\n    80\u2192    pub(super) fn is_html_element(&self) -> bool {\n    81\u2192        self.element.is_html_element()\n    82\u2192    }\n    83\u2192\n    84\u2192    #[inline]\n    85\u2192    fn get_attr_enum(&self, namespace: &Namespace, name: &LocalName) -> Option<&AttrValue> {\n    86\u2192        self.element.get_attr_for_layout(namespace, name)\n    87\u2192    }\n    88\u2192\n    89\u2192    #[inline]\n    90\u2192    fn get_attr(&self, namespace: &Namespace, name: &LocalName) -> Option<&str> {\n    91\u2192        self.element.get_attr_val_for_layout(namespace, name)\n    92\u2192    }\n    93\u2192\n    94\u2192    fn get_style_data(&self) -> Option<&StyleData> {\n    95\u2192        self.as_node().style_data()\n    96\u2192    }\n    97\u2192\n    98\u2192    /// Unset the snapshot flags on the underlying DOM object for this element.\n    99\u2192    ///\n   100\u2192    /// # Safety\n   101\u2192    ///\n   102\u2192    /// This function accesses and modifies the underlying DOM object and should\n   103\u2192    /// not be used by more than a single thread at once.\n   104\u2192    pub unsafe fn unset_snapshot_flags(&self) {\n   105\u2192        unsafe {\n   106\u2192            self.as_node()\n   107\u2192                .node\n   108\u2192                .set_flag(NodeFlags::HAS_SNAPSHOT | NodeFlags::HANDLED_SNAPSHOT, false);\n   109\u2192        }\n   110\u2192    }\n   111\u2192\n   112\u2192    /// Unset the snapshot flags on the underlying DOM 117\u2192    /// not be used by more than a single thread at once.\n   118\u2192    pub unsafe fn set_has_snapshot(&self) {\n   119\u2192        unsafe {\n   120\u2192            self.as_node().node.set_flag(NodeFlags::HAS_SNAPSHOT, true);\n   121\u2192        }\n   122\u2192    }\n   123\u2192\n   124\u2192    /// Returns true if this element is the body child of an html element root element.\n   125\u2192    fn is_body_element_of_html_element_root(&self) -> bool {\n   126\u2192        if self.element.local_name() != &local_name!(\"body\") {\n   127\u2192            return false;\n   128\u2192        }\n   129\u2192\n   130\u2192        self.parent_element().is_some_and(|element| {\n   131\u2192            element.is_root() && element.element.local_name() == &local_name!(\"html\")\n   132\u2192        })\n   133\u2192    }\n   134\u2192\n   135\u2192    /// Returns the parent element of this element, if it has one.\n   136\u2192    fn parent_element(&self) -> Option<Self> {\n   137\u2192        self.as_node().parent_element()\n   138\u2192    }\n   139\u2192\n   140\u2192    fn is_root(&self) -> bool {\n   141\u2192        match self.as_node().parent_node() {\n   142\u2192            None => false,\n   143\u2192            Some(node) => matches!(node.script_type_id(), NodeTypeId::Document(_)),\n   144\u2192        }\n   145\u2192    }\n   146\u2192}\n   147\u2192\n   148\u2192pub enum DOMDescendantIterator<E>\n   149\u2192where\n   150\u2192    E: TElement,\n   151\u2192{\n   152\u2192    /// Iterating over the children of a node, including children of a potential\n   153\u2192    /// [ShadowRoot](crate::dom::shadow_root::ShadowRoot)\n   154\u2192    Children(DomChildren<E::ConcreteNode>),\n   155\u2192    /// Iterating over the content's of a [`<slot>`](HTMLSlotElement) element.\n   156\u2192    Slottables { slot: E, index: usize },\n   157\u2192}\n   158\u2192\n   159\u2192impl<E> Iterator for DOMDescendantIterator<E>\n   160\u2192where\n   161\u2192    E: TElement,\n   162\u2192{\n   163\u2192    type Item = E::ConcreteNode;\n   164\u2192\n   165\u2192    fn next(&mut self) -> Option<Self::Item> {\n   166\u2192        match self {\n   167\u2192            Self::Children(children) => children.next(),\n   168\u2192            Self::Slottables { slot, index } => {\n   169\u2192                let slottables = slot.slotted_nodes();\n   170\u2192                let slot = slottables.get(*index)?;\n   171\u2192                *index += 1;\n   172\u2192                Some(*slot)\n   173\u2192            },\n   174\u2192        }\n   175\u2192    }\n   176\u2192}\n   177\u2192\n   178\u2192impl<'dom> style::dom::AttributeProvider for ServoLayoutElement<'dom> {\n   179\u2192    fn get_attr(&self, attr: &style::LocalName) -> Option<String> {\n   180\u2192        self.element\n   181\u2192            .get_attr_val_for_layout(&ns!(), attr)\n   182\u2192 ServoLayoutNode<'dom>;\n   188\u2192    type TraversalChildrenIterator = DOMDescendantIterator<Self>;\n   189\u2192\n   190\u2192    fn as_node(&self) -> ServoLayoutNode<'dom> {\n   191\u2192        ServoLayoutNode::from_layout_js(self.element.upcast())\n   192\u2192    }\n   193\u2192\n   194\u2192    fn traversal_children(&self) -> LayoutIterator<Self::TraversalChildrenIterator> {\n   195\u2192        let iterator = if self.slotted_nodes().is_empty() {\n   196\u2192            let children = if let Some(shadow_root) = self.shadow_root() {\n   197\u2192                shadow_root.as_node().dom_children()\n   198\u2192            } else {\n   199\u2192                self.as_node().dom_children()\n   200\u2192            };\n   201\u2192            DOMDescendantIterator::Children(children)\n   202\u2192        } else {\n   203\u2192            DOMDescendantIterator::Slottables {\n   204\u2192                slot: *self,\n   205\u2192                index: 0,\n   206\u2192            }\n   207\u2192        };\n   208\u2192\n   209\u2192        LayoutIterator(iterator)\n   210\u2192    }\n   211\u2192\n   212\u2192    fn traversal_parent(&self) -> Option<Self> {\n   213\u2192        self.as_node().traversal_parent()\n   214\u2192    }\n   215\u2192\n   216\u2192    fn inheritance_parent(&self) -> Option<Self> {\n   217\u2192        if self.is_pseudo_element() {\n   218\u2192            // The inheritance parent of an implemented pseudo-element should be the\n   219\u2192            // originating element, except if `is_element_backed()` is true, then it should\n   220\u2192            // be the flat tree parent. Note `is_element_backed()` differs from the CSS term.\n   221\u2192            // At the current time, `is_element_backed()` is always false in Servo.\n   222\u2192            //\n   223\u2192            // FIXME: handle the cases of element-backed pseudo-elements.\n   224\u2192            return self.pseudo_element_originating_element();\n   225\u2192        }\n   226\u2192\n   227\u2192        self.traversal_parent()\n   228\u2192    }\n   229\u2192\n   230\u2192    fn is_html_element(&self) -> bool {\n   231\u2192        ServoLayoutElement::is_html_element(self)\n   232\u2192    }\n   233\u2192\n   234\u2192    fn is_mathml_element(&self) -> bool {\n   235\u2192        *self.element.namespace() == ns!(mathml)\n   236\u2192    }\n   237\u2192\n   238\u2192    fn is_svg_element(&self) -> bool {\n   239\u2192        *self.element.namespace() == ns!(svg)\n   240\u2192    }\n   241\u2192\n   242\u2192    fn has_part_attr(&self) -> bool {\n   243\u2192 {\n   308\u2192                callback(AtomIdent::cast(class))\n   309\u2192            }\n   310\u2192        }\n   311\u2192    }\n   312\u2192\n   313\u2192    #[inline(always)]\n   314\u2192     self.as_node()\n   368\u2192                .node\n   369\u2192                .set_flag(NodeFlags::HANDLED_SNAPSHOT, true);\n   370\u2192        }\n   371\u2192    }\n   372\u2192\n   373\u2192    unsafe fn set_dirty_descendants(&self) {\n   374\u2192        debug_assert!(self.as_node().is_connected());\n   375\u2192        unsafe {\n   376\u2192            self.as_node()\n   377\u2192                .node\n   378\u2192                .set_flag(NodeFlags::HAS_DIRTY_DESCENDANTS, true)\n   379\u2192        }\n   380\u2192    }\n   381\u2192\n   382\u2192    unsafe fn unset_dirty_descendants(&self) {\n   383\u2192        unsafe {\n   384\u2192            self.as_node()\n   385\u2192                .node\n   386\u2192                .set_flag(NodeFlags::HAS_DIRTY_DESCENDANTS, false)\n   387\u2192        }\n   388\u2192    }\n   389\u2192\n   390\u2192    /// Whether this element should match user and content rules.\n   391\u2192    /// We would like to match rules from the same tree in all cases and optimize computation.\n   392\u2192    /// UA Widget is an exception since we could have a pseudo element selector inside it.\n   393\u2192    #[inline]\n   394\u2192    fn matches_user_and_content_rules(&self) -> bool {\n   395\u2192        !self.as_node().node.is_in_ua_widget()\n   396\u2192    }\n   397\u2192\n   398\u2192    /// Returns the pseudo-element implemented by this element, if any. In other words,\n   399\u2192    /// the element will match the specified pseudo element throughout the style computation.\n   400\u2192    #[inline]\n   401\u2192    fn implemented_pseudo_element(&self) -> Option<PseudoElement> {\n   402\u2192        self.as_node().node.implemented_pseudo_element()\n   403\u2192    }\n   404\u2192\n   405\u2192    fn store_children_to_process(&self, n: isize) {\n   406\u2192        let data = self.get_style_data().unwrap();\n   407\u2192        data.parallel\n   408\u2192            .children_to_process\n   409\u2192            .store(n, Ordering::Relaxed);\n   410\u2192    }\n   411\u2192\n   412\u2192    fn did_process_child(&self) -> isize {\n   413\u2192        let data = self.get_style_data().unwrap();\n   414\u2192        let old_value = data\n   415\u2192            .parallel\n   416\u2192            .children_to_process\n   417\u2192            .fetch_sub(1, Ordering::Relaxed);\n   418\u2192        debug_assert!(old_value >= 1);\n   419\u2192        old_value - 1\n   420\u2192    }\n   421\u2192\n   422\u2192    unsafe fn clear_data(&self) {\n   423\u2192        unsafe { self.as_node().get_jsmanaged().clear_style_and_layout_data() }\n   424\u2192    }\n   425\u2192\n   426\u2192    unsafe fn ensure_data(&self) -> AtomicRefMut<'_, ElementData> {\n   427\u2192        unsafe {\n   428\u2192            self.as_node().get_jsmanaged().initialize_style_data();\n   429\u2192        };\n   430\u2192        self.mutate_data().unwrap()\n   431\u2192    }\n   432\u2192\n   433\u2192    /// Whether there is an ElementData container.\n   434\u2192    fn has_data(&self) -> bool {\n   435\u2192        self.get_style_data().is_some()\n   436\u2192    }\n   437\u2192\n   438\u2192    /// Immutably borrows the ElementData.\n   439\u2192    fn borrow_data(&self) -> Option<AtomicRef<'_, ElementData>> {\n   440\u2192        self.get_style_data().map(|data| data.element_data.borrow())\n   441\u2192    }\n   442\u2192\n   443\u2192    /// Mutably borrows the ElementData.\n   444\u2192    fn mutate_data(&self) -> Option<AtomicRefMut<'_, ElementData>> {\n   445\u2192        self.get_style_data()\n   446\u2192            .map(|data| data.element_data.borrow_mut())\n   447\u2192    }\n   448\u2192\n   449\u2192    fn skip_item_display_fixup(&self) -> bool {\n   450\u2192        false\n   451\u2192    }\n   452\u2192\n   453\u2192    fn has_animations(&self, context: &SharedStyleContext) -> bool {\n   454\u2192        // This is not used for pseudo elements currently so we can pass None.\n   455\u2192        self.has_css_animations(context, /* pseudo_element = */ None) ||\n   456\u2192            self.has_css_transitions(context, /* pseudo_element = */ None)\n   457\u2192    }\n   458\u2192\n   459\u2192    fn has_css_animations(\n   460\u2192        &self,\n   461\u2192        context: &SharedStyleContext,\n   462\u2192        pseudo_element: Option<PseudoElement>,\n   463\u2192    ) -> bool {\n   464\u2192        let key = AnimationSetKey::new(self.as_node().opaque(), pseudo_element);\n   465\u2192        context.animations.has_active_animations(&key)\n   466\u2192    }\n   467\u2192\n   468\u2192    fn has_css_transitions(\n   469\u2192        &self,\n   470\u2192        context: &SharedStyleContext,\n   471\u2192        pseudo_element: Option<PseudoElement>,\n   472\u2192    ) -> bool {\n   473\u2192        let key = AnimationSetKey::new(self.as_node().opaque(), pseudo_element);\n   474\u2192        context.animations.has_active_transitions(&key)\n   475\u2192    }\n   476\u2192\n   477\u2192    #[inline]\n   478\u2192    fn lang_attr(&self) -> Option<SelectorAttrValue> {\n   479\u2192        self.get_attr(&ns!(xml), &local_name!(\"lang\"))\n   480\u2192            .or_else(|| self.get_attr(&ns!(), &local_name!(\"lang\")))\n   481\u2192            .map(|v| SelectorAttrValue::from(v as &str))\n   482\u2192    }\n   483\u2192\n   484\u2192    fn match_element_lang(\n   485\u2192        &self,\n   486\u2192        override_lang: Option<Option<SelectorAttrValue>>,\n   487\u2192        value: &Lang,\n   488\u2192    ) -> bool {\n   489\u2192        // Servo supports :lang() from CSS Selectors 4, which can take a comma-\n   490\u2192        // separated list of language tags in the pseudo-class, and which\n   491\u2192        // performs RFC 4647 extended filtering matching on them.\n   492\u2192        //\n   493\u2192        // FIXME(heycam): This is wrong, since extended_filtering accepts\n   494\u2192        // a string containing commas (separating each language tag in\n   495\u2192        // a list) but the pseudo-class instead should be parsing and\n   496\u2192        // storing separate <ident> or <string>s for each language tag.\n   497\u2192        //\n   498\u2192        // FIXME(heycam): Look at `element`'s document's Content-Language\n   499\u2192        // HTTP header for language tags to match `value` against.  To\n   500\u2192        // do this, we should make `get_lang_for_layout` return an Option,\n   501\u2192        // so we can decide when to fall back to the Content-Language check.\n   502\u2192        let element_lang = match override_lang {\n   503\u2192            Some(Some(lang)) => lang,\n   504\u2192            Some(None) => AtomString::default(),\n   505\u2192            None => AtomString::from(&*self.element.get_lang_for_layout()),\n   506\u2192        };\n   507\u2192        extended_filtering(&element_lang, value)\n   508\u2192    }\n   509\u2192\n   510\u2192    fn is_html_document_body_element(&self) -> bool {\n   511\u2192        self.is_body_element_of_html_element_root()\n   512\u2192    }\n   513\u2192\n   514\u2192    fn synthesize_presentational_hints_for_legacy_attributes<V>(\n   515\u2192        &self,\n   516\u2192        _visited_handling: VisitedHandlingMode,\n   517\u2192        hints: &mut V,\n   518\u2192    ) where\n   519\u2192        V: Push<ApplicableDeclarationBlock>,\n   520\u2192    {\n   521\u2192        self.element\n   522\u2192            .synthesize_presentational_hints_for_legacy_attributes(hints);\n   523\u2192    }\n   524\u2192\n   525\u2192    /// The shadow root this element is a host of.\n   526\u2192    fn shadow_root(&self) -> Option<ServoShadowRoot<'dom>> {\n   527\u2192        self.element\n   528\u2192            .get_shadow_root_for_layout()\n   529\u2192            .map(ServoShadowRoot::from_layout_js)\n   530\u2192    }\n   531\u2192\n   532\u2192    /// The shadow root which roots the subtree this element is contained in.\n   533\u2192    fn containing_shadow(&self) -> Option<ServoShadowRoot<'dom>> {\n   534\u2192        self.element\n   535\u2192            .upcast()\n   536\u2192            .containing_shadow_root_for_layout()\n   537\u2192            .map(ServoShadowRoot::from_layout_js)\n   538\u2192    }\n   539\u2192\n   540\u2192    fn local_name(&self) -> &LocalName {\n   541\u2192        self.element.local_name()\n   542\u2192    }\n   543\u2192\n   544\u2192    fn namespace(&self) -> &Namespace {\n   545\u2192        self.element.namespace()\n   546\u2192    }\n   547\u2192\n   548\u2192    fn query_container_size(\n   549\u2192        &self,\n   550\u2192        _display: &Display,\n   551\u2192    ) -> euclid::default::Size2D<Option<app_units::Au>> {\n   552\u2192        todo!();\n   553\u2192    }\n   554\u2192\n   555\u2192    fn has_selector_flags(&self, flags: ElementSelectorFlags) -> bool {\n   556\u2192        self.element.get_selector_flags().contains(flags)\n   557\u2192    }\n   558\u2192\n   559\u2192    fn relative_selector_search_direction(&self) -> ElementSelectorFlags {\n   560\u2192        self.element\n   561\u2192            .get_selector_flags()\n   562\u2192            .intersection(ElementSelectorFlags::RELATIVE_SELECTOR_SEARCH_DIRECTION_ANCESTOR_SIBLING)\n   563\u2192    }\n   564\u2192\n   565\u2192    fn each_custom_state<F>(&self, callback: F)\n   566\u2192    where\n   567\u2192        F: FnMut(&AtomIdent),\n   568\u2192    {\n   569\u2192        self.element.each_custom_state_for_layout(callback);\n   570\u2192    }\n   571\u2192\n   572\u2192    /// Returns the implicit scope root for given sheet index and host.\n   573\u2192    fn implicit_scope_for_sheet_in_shadow_root(\n   574\u2192        opaque_host: ::selectors::OpaqueElement,\n   575\u2192        sheet_index: usize,\n   576\u2192    ) -> Option<ImplicitScopeRoot> {\n   577\u2192        // As long as this \"unopaqued\" element does not escape this function, we're not leaking\n   578\u2192        // potentially-mutable elements from opaque elements.\n   579\u2192        let host = unsafe {\n   580\u2192            let ptr = opaque_host.as_const_ptr::<JSObject>();\n   581\u2192            let untrusted_address = UntrustedNodeAddress::from_id(ptr as usize);\n   582\u2192            let node = Node::from_untrusted_node_address(untrusted_address);\n   583\u2192            let trusted_address = node.to_trusted_node_address();\n   584\u2192            let servo_layout_node = ServoLayoutNode::new(&trusted_address);\n   585\u2192            servo_layout_node.as_element().unwrap()\n   586\u2192        };\n   587\u2192        host.shadow_root()?.implicit_scope_for_sheet(sheet_index)\n   588\u2192    }\n   589\u2192\n   590\u2192    fn slotted_nodes(&self) -> &[Self::ConcreteNode] {\n   591\u2192        let Some(slot_element) = self.element.unsafe_get().downcast::<HTMLSlotElement>() else {\n   592\u2192            return &[];\n   593\u2192        };\n   594\u2192        let assigned_nodes = slot_element.assigned_nodes();\n   595\u2192\n   596\u2192        // SAFETY:\n   597\u2192        // Self::ConcreteNode (aka ServoLayoutNode) and Slottable are guaranteed to have the same\n   598\u2192        // layout and alignment as ptr::NonNull<T>. Lifetimes are not an issue because the\n   599\u2192        // slottables are being kept alive by the slot element.\n   600\u2192        unsafe {\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"
